notes: archive includes all decrypted records from the source file. Normalized question records are provided for lookup, and source_payload preserves original answer/audio fields when present.

questions.content: What are some changes in the area recently?
Are the people in your neighborhood nice and friendly？
Do you live in a noisy or a quiet area?
Do you know any of your neighbors?
Do you like the area that you live in?
What changes have been made to the area recently?
Do you know any famous people in your area?
Where do you like to go in that area?

sub_questions.content: What are some changes in the area recently?

answers.content: Recently, the neighborhood has seen significant improvements. New parks and bike lanes have been added, making it more eco-friendly. Additionally, several modern cafes and shops have opened, attracting younger crowds. These changes have transformed the area into a lively and vibrant community hub.

According to my observation, the biggest change that took place here is that there are more and more entertaining venues now. People here have come up with a variety of stuff to relax recently, so accordingly, a lot more related facilities have been constructed. And another difference is that, the public transport system is far more completed, with more metro lines being built.

sub_questions.content: Are the people in your neighborhood nice and friendly？

answers.content: Yes, the people in my neighborhood are incredibly nice and friendly. They always greet each other with a smile and are willing to lend a helping hand whenever needed. One person that stands out is Auntie Liu, who loves to share the delicious snacks she makes at home. She often brings over freshly baked pastries and sweet treats for everyone to enjoy. It really creates a sense of warmth and community in our neighborhood.

Absolutely, my neighbors are very warm and approachable. For example, they often organize community gatherings, which creates a strong sense of belonging. I especially appreciate how everyone looks out for one another, making the neighborhood feel safe and welcoming. This friendly atmosphere truly enhances the quality of life here.

sub_questions.content: Do you live in a noisy or a quiet area?

answers.content: I live in a relatively tranquil neighborhood, which allows me to focus on my studies without distractions from noise. The peaceful environment is perfect for unwinding after a busy day of lectures and assignments. It fosters a sense of calmness which is beneficial for my overall well-being and productivity. I truly appreciate the serenity that my quiet area provides.

I live in a quiet area, which I find quite peaceful and conducive for my daily activities. It allows me to concentrate on my studies and other tasks without any distractions. However, the downside is that there are limited social activities and entertainment options around here. Nonetheless, the tranquility and serenity of my neighborhood make it the perfect place to unwind after a long day.

sub_questions.content: Do you know any of your neighbors?

answers.content: To be honest, since I just moved into my new flat, I wasn't familiar with any of my neighbours yet. Not only that, but I leave early in the morning and return late at night every day, so I haven't had a chance to get to know them either.

Well, since I’ve only just moved into a new apartment, I am not familiar with any of my neighbors yet. Not only that, but I leave the apartment early in the morning and return late at night, so I don’t really have chance to get to know them either.

sub_questions.content: Do you like the area that you live in?

answers.content: Honestly speaking, not very much. Although I can see how much this area has developed, I am still not very satisfied with the current situation. One reason is that, I suppose this place is so overpopulated, so commuting is really a torture for me every day. Meanwhile, the air quality here is also terrible, and I am afraid that I will get some lung diseases one day if I keep living here.

Of course, yes! There are so many aspects in this area that take my fancy. The climate of it is perfect for me, although the winter is a bit freezing. And the modernism there is what attracts me most. Living in this place, I feel that I can always keep up with the times.

sub_questions.content: What changes have been made to the area recently?

answers.content: Well, a lot of changes are taking place nowadays in my city. I can see more and more skyscrapers have been built and the public transportation system is becoming more diversified and completed. But for me, the most fantastic change is that there is greater diversity in restaurants! Now I can taste genuine foreign food in my city. How marvelous it is!

sub_questions.content: Do you know any famous people in your area?

answers.content: I remember that some popular singers in China come from where I live now, who have won several singing competitions, and produced songs which enjoy great popularity. And in ancient times, this land also cultivated a large number of productive poets who made a valuable contribution to the development of Chinese poetry.

Actually yes! I am aware that a lot of movie stars come from where I live. I feel very proud every time I see them in a fantastic film. Also, there are some poets and writers who are living in the area that I come from. So, I suppose my city is an artistic and poetic area.

sub_questions.content: Where do you like to go in that area?

answers.content: The city library is where I frequently visit in the place I live, because I prefer a tranquil atmosphere and people in the library are always silent. So, even if I don't want to read, going to the library to work/study is also what I would do. Besides, there are many activities held by the library regularly, which I may find meaningful to participate in.

The sports stadium next to the building I live in, is where I would like to go frequently. The stadium is not very spacious but well-equipped with plenty of sports facilities. Also, there is an indoor swimming pool with a small number of people so I can swim whenever I want. That's really awesome!

sub_questions.content: What subject(s) are you studying?

answers.content: I am currently studying a range of subjects including biology, chemistry, and mathematics. These subjects offer a diverse perspective on the world, allowing me to explore the intricacies of life, understand the principles behind various chemical reactions, and develop problem-solving skills. Learning about these subjects enables me to gain a better understanding of the world we live in and opens up opportunities for scientific research and discovery.

The main subjects I’m studying at the moment are financial management, micro-economics, statistics and audition. Apart from these major ones, I also have to go to classes like Chinese literature, politics and English. I only study these because they contribute to my GPA.

sub_questions.content: What technology do you use when you study?

answers.content: I mainly utilize AI-powered study tools that assist in language learning and provide instant feedback on my progress. These tools help me practice listening, reading, and speaking skills efficiently. For instance, I use language learning apps that use AI algorithms to personalize study plans based on my specific needs.

Well, I normally use my iPad for online lessons. You see, I’ve signed up for many online lectures because I like to use my spare time to increase my knowledge. I also use the Kindle for reading which helps me to learn more about various topics and broaden my horizons.

sub_questions.content: Who helps you the most？And how?

answers.content: My literature teacher has been the most helpful. She not only explains complex concepts clearly but also provides insightful feedback on my essays, helping me improve my writing skills significantly. Her guidance and support have been crucial to my academic progress.

My mother is always there for me. Whenever I need help, she’s the first one to provide it. She paid for my English training fees when I first entered college. It was a big sum of money at the time. That training course has helped me a lot since then.

sub_questions.content: How much time do you spend on your studies each week?

answers.content: On average, I dedicate about 35 hours per week to my studies. This amounts to roughly 5 hours of study each day. I find that this study routine helps me effectively manage my coursework and allows me to maintain a good balance between academics and other activities.

I devote approximately 30 to 35 hours per week to my studies. This includes attending lectures, participating in tutorials, conducting research, completing assignments, and preparing for examinations. I believe it’s crucial to allocate a significant amount of time to academic pursuits in order to excel in my chosen field of study. Additionally, I organize my schedule effectively to ensure a balance between my academic commitments and other aspects of life, such as personal interests, extracurricular activities, and socializing with friends.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to study in the mornings or in the afternoons?

answers.content: I would prefer to study in the afternoons because I usually feel more awake and alert during that time. Mornings are quite tiring for me, so I find it easier to focus on my studies later in the day. Afternoons also give me a chance to review the concepts I learned in the morning.

I wish I was a morning person because I’m actually more productive in the morning. However, it’s so difficult for me to rise early in the morning, so I have to arrange most of my study plans in the afternoons. I’m so used to this study schedule now.

sub_questions.content: Are you looking forward to working?

answers.content: Absolutely! I am eagerly anticipating the opportunity to delve into the world of work. I firmly believe that gaining practical experience will complement my academic knowledge and enhance my future prospects. By working and furthering my studies, I aim to achieve my long-term goal of earning a postgraduate degree.

Yes, absolutely. I am working part time at the moment. I like to make my own money because it gives me freedom. I’m looking forward to my graduation when I can have a proper job, make a decent income and enjoy being able to manage my own money.

sub_questions.content: Is it very interesting?

answers.content: I think so. First of all, studying English literature means that you can not only learn a language but also get to know a culture. Moreover, it gives me a chance to read a lot of great books. I will never pick up some of the books if they hadn't been on the syllabus. Whilst the occasional book or style of writing may not resonate with you, it's really interesting and important to read books out of the box.

Learning another language is not only an interesting experience but also a challenging one. It's full of fun when it comes to American and British humor. With excellent English, you can watch English TV series and movies without subtitles. But on the other hand, it requires substantial amount of patience to remember a wide range of vocabulary and to use it in the appropriate situation.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your subject?(Why?/Why not?)

answers.content: Yes, of course I do. I would say I love the major I’m studying because learning another language is never simply about the grammar or the words themselves. It's more about the cultural background, serving as a way for communication and passing on the wisdom of humanity from generation to generation.

sub_questions.content: Why did you choose to study that subject?/Why did you choose to study those subjects?

answers.content: I chose to study Economics because I have always been fascinated by how individuals and societies make decisions about allocating resources. I find analyzing markets, policies, and trends to be both intellectually stimulating and relevant to addressing societal issues. The dynamic nature of economics and its widespread applicability to various real-world situations further fueled my interest in pursuing this field of study.

Well, finance wasn’t my primary choice. To be honest, I was convinced by my parents that it was the most worthwhile subject to learn. Perhaps they were right. What I’ve learned has helped me a great deal in real life. I think I can think more rationally nowadays about any financial decisions I make.

sub_questions.content: Do you think that your subject is popular in your country?

answers.content: Yes, I do believe that my subject, which is Business, is quite popular in my country. With the increasing growth and importance of commerce and market, studying business has become a popular choice among students here. Business-related programs are in high demand as they provide opportunities for better career prospects, entrepreneurship, and economic development. The practicality and versatility of the business field appeal to many individuals, making it a sought-after subject in my country.

Yes, I believe that law is indeed a popular subject in my country. With the increasing demand for legal professionals and the significance of upholding justice, many students are drawn towards studying law. The evolving legal system and frequent high-profile cases have also contributed to its popularity and the desire to pursue a career in this field. In addition, the diverse career opportunities offered by the legal profession have further attracted individuals to take an interest in law.

sub_questions.content: Do you have any plans for your studies in the next five years?

answers.content: Yes, I certainly have plans for my studies in the next five years. Firstly, I aim to pursue a master's degree in my field of interest. This will further enhance my knowledge and make me more competitive in the job market. Additionally, I plan to engage in internships or research projects to gain practical experience. Moreover, I will attend various workshops and seminars to stay updated with the latest advancements in my field. Ultimately, these plans will help me achieve my career goals and become a successful professional.

In the next five years, my plans for studies include expanding my knowledge in various fields, such as computer science and psychology. I aim to pursue higher education and engage in research projects to further understand these subjects. Additionally, I plan to participate in internships or exchange programs to gain practical experiences and broaden my international perspective. Overall, my goal is to become a well-rounded individual through dedicated studies and constant learning.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to change your major?

answers.content: Yes, I do want to change my major. After careful consideration and self-reflection, I have realized that my current major does not align with my true passion and career goals. I feel that pursuing a different major will allow me to explore new areas of interest and develop skills that are relevant to the industry I aspire to work in. Additionally, changing my major will provide me with a fresh academic perspective, enabling me to broaden my knowledge and enhance my future job prospects.

No, I do not want to change my major. I have always been passionate about my field of study and I believe it aligns perfectly with my career goals. I enjoy the challenges and opportunities that my major offers, and I am excited to pursue further knowledge and skills in this area.

sub_questions.content: What changes would you like to see in your school?

answers.content: I would like to see an upgrade in the school's gymnasium. It would be great if we could have newer and more advanced sports equipment, as well as improved facilities for various sports activities. A renovated gym would encourage students to engage more in physical activities and promote a healthier lifestyle. It would also provide a better environment for both practice and competitions, fostering a sense of competitiveness and teamwork among students. Overall, an updated gym would enhance the overall sports culture in our school.

I would like to see an update in our school's library, with more diverse and current book selections, as well as modern technology for research and learning purposes. This would greatly enhance our academic experience and provide us with a wider range of resources for our studies.

sub_questions.content: What do you think is the most important at the moment?

answers.content: At this moment, I believe the most crucial thing is maintaining good mental health. With the fast-paced world we live in, managing stress and staying positive is essential. For example, many people now practice mindfulness or meditation to cope. Prioritizing inner well-being helps improve overall productivity and happiness.

I believe that personal well-being and mental health are the most important at the moment. In the current fast-paced and demanding work environment, it is crucial to prioritize self-care and ensure a healthy work-life balance. Taking breaks, engaging in hobbies, and maintaining good relationships with loved ones can help reduce stress and improve overall happiness. By taking care of ourselves, we can perform better at work and lead a more fulfilling life.

sub_questions.content: What work do you do?

answers.content: I am an English teacher in a senior high school. Besides teaching English, I also teach my students how to read and write well, how to understand what they read, how to learn from their peers, and how to have productive and challenging conversations. I think my students can understand more complex thoughts and ideas than others. Therefore, I start having intelligent, challenging discussions with them. I will bring the classics like Shakespeare or Chaucer, and open up their minds to a world of reading that doesn't just revolve around vampire books.

I’m working as a teacher at XXX high school/ training organization right now. Usually I teach English lessons four days a week. Correcting my students' homework and other assignments is an essential part of my job. I also spend time taking care of and playing with the students outside the classroom.

sub_questions.content: Do you miss being a student?

answers.content: Yes. I often reminisce about my student days, yearning for the comforting ambiance of the campus. The bustling corridors, the lively chatter in the cafeteria, and the thrill of exploring new subjects, all hold a special place in my heart. The vibrant energy of the student community always left me feeling invigorated and inspired.

Yes and no. I miss the worry-free time as a student. Studying hard was my mission. I didn’t have to worry about rent, office affairs, work productivity and so on. However, when I didn’t have the freedom to make financial decisions, I had to discuss almost every financial matter with my parents. That’s the part I don’t miss.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to change jobs?

answers.content: No, I am content with my current job and do not feel the need to change at the moment. I find my work engaging and fulfilling, which allows me to grow both personally and professionally. Changing jobs is not on my agenda as I value the opportunities and challenges my current role presents.

I’m quite content with my current job and don’t have any immediate plans to switch to another position. I find my work fulfilling and enjoy the challenges and opportunities it offers. Additionally, I have developed valuable skills and expertise in my field, which I believe will help me grow and advance in my career. Therefore, I’m committed to continuing my journey in my current role.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of technology do you use at work?

answers.content: At my workplace, we utilize various types of technology to enhance efficiency and productivity. For instance, we rely on advanced software programs for data analysis, communication tools like video conferencing, and specialized equipment such as 3D printers for prototyping. These technologies greatly facilitate our work processes and collaborations.

In my current job, I use various technologies to efficiently carry out my tasks. These include software such as Microsoft Office for document creation and data analysis. Additionally, I utilize project management tools like Trello or Asana to organize and collaborate on team projects. Moreover, I use communication tools like Slack or Microsoft Teams to facilitate seamless communication with colleagues and clients. Furthermore, I’m familiar with industry-specific software and database management systems that are essential for my profession.

sub_questions.content: Is your work interesting?

answers.content: Yes, I find my work quite fascinating. I work in the field of healthcare research where I get to analyze data and contribute to improving patient care. It's rewarding to know that my work has a positive impact on people's lives.

It’s not particularly exciting, but it’s very practical. You know, finance is related to almost every aspect of our life. Also, it’s a hot major in the job market. With financial knowledge, I could at least find a decent job and live a reasonable life.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your job?

answers.content: Yes, I truly enjoy my job as a teacher. It is fulfilling to inspire and empower students to achieve their full potential. Every day presents new challenges and opportunities for growth, allowing me to continuously learn and develop as an educator. The sense of pride and satisfaction in seeing my students succeed is incredibly rewarding. Teaching is not just a profession for me, but a passion that brings me great joy.

I’m very fond of my job. Actually, teaching is more of a career instead of a job to me. As a child, I always dreamed of being a teacher, standing on the stage in front of the school. I love embracing my students every day and passing on the knowledge I have accumulated over the years. I’ve always had this feeling that as long as I stay with my students, I will always be energetic and passionate.

sub_questions.content: Why did you choose to do that type of work (or, that job)?

answers.content: I chose to work in this field because I have always been passionate about helping others. The job allows me to make a positive impact on people's lives and contribute to society. Moreover, I find the work intellectually stimulating and enjoy the opportunity to constantly learn and grow in my role.

There’s no doubt that teachers play an important role in students' lives. Back in my school days, I was lucky to encounter many excellent teachers who’ve had a profound influence on me. Their passion for teaching and patience for caring inspired me to be a teacher myself. With this idea in mind, I chose to study English at university with the aim of becoming an English teacher.

sub_questions.content: What are the benefits of being your age?

answers.content: As a 25-year-old working professional, there are several benefits that come with my age. Firstly, I have the energy and enthusiasm to take up new challenges and work towards my career goals. Secondly, being in my mid-20s allows me to have a good balance between youthfulness and maturity, which helps me adapt to different social and professional settings. Additionally, at this age, I have gained some work experience and developed a certain level of expertise in my field, which gives me an edge in my job. Lastly, I have a strong network of friends and colleagues within my age group, providing a sense of support and camaraderie in both personal and professional life.

Being a 20-year-old university student has its perks. Firstly, I have the energy and enthusiasm to explore various opportunities and try new things. Secondly, this age provides a perfect balance between youthful playfulness and adult responsibilities. Additionally, I can learn and adapt swiftly, making it easier to grasp new knowledge and acquire skills. Lastly, I have a chance to develop resilience and learn from mistakes while forming an identity that will shape my future.

sub_questions.content: What requirements did you need to meet to get current job？

answers.content: To secure my current job, I had to fulfill several requirements. Firstly, having a relevant educational background was crucial. I needed at least a bachelor's degree in my field. Additionally, I had to possess prior experience and demonstrate strong skills in areas related to my job role. Fluency in English was a must, as it is the primary language used in my workplace. Moreover, I needed to excel in the interview process, showcasing my communication abilities, problem-solving skills, and adaptability. Meeting these qualifications significantly contributed to my successful attainment of my current job.

To obtain my current job, I had to fulfill several prerequisites. Firstly, a bachelor's degree in a related field was essential. Additionally, a minimum of two years of relevant work experience was required. Fluency in both spoken and written English was crucial, as the job demanded effective communication with international clients. Proficiency in using computer software and analytical skills were also essential. Furthermore, a strong ability to work in a team, problem-solving aptitude, and adaptability were highly valued qualities.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your subject?

answers.content: I thoroughly enjoy my subject. It allows me to delve deep into the realms of knowledge and expand my horizons. The diverse range of topics covered in my subject truly fascinates me, keeping me engaged and motivated. Additionally, my professors are highly experienced and passionate, creating an inspiring learning environment. Overall, I feel fortunate to have chosen a subject that ignites my curiosity and fuels my thirst for learning.

Of course. I think it is my love towards this subject that keeps me continuing my study. Studying English actually broadens my horizon. Learning English helps me to enhance my knowledge of every field over the world through reading English documents which are not only in books, newspapers but also on the internet.

sub_questions.content: Do you have any plants for your work in the next five years?

answers.content: Oh, absolutely! In the next five years, I'm planning to thrive in my career by exploring new opportunities, acquiring additional skills, and taking on challenging projects. I aim to enhance my problem-solving abilities, develop leadership qualities, and build a strong professional network. Furthermore, I hope to contribute to my field through research and innovation, ultimately making a positive impact. Overall, I'm excited about the possibilities that lie ahead and can't wait to see where my hard work and determination take me!

Yes, I do have plans for my work in the next five years. I aspire to excel in my current role and gain expertise in my field. Additionally, I plan to pursue further education or professional certifications to enhance my skills. Moreover, I aim to take on more challenging projects and leadership roles, allowing me to contribute significantly to my organization's growth. Ultimately, my goal is to climb the corporate ladder and make a positive impact in my industry.

sub_questions.content: What technology do you use at work?

answers.content: At work, I utilize various technologies to enhance efficiency and productivity. This includes software such as project management tools, video conferencing platforms, and data analysis software. Additionally, I use hardware such as computers and mobile devices to access online resources, communicate with colleagues, and complete tasks. These technologies have revolutionized the way we work and collaborate, allowing for seamless communication and streamlined workflows.

At work, I use a variety of technologies to enhance productivity and efficiency. These include computer software such as Microsoft Office for document creation and analysis, project management tools for task coordination, and video conferencing platforms for virtual meetings. Moreover, I am adept at utilizing cloud storage systems for data organization and collaboration with colleagues. Additionally, I frequently employ email and instant messaging applications for communication and coordination. Overall, these technologies greatly support and streamline my work processes.

sub_questions.content: Did you learn about the history of your hometown at school?

answers.content: Actually, not really. In history class, the teacher only focused on textbooks. We were asked to memorize the historical events of the world and of our country, but nobody taught us about our hometown. Perhaps it’s too small to be mentioned.

sub_questions.content: What’s your hometown famous for?

answers.content: My hometown is renowned for its vibrant street food culture, particularly for the mouth-watering local delicacy called "Dragon's Breath". This popular snack consists of liquid-nitrogen-infused cereal puffs that emit a dramatic mist when eaten. It's a hit among both locals and tourists, adding a touch of whimsy to our culinary scene.

sub_questions.content: How long have you lived there?

answers.content: I have been a resident there for approximately five wonderful years now. It has been such a pleasure to call that place my home. The community is vibrant, and the surrounding nature is simply breathtaking, making everyday life serene. Living there has enriched my life in countless ways.
I’ve lived here for 18 years. I left for Beijing when I went to college. It’s quite challenging for me to get out of my comfort zone. However, I made it.

sub_questions.content: Is that a big city or a small place?

answers.content: That's actually a mid-sized city with a population of around half a million people. It's not too hectic like the big metropolises, but it's also not a tiny town where everyone knows each other. I find it has a nice balance between urban amenities and a slower pace of life, which suits me quite well.

Chengdu is a big city. I think it is the biggest city in southwest China. Moreover, it is a fast-developing city. What’s fascinating about Chengdu is that it is not only a comfortable city for senior citizens to enjoy their old ages, but also a city with a lot of opportunities where young people can work hard and fulfill their dreams.

sub_questions.content: What's (the name of) your hometown ?

answers.content: My hometown is Chengdu, the capital city of Sichuan Province.

sub_questions.content: Do you think you will continue living there for a long time?

answers.content: Good question! I imagine I will. You see, I spend most of the year living in the city, because my work is in the city. However, I have plans to go back home someday in the future. When I’ve made enough money to retire, I will move back to my hometown.

sub_questions.content: Is there anything you dislike about it?

answers.content: Well, if I have to name one, then it’s the poor job prospects. Since it’s only a small town, the government doesn’t have any plans to invest in it. Compared to big cities, it has poor infrastructure and transportation facilities, so there aren’t many promising jobs there.

sub_questions.content: What do you like about your hometown?

answers.content: Well, the most attractive thing about my hometown is that it has a number of scenic spots, like the West Lake, and Lingyin Temple. People of various age groups can enjoy or entertain themselves when they visit the West Lake. Retired people usually sing opera near that area.

sub_questions.content: Do you like living there?

answers.content: Of course. I made many friends while I was growing up. Most of them settled down in my hometown. I like to hang out with them. Also, my family all live in my hometown. If I live there, I can spend time with my parents too. Above all, I enjoy the great scenery back home.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your hometown?

answers.content: Definitely. I often tell my city friends about my hometown. The greenery, the nature, the sweet people, and the quiet evenings have captured me deep inside. I’ve traveled to many places, but I believe my hometown has the best to offer, at least to me.

sub_questions.content: How long have you been living there?

answers.content: I lived there until I turned 18. I left my hometown for university but in the past few years I have traveled back to my hometown at least twice a year to spend holidays. To me, it’s always the best place to be.

sub_questions.content: Please describe your hometown a little.

answers.content: It is located in southwest China. It is the capital of Sichuan province and metropolis of southeast China. It is a big city with a convenient traffic system. The surrounding Chengdu Plain is also known as the "Country of Heaven" and the "Land of Abundance". I am very proud of my hometown.

Well, it’s a small town where the weather is mild. There’s no major public transport facilities like a train station or airport, however, the roads are solid and lead to all directions. Traveling is easy if you have a vehicle. Most importantly, people there are very nice and modest.

sub_questions.content: Is your hometown a big city or a small place?

answers.content: My hometown is a small town. It has a small population so there’s not even a train station, but it’s quite a livable place. It isn’t busy or crowded like a big city. People can manage their lives at a slow pace. Life is pretty enjoyable in a small town.

sub_questions.content: Where would you like to live?

answers.content: Ideally, I would be able to afford an apartment in the city that I’m working in now, and a town house in my hometown. I’ll live in the city during the weekdays and travel back to my hometown to spend the weekends and holidays.

sub_questions.content: Do you plan to continue living there?

answers.content: I plan to buy a house in my hometown when I can afford it, and quite likely, I will travel back and forth between my hometown and the city that I’m living in now. That’s because even though my hometown is beautiful, it doesn’t have the kind of jobs that I am interested in.

sub_questions.content: Where's your hometown?

answers.content: I come from a small town in the middle of the country. It’s an agricultural town so the scenery is pretty stunning with plenty of trees and bushes lining both sides of the streets. The buildings aren’t very tall like in big cities, and people are quite nice, so it’s a very lovely place.

sub_questions.content: Do you wear a watch?

sub_questions.content: Why do some people wear expensive watches?

For example, rich men buy expensive watches to show off their social status, while some women like to wear luxurious watches to match their expensive clothes. Sometimes it’s about status, sometimes it’s about fashion.

Not really. Watches aren’t necessary ornaments for me at all. If it’s for checking the time, I can totally read the time on my phone. On the other hand, I’m not like rich or anything, so I have nothing to show off by a watch. So watches are not must-have items for me.

sub_questions.content: Do you think car colours are important？

answers.content: I'd say car colours do matter, but not for everyone. From a personal perspective, colour reflects the owner's taste and identity. For example, someone who chooses a bright red or blue car probably wants to stand out, while a person who picks grey or black might prefer a more classic or understated look. On a practical level, lighter colours like white or silver are safer because they're more visible in low light. Also, some colours have better resale value than others. So overall, while it's not the most important factor, car colour still plays a role.

Yes, I think car colours are quite important. First, colour shows a person's style and personality. For example, some people like black because it looks cool and professional, while others prefer white because it's clean and simple. Second, colour can affect safety. Bright colours like red or yellow are easier to see on the road, especially at night. So I believe choosing a car colour is not just about looking good, but also about being practical.

sub_questions.content: Did you enjoy traveling by car when you were a kid?

answers.content: I didn’t really like traveling by car when I was a kid. Back in my childhood, the road was in terrible condition, with all the ups and downs. As a result, I always felt carsick whenever traveling by car. I remember a time when my father drove me to school, and I was sitting at the back of the car. Suddenly, I just felt sick and lay down at the backrow. I couldn’t remember a single word during the class for the whole morning. It was not until lunch break that I gradually recover from that. So, traveling by car was a misery for me when I was young.

Yes of course. My dad bought his first car when I was in kindergarten. For me back then, traveling by car would mean picnics at the weekends. There were fewer cars on the road then so more convenient to go to the countryside. As a result, traveling by car not only made it possible for me to go to faraway places, such as nearby parks or a neighboring city, but also made the trip more comfortable. As a result, I really enjoyed that.

sub_questions.content: What types of cars do you like?

answers.content: I prefer electric vehicles to traditional fossil fuel cars. First of all, I like driving very much, and electric vehicles offer better driving experience with more responsive acceleration. Besides, it’s cheaper to drive electric vehicles since electricity costs far less compared to fossil fuel. There's also cheaper service and maintenance plus low or no vehicle tax. Last but not least, electrical vehicles are environment friendly. With all the extreme weathers raging nowadays, climate change has become a pressing issue for us all. I think it’s the obligation of each and every person to protect the environment. In this sense, electric cars outperform traditional ones in terms of zero carbon emission.

I prefer small cars like Smart and Mini. First of all, for urban residents like me, finding a parking place in the city center is always a headache. As a result, it’s easier to park a small car. You don’t need to worry when parking it on a narrow road or driving it through a narrow alley. Besides, small cars cost less compared to larger SUVs. It requires less fossil fuel, which not only saves money, but also contributes to environment protection. As a result, I prefer smaller cars to bigger ones.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to be a driver or a passenger?

answers.content: I prefer to be a passenger. Driving is an exhausting task, especially during dawn and dusk when the light was dim. Drivers need to focus on the road, which is quite tiring and may harm the eyes. Besides, after a whole day of work, it’s quite troublesome when you come across a terrible traffic jam when driving back home. You have lost all your patience and energy at workplace, but you still need to cope with the hustle and bustle, endure the annoying horn and maybe sacrifice the whole evening if there occurred an accident. As a result, I prefer to be a passenger, so that I can have a rest at the backrow and even take a short sleep before arriving at the destination.

I prefer driving cars. Compared to passengers, drivers can take a better control of the trip. I enjoy the sense of feeling of controlling the direction. I think driving itself is highly enjoyable. . Whether you want to go slower to enjoy the view or faster to experience speed, is totally your decision. Besides, I may get carsick when sitting at the back of the car. When driving, you tend not to get motion sick, compared to back seat passengers , which, according to research, is perhaps because I can anticipate the movement of the car.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do when there is a traffic jam?

answers.content: I’ll search for shortcuts when there’s a traffic jam in front of my car. As more people are using the GPS nowadays, sometimes the app will guide everyone onto the same road. As a result, a traffic jam took place. However, there’re alternatives that can bring you to the same place. Since I’m pretty familiar with the road system, I will look for other routes. But if I’m already in the middle of the traffic jam, then I will just wait patiently and keep alert to other cars who might have the intention to cut in the queue.

Well, if I'm stuck in a traffic jam, my first reaction is often to get restless. You know, I start complaining and keep looking out of the window to estimate how long I'll have to wait. Though once I manage to calm down, I like to play some soft music or play games on my phone.

Her teaching style is unique and engaging, making every lesson enjoyable. She incorporates various interactive activities and discussions that enhance our language skills. Additionally, she encourages us to read extensively, expanding our vocabulary and understanding of different cultures.

Yes, I do have a favorite teacher. In particular, my Chinese language teacher holds a special place in my heart. Her name is Ms. Li, and she has been my teacher for the past three years. What makes her unique is her passion for teaching and her commitment to helping her students succeed. She always goes the extra mile to make her classes engaging and interactive, using various teaching methods and incorporating real-life examples. I appreciate her patience and dedication in ensuring that each student understands the content thoroughly.

sub_questions.content: Who is your favourite teacher?

Mr. Joseph's teaching style is a perfect fit for my learning needs and I greatly appreciate his dedication and commitment to student success.

sub_questions.content: In what way has your favourite teacher helped you?

This not only enhances my understanding but also accelerates my ability to retain information.

My favorite teacher has greatly helped me in developing my thinking skills and organizing my daily tasks effectively. sub_questions.content: Do you have a teacher from your past that you still remember?

Ms. Wang's dedication and commitment to her students left a lasting impression on me and I will always be grateful for her guidance.

Yes, I had a remarkable teacher from my middle school whom I still vividly remember. Mrs. Joanna was an exceptional English teacher who had an immense impact on my life. I recall her organizing creative writing competitions, where we had the chance to showcase our imagination and language skills. Furthermore, she would often share personal stories and experiences, giving us a broader perspective on various topics.

sub_questions.content: Are you still in touch with your primary school teachers?

However, I do hope to reconnect with them in the future if I am able to find a way to get in touch. It would be wonderful to catch up with them and reminisce about my time in primary school.

Yes, I am still in touch with my primary school teachers. They played an influential role in my education and I appreciate their guidance. We have regular reunions and keep in touch through social media platforms. Recently, I reached out to one of my favorite teachers to seek advice on a career decision. It's wonderful to maintain a connection with them as they continue to inspire and support me even after all these years.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to be a teacher in the future?

answers.content: My future career aspirations are still evolving. While I have considered the possibility of becoming a teacher, I am also exploring other potential paths. Ultimately, my goal is to pursue a career that allows me to make a positive impact on the world and continuously grow both personally and professionally.

Yeah, well, I don’t mind teaching. To a point, I think teaching is the most rewarding job in the world. Because you are cultivating the next generation for this society. Your devotion will be paid off in the future. So yeah, if I am qualified to be a teacher, I will make it my career.

sub_questions.content: Do you think teachers should be angry at students or not?

answers.content: I definitely don’t think a teacher being angry with students would help the situation. A good teacher should never lose his/her cool. When a student makes a mistake, either in study or behavior, the signal he/she sends out is that he/she needs help from the teacher. Losing temper is not gonna solve the student’s confusion.

sub_questions.content: What's the different between young and old teachers?

answers.content: Well, it's like comparing apples and oranges. In my experience, most young teachers are energetic, creative and can get along easily with students. However, old teachers have their advantages too. They are experienced in teaching, so they convey knowledge efficiently and are good at guiding students to think.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of teachers do you like?

answers.content: I get along well with those teachers who are friendly, patient and have a good sense of humor. I mean, I’m quite sensitive. My teacher’s attitude can affect me greatly. Also, it’s important that the teacher knows how to guide me. I only learn efficiently when I am guided gently and patiently.

questions.content: In what conditions, you won't use headphones？

Do you use headphones？

sub_questions.content: In what conditions, you won't use headphones？

answers.content: I never use the headphones when I’m in the car, or on a plane. ‘cause I feel that there’s already enough noise from the vehicle running, my ears really can’t afford any more of that. As much as I love to enjoy music, I do worry about my hearing safety.

sub_questions.content: When would you use headphones？

answers.content: I use them when I want to enjoy some good music. You know, with the headphones, I can get better solid quality of the sound. And also, when I’m having online classes, I need the headphones, ‘cause then they help me focus on the class content, rather than being distracted by the outside noise.

sub_questions.content: Which ones do you use more in your country？

answers.content: Do you mean what type of headphones we use more in China? Well, the most widely advertised ones in the market are the on-ears headphones, I think. They are small and easy to carry. Besides, they are relatively cheap so even students can afford them. So, yeah, those ones are more popular.

sub_questions.content: What do you do with them? Why？

answers.content: Well, as I mentioned earlier, I use them to listen to some music and watch shows on a daily basis. But these days I’ve also been using them for some online classes, due to the COVID-19 lockdown. Yeah, they’ve kinda become living necessities for me.

sub_questions.content: In what situations would you not use headphones？

answers.content: Headphones are portable and widely used in our lives, although there are certain situations when they are not needed. Firstly, I would not use them when eating because they would distract me from my food. Secondly, I never use them when sleeping because that might be harmful for my ears.

sub_questions.content: Which are used more in your country, headphones or microphones？

answers.content: Compared with microphones, headphones are used far more in my country. First of all, people need more privacy than ever before, which is where headphones are extremely useful. Secondly, headphones have become commonplace in our working lives, since we can use them to join a conference call or to make a phone call while driving, and so on. So these are some of the reasons why headphones are widely used in my country.

sub_questions.content: Do you use headphones？

answers.content: Yeah, I use it every day. Well, I live in a shared flat with three roommates. We all love to listen to music and watch shows before bed. And the thing is that, we have very different tastes in music. So, without headphones, I’m afraid we’d be very unhappy with each other.

People are more reliant on headphones nowadays, and I am no exception. In my spare time, I love to enjoy some quiet time by putting in my headphones and playing some of my favorite music. During work, they’re always in my ears, because I need to receive many phone calls every day, and in order to be more efficient, I like to communicate via headphones rather than typing emails.

sub_questions.content: What do you do with headphones?

answers.content: Headphones have become an absolute necessity for me because I need them every day. For example, I always listen to BBC news when I wake up in the morning, so I use them in order not to make any noise. Also, to help me relax before sleep, I listen to some gentle music which I need my headphones for. Most importantly, they provide me with privacy in whatever I do.

sub_questions.content: When do you use headphones？

answers.content: I actually use my headphones quite often because the Bluetooth technology behind them makes them so easy to use. For example, in my spare time, I love listening to BBC news or some relaxing music. What’s more, I usually wear them when driving and sending emails in case of any urgent phone calls. In a word, headphones bring me so much convenience.

sub_questions.content: What kind of headphones do you use？

answers.content: Well, I use the shut-back earphones. Actually, I’ve tried many different types of earphones, the in-ears ones, on-ears ones and earbuds as well. I found that the shut-back earphones offer better solid quality. And they can completely disconnect you from the outer world.

Among all the headphones on the market, I prefer to use Bluetooth headsets, which connect to mobile phones via Bluetooth instead of a cable, making them extremely free and easy to use. This also makes them much more comfortable to wear than a wired headset.

questions.content: Do you prefer online shopping or in-store shopping？
How often do you go shopping？
Do you think expensive products are always better than cheaper ones?
Is it difficult for you to make choices (when you shop)?
Do you compare prices when you shop? Why?
Do you like shopping?

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer online shopping or in-store shopping？

answers.content: It depends on what I'm buying. For everyday items like books, electronics, or household goods, I definitely prefer online shopping because it's time-saving and often cheaper. I can compare prices easily and read reviews from other customers. However, when it comes to clothes, shoes, or furniture, I prefer going to a physical store. I want to see the quality, try things on, or test the product before making a decision. So I'd say I use both, depending on the situation.

I prefer online shopping, to be honest. It's very convenient because I can buy things anytime, even late at night. There are also more choices online, and prices are often cheaper than in physical stores. But sometimes I still go to real shops, especially for clothes or shoes, because I want to try them on before buying. So overall, online shopping is my first choice, but in-store shopping is still useful sometimes.

sub_questions.content: How often do you go shopping？

answers.content: I wouldn't say I shop very often. For essentials like food and household items, I go to the supermarket roughly twice a week. As for clothes, shoes, or electronics, I only shop when necessary — maybe once a month or even less. I'm not really into window shopping or browsing for fun because I find it a bit tiring and time-consuming. So overall, I'd describe myself as a practical shopper who buys things only when needed.

It depends. For groceries and daily necessities, I go shopping about once or twice a week. I usually go to a supermarket near my home. For clothes or other things I don't need urgently, I shop online maybe once or twice a month. So overall, I'm not a person who shops every day. I prefer to buy things only when I really need them.

sub_questions.content: Do you think expensive products are always better than cheaper ones?

answers.content: No, I don't believe that expensive products are always better than cheaper ones. While price can sometimes be an indicator of quality, it's not always the case. There are many factors to consider, such as brand reputation, materials used, and personal preferences. Sometimes cheaper products can offer excellent value for money, while expensive ones may not necessarily meet one's needs or expectations. It's essential to evaluate products based on their merits rather than solely on their price tag.

I disagree with this. It really depends on the quality of the product. For example, when a new business starts, the prices of the products may be low, however, this doesn’t mean that the quality is worse than products from more established companies. Price is often associated with branding. Branded products are more expensive than those unbranded. Some are more expensive because they are actually great products worth the money while others may be just the same or even not quite as go.

sub_questions.content: Is it difficult for you to make choices (when you shop)?

answers.content: Sometimes it can be challenging for me to make choices when I shop, especially when there are numerous options available or when I'm purchasing something important. I tend to weigh the pros and cons, consider my needs and preferences, and sometimes seek advice from friends or family to make the best decision.

I think I rarely hesitate while making choices. In fact, I always have this visceral feeling about things. While shopping, I can tell instinctively whether I want it or not. And of course, sometimes you have to make a hard choice between two equally desirable things, but still I always make up my mind quickly as to which one is my favorite.

sub_questions.content: Do you compare prices when you shop? Why?

answers.content: Yes, I often compare prices when I shop, as it helps me make informed decisions and ensure that I'm getting the best value for my money. By comparing prices across different stores or online platforms, I can find the most cost-effective option without compromising on quality.

Yes, I do compare prices when I shop as there is a Chinese saying that goes “one has to shop around for excellent quality at a reasonable price.” In other words, if you don’t compare prices in stores, you will never know whether you’ve made the best choice or not.

sub_questions.content: Do you like shopping?

answers.content: Yes, I do enjoy shopping, especially when I have the time to browse and explore different stores or markets. It can be quite a fun and rewarding experience, whether I'm shopping for clothes, gadgets, or groceries.

I like shopping. I mean shopping is a great way to relieve stress as long as you don’t overdo it. Also, because you often shop with friends, it’s a great chance for you to connect with one another by creating shared memories. Moreover, putting on beautiful and stylish clothes will actually make you feel good about yourself, thus boosting one’s self-confidence.

sub_questions.content: Did you use to keep your room tidy as a child？

answers.content: To be honest, I was quite a messy child. I rarely kept my room tidy because I was too busy playing or watching cartoons. My desk was usually covered with schoolbooks, toys, and snack wrappers. My parents often reminded me to clean up, but I would just push things under the bed or into the closet. Looking back, I think I was just lazy, but as I grew older, I gradually learned to be more organized.

Not really, to be honest. When I was a child, I didn't like tidying my room. My desk was always full of books and pens, and my clothes were everywhere on the floor. My mother often got angry and told me to clean up. Sometimes I did, but usually I just left everything there until she helped me. So I would say I was quite messy as a child.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to keep things tidy？

answers.content: Yes, definitely. I'm someone who really appreciates a clean and organized space. I find that when my surroundings are tidy, I feel calmer and more productive. I usually make my bed every morning and put things back in their place after using them. That said, I'm not obsessive about it. If I'm busy with exams or projects, I might let things slide for a few days, but I always tidy up afterwards. So yes, I do like keeping things tidy.

Yes, I do like to keep things tidy, but I'm not super strict about it. I feel better when my desk is clean and my clothes are put away. A tidy room helps me focus on my work or study. However, sometimes when I'm very busy, my room can get a little messy. But I always clean it up at the end of the week. So overall, I'd say I like tidiness.

sub_questions.content: Are you a tidy person?

answers.content: I’m a messy person by nature. Ever since I was a kid, I never tried to be organized but I always knew where my things were, under the bed, in the corner, on the top of my cat’s head. As I’m fully aware of the whereabouts of my personal stuff, I’m really angry at my mom when she tries to organize my room and get me out of the chaos I am so used to.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people should be tidy all the time?

answers.content: No. It is unnecessary to keep yourself or your place tidy all the time. You clean the place when you are in a good mood, but there are times in your life you need to loosen yourself, no energy for room-cleaning. I believe that you have to give a full spleen to your anger, sorrowfulness or so; messing up the surrounding is one of the effective ways.

sub_questions.content: Are you tidier at work or at home?

answers.content: I’m tidier at home. At home, I’m a tidy person, while at the workplace, I’m just someone else. For me, the workplace is a place to show your efficiency and proficiency; spending too much time on cleaning is definitely uneconomic. That doesn’t mean that I refuse to clean the work table, because the last task on my daily schedule is to get all the trash off it.

sub_questions.content: Is your room tidy?

answers.content: No. My room doesn’t exactly look like a shipwreck or anything, but it isn’t exactly tidy and organized either. I always misplace things and lose stuff and just put things at random places as I go about my life. But I am bothered when it becomes too messy for my liking; once a week or so I’d go on a cleaning spree and put things away where they should be.

sub_questions.content: What do you think of a tidy house?

answers.content: It’s definitely great to have a tidy house. You could enjoy the benefits of maintaining the house tidy. Just a little time every day on maintenance frees us from the burden of hours-long clean jobs on weekends. It makes cleaning much easier and you feel much less stressful. And a tidy house also means that you’ll always know exactly where to find whatever you need.

sub_questions.content: Do people think you are a tidy person?

answers.content: No, they don’t think so. I never do two-faced things, like stashing the dirty clothes away when someone is paying a visit, or doing exquisite makeup for a party while living like a vagabond at home. I have only one face: untidy me. People frown upon it once they come to my room, but the fact is that living untidily is not as unbearable as people might think. I feel free, because I’m not trapped with the housework.

sub_questions.content: Do Chinese people often visit science museums?

Adults are so busy with their daily work and social life, not many would can spare time for science museums.

I'm not sure if the majority Chinese are interested in the science museums. You see, most Chinese parents would like to cultivate their children's interest in science. So they may pay to send their children to these museums, but for themselves, I'm afraid they would rather spend their spare time somewhere else, like a theater, rather than visiting museums.

sub_questions.content: Do you like watching science TV programs?

answers.content: Yes，I do. I like to watch science documentaries on the TV. I usually watch the documentaries made by the American Nasa, where they always update new discoveries in time. I like to learn about the new information and improvements that the science has made.

Yep! I like to watch science documentaries, especially those that explore the secrets in the universe. Actually there is a science program sponsored by Nasa in America. I watch that regularly. Sometimes I would even invite friends over to enjoy the show together.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of interesting things you have done with science?

answers.content: When I was a primary student, my science teacher guided me to do a lot of science experiments. I learned many chemistry reactions in the school lab. Even today, when I think about it, I still think those experiments were quite interesting.

I used to do an experiment related to nutrition on myself. I was curious about the impacts of carb on human body, so I decided to stop taking in carbs for a week to see if there are any changes on my body. I felt tired and anxious on the fourth day after stopping eating carbs and felt a bit dizzy on the fifth day. So, I stopped the experiment on the sixth day because I felt really bad.

sub_questions.content: Which science subject is interesting to you?

answers.content: I am quite interested in learning about the chemistry reactions. However, what intrigues me even more is astronomy. I watch BBC astronomy documentaries regularly. Last year, I even spent a big deal of money for a professional telescope. But it’s totally worth it. I’ve got so much fun from looking through this telescope lenses.

I like biology the most. We started to learn biology in the middle school. Because I am very into human bodies, I am deeply attracted to how human body functions which is a core part in biology. I am obsessed with how cells work with each other to become organs, blood and many other important parts in our body.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of interesting things have you done with science?

answers.content: Well, back in high school, in order to grasp the concept of gravity, I went to the roof of a tall building and dropped a few objects of different weights and volumes to experiment which ones would hit the ground first. I think it was quite an interesting experiment and I really enjoyed studying the science behind it.

sub_questions.content: Which science subject interests you?

answers.content: Among all the science books I’ve read, I’m most interested in astronomy. I often wonder if there are any other universes out there. You see, I believe in the parallel universe theory. In fact, when I was at school I asked my father for a professional telescope so I could look into the starry night sky and let my thoughts go wild.

sub_questions.content: When did you start to learn about science？

answers.content: Well, my father guided me into science learning when I was about six years old. He bought me a set of Lego, and explained many physical formulas to me. We built different shapes together. It was so much fun. I enjoyed the early science education that my father gave to me.

I started to learn about science since I was 10. At that time, I was in grade 4 in primary school and I took a class called “science”. In that course, we did a lot of physics experiments including paper planes, mini-rocket and so on. I did not know that was physics knowledge but I felt that it was pretty interesting and willing to learn more in the future.

Well, my experience with science began as a child because my father and mother both had scientific interests. When I was a kid, we had lots of books about astronomy, physics and nature, so my interest in science was established by reading those books and hearing my parents discuss it all the time.

sub_questions.content: Do you like science?

answers.content: Absolutely. I think science is so helpful. It has provided solutions to most tricky problems that the human society faces, such as environment pollution, poverty, epidemic and etc. Also, science calls for innovation, which is something I’m especially interested in.

Of course, I do, when I was a little kid, my parents took me to many stunning science museums and galleries, which really broadened my horizons. Also, science provided me a deeper understanding of the earth.

Yes, I do, if I remember correctly, I fell in love with it in an early age. I always have a lively curiosity(强烈的好奇心) about things, and thanks to science books, I learned a lot about the natural phenomena and the laws of science.

sub_questions.content: Did you enjoy singing when you were younger?

sub_questions.content: Will you take singing lessons in the future?

However, I reckon that if I have more free time in the future, I would probably give it a go.

sub_questions.content: When do you sing?

Apart from that, there are some special occasions when I really like singing, like at friends' birthday parties, Christmas and also New Year.

sub_questions.content: Do you like singing? Why or why not?

answers.content: Yeah, I like singing. I always can’t help singing when I take a shower. Well, I mean singing is indeed a suitable way to express one’s feelings. If someone broke up with his partner, he will sing sad love songs all night. And some excellent singers can touch the bottom of my heart by singing. But to be honest, I’m not so good at singing. I can’t find the pitch precisely. So, I really seldom sing in public.

Absolutely! Although I’m not a professional singer and not very talented in that respect, I’m still very into singing, especially when I’m taking a shower. The echo of my voice in the shower cubicle always sounds better than elsewhere, so I often hum to myself in the bathroom with my Bluetooth speaker on.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever learnt how to sing?

answers.content: No, I never learned to sing. Cause singing is just entertainment to me. I only sing when I go to karaoke with my friends or in the bathroom. So, there is indeed no need for me to learn how to sing. Anyway, I’m not a professional singer and I never want to be. Actually, there was a chance for me to learn singing in the music class in my high school. But I chose to learn how to play the ukulele.

No, not really. Throughout my entire school years, I never really had a real music lesson. The school I went to focused only on the academic side. At school, singing wasn't really a priority, so I never had the chance to learn it seriously. But I used to I used to teach myself some singing skills through videos on the Internet.

sub_questions.content: Who do you want to sing for?

answers.content: Well, I’d like to sing for my girl. You know, that’s quite romantic to some extent. I’ll sing a sweet and slow love song for her. There are a bunch of amazing love songs. Maybe I’ll choose Justin’s song. And I prefer to sing for her on a special day, like her birthday or Valentine’s Day.

Ah, that’s a tricky question for me. Normally I only sing to myself, cause I’m such a terrible singer. But perhaps I can sing for my parents on their birthdays. They certainly wouldn’t laugh at my bad singing, but they are the only people I would sing for, cause I love them so much.

sub_questions.content: Do you think singing can bring happiness to people?

answers.content: Well, it depends. It depends on the singer’s singing level. If your friend is a poor singer who persists to sing all the time, that will be a disaster. But generally speaking, I believe that singing is a fabulous art, which can convey feelings and emotions to listeners. If it is a happy song, it will definitely bring happiness to people. But if it is a sad song, it may trigger off people’s unpleasant memories.

Perhaps. I have a lot of friends who enjoy singing! They pay to go to KTVs to sing. They throw themselves into music and seem to be completely involved in their singing. So I think singing must bring them pleasure and comfort in some ways. Yeah, so they must find some sort of happiness in singing.

questions.content: Do you like modern art or traditional art?
Do you want to be an artist?
Do you like visiting art galleries?
Do you like art?

sub_questions.content: Do you like modern art or traditional art?

answers.content: Well, if I have to say, I’m more into traditional art, especially Chinese traditional art. I suppose that’s because, in most Chinese traditional paintings, I can find a sense of harmony and peace. That gives me a sense of comfort and security. That’s also something that modern art can’t offer.

Compared with traditional art, I prefer modern art. Of course, this does not mean that traditional art is unappealing. Traditional art gives people the enjoyment of eternal beauty, and there is a reason why these beautiful treasures have been handed down through the years. But modern art has a stronger visual impact, which makes more of an impression on me.

answers.content: Well, in China, I would say R&B, for rhythm and blues. You know, quite a number of young people leave their hometowns and live in big cities alone, therefore, they need such peaceful music so that they can resonate with its melancholy lyrics. sub_questions.content: Which singer or musician would you like to see in person?

answers.content: I will definitely choose Avril Lavigne. Her songs Girlfriend and Complicated were the first two songs I started to learn back to junior high, so I would say she is the reflection of my adolescence. And I like her not only because of the lyric of her word, but also for the attitude she wanted to deliver through her songs, in which girls can be as independent and strong as boys.

Honestly, I would really like to meet Ed Sheeran in person because I think the lyrics in the music he produces are really inspiring and heart-touching. Also, I am a guitar player and I really want to talk to him about playing musical instruments and composing songs. I believe that by talking to him, I would be able to appreciate his music even more.

To be honest, I don’t share her passion for rock music, because I think it’s noisy and insane.

To be honest, I reckon that my friends like a variety of music. I see that sometimes they listen to rock and roll, sometimes it’s just jazz and occasionally they will listen to country music. For me, I actually enjoy a very different type of music, I really like blues because it really makes me feel relaxed and calm.

sub_questions.content: Do you often listen to one type of music?

answers.content: Well, I think I do, because people sometimes are in a particular mood. When I feel down, I wanna listen to some jazz music, or maybe blues . When I feel joyful, kpop music becomes my first choice I could replay it over and over again.

No, I like to explore different types of music, because I'm open to more possibilities. In my spare time, I tend to listen to classical music to help myself calm down. However, when I want some excitement, I would turn to pop music or even rock music to let off steam. Also,, to have more topics to talk about with my friends, I also listen to the newest popular music.

sub_questions.content: What music do you like?

answers.content: I enjoy country music a lot. The biggest reason for my preference is that my childhood idol is Taylor Swift. She was a typical singer of country music, and Love Story was a great work done by her. The relaxed and tender rhythm of it really impresses me and will always do.

Among all kinds of music, I enjoy classical music the most. I believe classical music has a calming effect and it’s absolutely timeless.
