sub_questions.content: Where do you like to go to take photos?

answers.content: I personally enjoy capturing the charm of urban architecture. The blend of old and new buildings in cities fascinates me. Exploring the bustling streets, I find myself drawn to the intricate details of historical structures as well as the sleek lines of modern skyscrapers. Additionally, I love visiting museums and art galleries, as they offer unique subjects that tell stories through paintings, sculptures, and artifacts. Lastly, libraries provide a tranquil setting, allowing me to delve into my photography with a focus on the power of knowledge and the serenity it brings.

I absolutely love capturing moments in nature, so one of my favorite places to go and take photos would be the serene and picturesque countryside. The lush green fields, vibrant flowers, and peaceful atmosphere there never fail to inspire me. Whether it's capturing the sunrise casting a golden glow over the landscapes or capturing the beauty of wildlife, the countryside offers endless possibilities for breathtaking shots. It's like stepping into a different world filled with hidden gems just waiting to be discovered through my lens.

sub_questions.content: What is your favourite family photo?

answers.content: One of my favorite family photos is from New Year's Eve. It captures the joyous moment when we gather together, smiling and holding our glasses of champagne, ready to toast to the new year ahead. The photo symbolizes unity and celebration, reminding me of the importance of family bonds and the excitement of new beginnings.

My favorite family photo is the one taken during our annual beach vacation. It captures a beautiful sunset backdrop with all of us in our casual summer attire, enjoying the sand and waves together. It brings back cherished memories of laughter, bonding, and relaxation. This photo holds a special place in my heart as it represents the joy and happiness we experience as a family.

sub_questions.content: Do you like taking selfies?

answers.content: I like taking selfies. There are three reasons why I like to do so. First, I think it is very important for everyone to know how to appreciate themselves. And taking selfies is a good way to appreciate our appearance. I can gain confidence and courage from those selfies I have taken. Second, taking selfies is a means to record my journey of growth. Nowadays, with the progress of technology, it is easy for us to take selfies anytime anywhere. Wherever I go, especially at some significant occasions in my life, I usually take many selfies and these photos will become witness to my growth in the coming days. Third, taking seflies may give me the chance to become an Internet celebrity. Every time I take a selfie, I usually post it on my different social media accounts. I know I might not be the most beautiful person, but I am unique in this world. And if more and more people notice my selfies and they like those photos, they will follow my accounts. And I think the feeling of being an Internet celebrity is quite cool.

To be honest, I don’t really like taking selfies. I can even count on one hand the number of selfies I've taken. It's not just because I think it's kind of ridiculous, but it's also that I really don't like how I look in pictures. Sometimes, if someone points a camera in my direction, I usually hide my face. I don't actually think I'm unattractive, but when I see myself in pictures, I can be horrified, you know? My eyes go beady, my mouth narrows, my nose expands, and those cheekbones I thought I had have gone. I start to wonder if the photos are actually more accurate. Also, when I meet a lot of people, I feel particularly camera-shy.

sub_questions.content: What is your favorite family photo?

answers.content: There are many photos of me as a child, and in all of them, my favorite one is the photo in which I am wearing nothing more than basketball shorts and a tank top. It’s quite funny since I kind of looked like a boy when I was young. In the photo, I stood there grinning while wearing my usual attire. I quite obviously fit into the category of a stereotypical young boy then. That’s so fun. This photo reminds me that I loved being outside when I was little, so basketball shorts were the perfect outfit, especially on hot summer days. Each time when I see the photo, I still remember when I used to wear and to do, and that’s really precious.

The family photo that I really like is one which was taken almost twenty years ago. It was framed and enlarged by my father back then, and it is still hanging on the wall of our drawing room. The photo was taken on my parents' 6th wedding anniversary, and at that time I was only three years old. In the picture, my dad, mom and I were standing there, while my grandparents were sitting in front of us, and the picture captured the memory of a happy family. When this picture was taken, I was very little, and I don’t have any recollection of it, but every time I see it, I feel a real sense of joy.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to improve your picture taking skills?

answers.content: Definitely yes! I am very passionate about taking great pictures of people and scenery. However, when I travel with my friends, they usually do not encourage me to take the responsibility as a photographer. I’m not sure why but I think it relates to my picture taking skills. Therefore, I need to work on this more.

Who wouldn’t? To reiterate, I’m a big fan of photography, but I have to admit that I’m only a layman. Whenever I see breathtaking photos captured by other photographers, I feel this urge inside of me to improve my mediocre photography skills. I hope in the future I can invest more time and money into learning the art.

questions.content: What would you do if you had that opportunity?
Do you want to travel in the outer space?
Do you think it's necessary to see other planets?
Do you like to travel by air?
When was the last time you went travelling?

sub_questions.content: What would you do if you had that opportunity?

answers.content: You mean if I had the opportunity of traveling into the galaxy? Well, I would want to make good use of it. Personally，I am a huge sci-fiction fan! And I have a strong sense of curiosity. I do wish to experience the new space. So if I could, I would definitely go out there to satisfy my curiosity.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to travel in the outer space?

answers.content: If I had a chance, yeah, definitely! I have watched some BBC documentaries about the galaxy. They all look amazing! Well, although I imagine it’s just darkness in the outer space. Still, I would love to go out there and see it for myself if I ever had the opportunity.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it's necessary to see other planets?

answers.content: Well, that’s a rather philosophical question. To as far as human civilization goes, I think we shoud travel to other planets and explore them. But having said that, humans are maybe destinied to be born on the earth. So what’s the point of seeing other planets since we won’t survive in there?

sub_questions.content: Do you like to travel by air?

answers.content: I don’t mind it. Actually I kinda like it when the plane takes off. Although it is so thrilling that makes my heart tremble. Anyway, I feel excited to see the overall landscape of the city while I’m rising into the sky. It’s just amazing, really.

sub_questions.content: When was the last time you went travelling?

answers.content: That was last summer when I went to Australia to see the Great Ocean Road. The view there was breathtaking and the sea was so crystal blue. I have never seen a place so amazing! Yeah, that was a great trip. I totally enjoyed it.

questions.content: How will dictionaries change in the future?
What kinds of dictionaries have you used before?
Do you like paper dictionary or electronic dictionary?
How often do you use your dictionary?

sub_questions.content: How will dictionaries change in the future?

answers.content: I reckon all dictionaries might be stored on the internet probably in the next a few decades. cause the online dictionaries are cheaper, or even free, easy to access and could provide a few options at a time so you can compare the meanings and examples in different dictionaries.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of dictionaries have you used before?

answers.content: I used the Chinese dictionary when I was in primary school. I needed it to check for Chinese slangs and the complicated Chinese characters. But then as soon as I started to learn English, I also had to buy the Oxford English dictionary as well and it has become a necessity in my study.

sub_questions.content: Do you like paper dictionary or electronic dictionary?

answers.content: I do prefer paper dictionary myself. I like it when my fingers cross the dictionary pages, that kinda makes me feel like I’m doing something important. For some reason, with the electronic dictionary, I get distracted. But unfortunately, it’s not easy to carry a thick brick all the time. So I only use it at home.

sub_questions.content: How often do you use your dictionary?

answers.content: Whenever I read an academic English article, I’m afraid I have to keep the dictionary with me, either a paper dictionary or online dictionary. There never seem to be enough vocabulary in my head to make sense of those academic English essays or news and etc. So I need to keep referring to the dictionary when I read. Pretty exhausting~.

questions.content: How often do you take the bus
When was the first time you took a bus?
Is it convenient to take the bus in your city
Do people in your country take the bus?

sub_questions.content: How often do you take the bus

answers.content: I rarely take the bus actually, since in Shenzhen, subway is more convenient compared with bus, the tube will always arrive on time, but sometimes I have to wait for a bus for ages, which would make me late for work/class. That is why I prefer subway.

sub_questions.content: When was the first time you took a bus?

answers.content: Frankly speaking, I can’t recall the first time that I took a bus, probably when I was only an infant, but I remember that when I was a little boy/girl, bus was an essential means of transportation in my hometown, because we did not have subway back then and the bus ticket was really cheap, like 1 RMB for adult and 0. 5 RMB for kids and students.

sub_questions.content: Is it convenient to take the bus in your city

answers.content: As I mentioned before, taking the bus can be inconvenient due to the lack of punctuality, in this way, it is not an ideal choice for commuting; but bus stops are everywhere in Shenzhen, so you can even take a bus to the suburb where there is no subway station.

sub_questions.content: Do people in your country take the bus?

answers.content: Yes, bus is still very popular among Chinese people, most of employees take the bus for commuting during working days. Since the price of bus ticket is pretty low, compared with taxi and even subway, also you can also get a monthly ticket for the bus, which is more money-saving and efficient.

topics.title: Cooperation（Spending time with others）（疑似淘汰）

questions.content: Do you remember a time when you need to cooperate with others？
Would you prefer to study alone or with others?
How do you like spending time with your friends?
Do you like talking with people?
Do you remember a time when you needed to cooperate with others？
Do you prefer to study alone or with others?

source_payload.title: Cooperation（Spending time with others）（疑似淘汰）

sub_questions.content: Do you remember a time when you need to cooperate with others？

answers.content: Yep! Last summer, I signed up for an English training course where the teacher asked us to work in groups to perform the English dialogues. It was challenging ‘cause we really needed to always keep pace with one another. But it was lots of fun as well! We laughed so much and achieved a lot by the end. I totally enjoyed it.

sub_questions.content: Would you prefer to study alone or with others?

answers.content: Honestly, I like to study in the library where there are many other people studying, ‘cause when everyone around me is focused, well, at least, when they look focused, I can manage to concentrate as well. So, studying with others can be more effective.

sub_questions.content: How do you like spending time with your friends?

answers.content: Well, my friends and I have several ways of entertaining. Among them, I especially favor playing table games with my friends. We play many games like Monopoly and Werewolf in which we can spend a whole afternoon. Plus, we can grow a stronger friendship tie through the process of playing. Besides, shopping and watching movies with my friends are both great choices for us to relax and have fun.

As we get older, and life gets faster, the time we once had for our friends seems to diminish. Spending more time with our friends is beneficial to our mental well-being, as well as our relationships. With a friend, our happiness is doubled, and our sorrows are shared. So, we shouldn’t overlook this important part of life.

sub_questions.content: Do you like talking with people?

answers.content: Of course I do. I always find talking to people very helpful. Firstly, expressing myself is a good way to vent my negative feelings or share happiness with others. Moreover, having conversations with my friends or someone I just met triggers exchange of ideas and thoughts. It’s also an opportunity for me to learn more about the life I don’t know.

Yes, I like talking to other people, because it relieves stress and helps build friendships and connections. It also exposes you to new perspectives and ideas and helps with problem-solving. In contrast, social isolation and loneliness can lead to depression and poorer health conditions. So, it's important to find people you can share things with.

sub_questions.content: Do you remember a time when you needed to cooperate with others？

answers.content: I do. Last summer vacation I had a part-time job working in a café. Since the café was quite small and only provided take away drinks, there was only me and another barista on shift. I worked as the cashier and made basic drinks while she did the coffee-making, like espressos and hand drips. Our tacit cooperation ensured the quality and efficiency of the drinks we sold.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to study alone or with others?

answers.content: I like to study alone because it ensures that my study pace and schedule will not be disturbed. I've tried studying with friends before, but it turned out to be not very effective since we couldn't stop talking and chatting to each other. Since quiet environments help me concentrate better, I often go to cafés or libraries to study.

questions.content: Could you read a map if you traveled to a new place?
Do you feel nervous when you travel to new places？

sub_questions.content: Could you read a map if you traveled to a new place?

answers.content: Well, if you are talking about e-maps like Google Maps, I will say yes, because they’re very easy to operate. You simply input your destination and follow the directions that they provide. However, if I only had a paper map in hand, I would rather turn to a pedestrian for help.

sub_questions.content: Do you feel nervous when you travel to new places？

answers.content: For me, the novelty and excitement of traveling to a destination I've never been to are stronger than the anxiety it creates. What’s more, I always expect the uncertainty of the journey to offer a new experience. On the other hand, I think thoroughly preparing in advance and checking all the necessary documents can help ease my anxiety and calm my nerves.

questions.content: Can you read a map when you get lost？
How can you find your way when you are lost？
Have you ever lost your way？
Have you ever helped someone who got lost？
Could you read a map if you got lost？
How do you find your way when you are lost？
Have you ever been lost？（Have you ever lost your way）

sub_questions.content: Can you read a map when you get lost？

answers.content: Certainly. Since I was a child, my parents told me how important reading a map was when getting lost somewhere. Actually, I can tell you some helpful steps to read a map. Firstly, you should choose the right type of map and check its orientations. Secondly, make a note of the longitude and latitude, paying attention to its scale. Finally create checkpoints to keep from losing your way. See, it’s not an easy thing, right?

sub_questions.content: How can you find your way when you are lost？

answers.content: I think there are loads of ways to find my way. When I go somewhere new and get lost, the most effective way to find directions I suppose, should be using mapping apps. Besides, I can also use car-hailing applications like Uber or DiDiChuXing to go back the way I came. Even if the phone battery is died or fails to connect to the Internet, I can still ask people for directions.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever lost your way？

answers.content: Of course. I still remember ten years ago when I was a middle school student, I went to Beijing for travelling with my parents. One day when we arrived at a famous tourist spot, my mom went to the toilet and she told me to wait for her outside. However, I was attracted by something and wandered away, and that’s how I lost my way. Fortunately, I met some nice tourists and ultimately, they helped me find my mom.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever helped someone who got lost？

answers.content: Yes, I have. Last year, I met a freshman on campus and it seemed that she lost her way. She told me that it was the first time she came to the university and didn’t know how to go to the study hall. Then I showed her the way, but she still had no idea how to go there, so I just took her to the building.

I have. Once in a hotel I noticed a man quite anxiously talking to the receptionist. I heard something like“Betty was here just a second ago. In a yellow dress. Please help!” I had just checked out and was standing near the entrance when I caught sight of the little girl crying outside helplessly, so I went over and led her back to her father.

sub_questions.content: Could you read a map if you got lost？

answers.content: If it was a paper map, I‘m afraid not. Without technology, finding my bearings would be a real challenge due to my awful sense of direction, let alone reading a paper map, which I’ve never done before. But with navigational apps on our smartphones, I wouldn’t worry at all, as I would just follow the highlighted path and eventually find my way.

sub_questions.content: How do you find your way when you are lost？

answers.content: Well, what I used to do was asking a nearby security guard or a passer-by, but, as you know, nowadays everybody has a map app on their smartphone for finding directions, including myself. With the help of GPS, which provides accurate directions, I’m able to find my way, although it has lead me down a dead end a few times!

sub_questions.content: Have you ever been lost？（Have you ever lost your way）

answers.content: Well, it may sound a bit ridiculous, but I always used to get lost when I was younger, especially when I was in kindergarten. I used to get lost so easily that my mom never let me out of her sight. It's fair to say, without her efforts, I would have got myself into all sorts of problems.

questions.content: Do you wear the same style of cloth on weekday and weekend?
What kind of clothes do you never wear?
What kind of clothes do you usually wear?
What’s your favourite color of clothes?

sub_questions.content: Do you wear the same style of cloth on weekday and weekend?

answers.content: Not really. For workdays/schooldays, some casual outfit would be my choices since I have to go out to meet people I cannot show up in the street in pajamas. But for Saturdays and Sundays, when I don't have to leave my apartment, I would just wear some loungewear or stay in my pajamas for the whole weekend. That would give me a more relaxed and cozy feeling.

sub_questions.content: What kind of clothes do you never wear?

answers.content: Probably those very fancy evening gowns/ tuxes, which are well-designed and extremely expensive. I saw a lot of movie stars and celebrities wearing that type of clothes in some movie festivals or ceremonies, but personally I have never had such opportunity to put on that. I guess the only time I may probably wear that would be on my future wedding.

sub_questions.content: What kind of clothes do you usually wear?

answers.content: Personally, in most cases I would wear something quite casual like T-shirts, hoodies, sports shirts and leggings. Since I am still a student, for most of the occasions I don’t have to dress up or put on some really formal suits. Wearing comfortably is what I pursue, so I would normally go for some casual clothing with good quality fabric.

sub_questions.content: What’s your favourite color of clothes?

answers.content: Well, I guess it largely depends on the season. For summertime or some warm days, I think white or some light colors like yellow and mint would be ideal since those colors give me a sense of refreshment. However, in cold winter days I prefer some dark shades of colors like emerald or navy, but honestly, I don't know why.

questions.content: Do you do the same thing every weekend?
Has your weekend routine changed？
Do you like weekends？
What do you usually do on weekends？Do you study or work？
What did you do last weekend？
Do you make plans for your weekend？
What do other people in your hometown usually do on weekends?
What will you do next weekend?
What do you usually do on weekends?

sub_questions.content: Do you do the same thing every weekend?

answers.content: Not at all! My weekends are always filled with new and exciting activities. I believe in making the most of my free time. Some weekends, I go hiking in the mountains to connect with nature and admire the breathtaking views. Other times, I indulge in my passion for photography and explore unique locations to capture stunning images. Occasionally, I enjoy trying out different cuisines at local restaurants and discovering hidden gems in the city. So, you could say that routine is definitely not a part of my weekend agenda!

No, I don't do the same thing every weekend. I believe in keeping my weekends diverse and adventurous. Sometimes I explore new hiking trails in the countryside with friends, while other times I relax at home, reading books or watching movies. On certain weekends, I indulge in my passion for cooking by experimenting with new recipes. Overall, I try to make the most of my weekends by engaging in different activities and creating memorable experiences.

sub_questions.content: Has your weekend routine changed？

answers.content: Yes, my weekend routine has changed significantly. Previously, I used to spend my weekends lounging around at home, watching movies, and catching up on sleep. However, ever since I started pursuing my passion for photography, my weekends have taken on a new dynamic. Now, I often spend my Saturdays exploring different locations, capturing breathtaking images of nature and street life. On Sundays, I attend photography workshops and engage with other enthusiasts to learn new techniques. This change in routine has not only brought me joy but has also improved my photography skills.

Yes, my weekend routine has changed recently. With the final exams approaching, I find myself spending more time revising and preparing for them. Typically, I allocate a few hours each day to study and review important concepts. This includes going through textbooks, practicing past exam papers, and seeking clarification from my professors when needed. The increased workload during this period has resulted in a shift in my weekend activities, with less time available for leisure activities and socializing. However, I understand the importance of dedicating sufficient time to my studies in order to perform well in the exams.

sub_questions.content: Do you like weekends？

answers.content: Well, I do like weekends. It's a great time to relax and recharge after a long week. However, I must admit that sometimes I have to work overtime on weekends, so I don't always get to fully enjoy them. But whenever I do have a free weekend, I love spending time with my family, going out with friends, or simply indulging in some personal hobbies. It's a much-needed break from the daily grind and allows me to unwind and rejuvenate.

Yes, I do like weekends. They provide a much-needed break from the hustle and bustle of the workweek. Weekends give me the opportunity to relax and recharge my batteries. I enjoy spending time with friends and family, indulging in hobbies like reading and painting, and exploring new places. Weekends allow me to unwind and rejuvenate, making me more productive and motivated when the new week begins. Overall, weekends are an essential part of maintaining a healthy work-life balance.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do on weekends？Do you  study or work？

answers.content: On weekends, I enjoy a mix of leisure activities. When I'm not studying or working, I like to go shopping, explore the malls, and find unique items. It's a great way for me to relax and rejuvenate. Besides, I also value spending quality time with my family, which often includes going out for a nice meal together. These activities bring joy and help me strike a balance between my personal and professional life.

On weekends, I usually like to balance my time between studying and relaxation. The majority of my mornings are dedicated to studying to make progress towards my academic goals. In the afternoons, I enjoy engaging in recreational activities such as reading books, going for a walk in nature, or spending time with friends and family. This helps me recharge and maintain a healthy work-life balance, ensuring that I am refreshed and motivated for the upcoming week.

sub_questions.content: What did you do last weekend？

answers.content: Last weekend, I visited the Forbidden city with my roommate. Recently, there is a special exhibition in the Forbidden city where exhibits of Dunhuang are replicated. The once-in-seventy-years-special exhibition is quite popular online and we booked the exhibition ticket in advance. There, we got to know the history of Mogaoku, one of the most notable grottoes in China. We learned a lot about the history of western China and I think the replicated grottoes are fabulous and you may go and visit it.

To be honest, I spent my whole weekend preparing for this test. I had not given myself enough time to go through the model tests before, and I want to get a good result, so I stayed at home and studied all day. I guess that is a bit different from what I usually do because normally my weekends are for relaxing.

sub_questions.content: Do you make plans for your weekend？

answers.content: Yes, I do make plans for my weekends. Normally, I enjoy spending time outdoors, so I like to go hiking or have a picnic in the park. Sometimes, I also like to visit museums or attend cultural events in the city. However, if I don't have any specific plans, I enjoy relaxing at home, reading books or watching movies. It's nice to have a balance between having activities and having some downtime.

Yes, I always like to plan my weekends ahead of time. I usually organize gatherings with my friends for brunch or explore new cafes. Besides social activities, I allocate time for self-care routines like yoga classes or painting sessions. Planning enables me to make the most of my weekends and strike a balance between relaxation and productivity.

sub_questions.content: What do other people in your hometown usually do on weekends?

answers.content: People in my hometown often hang out with their friends or relatives on weekends. There is a small hill on the outskirt of my hometown where people can ride bikes, have a picnic or barbeque. After a week-long study or work, people can enjoy a tranquil weekend afternoon on that small hill while chitchatting with their peers. I really enjoy those happy moments back in my hometown.

According to what I have observed, in my hometown, people are more likely to hang out with friends on Saturdays and Sundays. Some of them may go to shopping malls, while others might just relax in a park or a garden. There are also some people who just stay at home but I think most of us choose to go out somewhere.

sub_questions.content: What will you do next weekend?

answers.content: My best friend will come to my city next weekend and we plan to drive to a small town called Gubeishuizhen on the vicinity of Beijing and stay there for two days. Gubeishuizhen is a famous tourism attraction site for people to get closer to nature. As office workers, most of our leisure time is spent in cinemas or shopping malls. So, we decided to escape from the hustle and bustle of metropolis and spend the next weekend there for some tranquility.

Well, as I will have finished my IELTS test next weekend, I guess I am going to treat myself to a good meal in a restaurant that I have been longing to go to for ages. Also, I might invite several friends so that we can order more food and share it. After that, I’ll probably just watch a movie and have a really good sleep.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do on weekends?

answers.content: Usually, I will hang out with my friends on weekends. My friends and I live in different parts of the city, so it is a little bit difficult for us to meet each other on work day evenings. On weekends, we will do a delicate makeup and take our cameras with us and visit some popular pubs or exhibition halls, where we can enjoy some tasty food and learn more about arts and history from the exhibition.

Since I have to study/work on weekdays, on weekends I will just do some recreational activities. For example, hanging out with a bunch of friends or just staying at home all day watching some shows. Also, cooking for myself is one of the things that I look forward to on Saturdays, because I love treating myself in that way.

questions.content: Is it difficult for you to stay focused on something?
What do you do to help you concentrate?
What can distract you when you’re trying to stay focused?
When do you need to be focused?

sub_questions.content: Is it difficult for you to stay focused on something?

answers.content: Well, I guess there are some occasions when I find it particularly hard to concentrate. The first one is when what I am doing is very tedious. For instance, long and boring lectures really make me feel tired. Also, when a task is too simple, it is a bit challenging for me to stay completely focused because I know I can do it without really trying.

sub_questions.content: What do you do to help you concentrate?

answers.content: Well, as I mentioned before, putting my phone on silent does not really work because I will still check it regardless. So, I came up with an alternative: I just leave my phone outside the room, such as leaving it on the dining table while I am in the study. This method definitely helps me focus on my work better.

sub_questions.content: What can distract you when you’re trying to stay focused?

answers.content: Probably the biggest distraction I have is the notifications popping out on my phone. Sometimes when I just get down to my business, a message may just come out and catch my attention. Although I tried to put my phone on mute while I needed concentration, I would still check my phone every now and then to make sure I didn’t miss any important messages.

sub_questions.content: When do you need to be focused?

answers.content: I guess there are two scenarios where I really need to concentrate. First is when I am studying/working. I have to pay a lot of attention to the task at hand otherwise it will not turn out well or take extra time. The other situation is when I am engaged in video games like Super Mario. If I get absent-minded for just one second, it is highly likely that I will lose the game.

questions.content: Do you enjoy living in the countryside?
What do you usually do in the countryside?
Have you ever lived in the countryside?
Will you live in the countryside in the future?

sub_questions.content: Do you enjoy living in the countryside?

answers.content: Yes, very much. My favourite thing about living in the countryside is that there’s a strong sense of community spirit. Generations upon generations of the same families have lived there and created great relationships with their neighbours. In cities, people don’t know each other, but in the suburbs, it’s a totally different story.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do in the countryside?

answers.content: Well, there are a bunch of things I can do there. Normally, I just go fishing with my dad or my friends. I like this activity - not that I’m very good at it. It just gives me an opportunity to appreciate the nature, the mountains surrounding us and the ripples on the lake.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever lived in the countryside?

answers.content: Yes. I spent my childhood in the countryside with my grandparents. It’s a wonderful place, with the trees, lakes and farms. I could enjoy the sunshine and fresh air without being interrupted by others, as it was less crowded than the cities.

sub_questions.content: Will you live in the countryside in the future?

answers.content: Yes, I think so, because I don’t really like the city lifestyle. Sometimes it’s not so easy to live in the city, as the fast pace of today’s society forces people to live in a less holistic way, without a strong connection with nature.

questions.content: Have you ever had a pet when you are young?
What's your favorite animals？Why?
Do you like to see animals in the zoo？
Are there many people keeping pets in China?
What pet will you keep if you want?
Where do you prefer to keep your pet, indoors or outdoors？
What is the most popular animal in China？
What's your favorite animal？Why?
What pet would you like to have?
Did you have a pet when you were younger?
Do many people keep pets in China?

sub_questions.content: Have you ever had a pet when you are young?

answers.content: Yes, I did have a pet when I was a child but it was a small goldfish with a very unique color. This tiny creature had two giant bubbles around its eyes and I loved it very much. But its life only lasted for several months and when it died, I felt extremely terrible. Since then, I have never had other pets because I am afraid of facing their death.

sub_questions.content: What's your favorite animals？Why?

answers.content: Tiger is my favorite animal always and forever. Tiger is not only my favorite animal but also my inspiration too. It is a solitary animal and does not stay in a group except in captivity. I like Tiger because it maintains the ecological balance in its habitat and also protects the forests from overgrazing.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to see animals in the zoo？

answers.content: I don't like to see animals in the zoo because zoo is an extremely uncomfortable place for all animals especially wild animals like lions and tigers who roam freely in a space that is many times larger than the cages they are kept in. As far as I'm concerned, zoos are just one of the ways to torture animals. They are held captive just for human entertainment and thus, are deprived of their freedom.

sub_questions.content: Are there many people keeping pets in China?

answers.content: Given the high degree of urbanization and people's rising incomes, pet raising has become so popular in China. It's not as difficult as before when keeping pets were mostly for the bourgeois. Now, in cities across China, you'll see people walking their dogs in the park. I guess Chinese people have really taken it to heart the concept of keeping pets as human's beloved companion.

sub_questions.content: What pet will you keep if you want?

answers.content: I will keep a dog if I have a chance. The presence of a dog, small or big, makes me chuckle at my gloomiest. They show their affection in many different ways: making your hand their favorite chew toy, scratching every corner of your house - leaving no piece of furniture or clothing unscathed. But that is all in good fun. Their joy is infectious and they are always excited for playtime!

sub_questions.content: Where do you prefer to keep your pet, indoors or outdoors？

answers.content: I'd like to keep my dog outdoors for most of the time because in that way, my dog gets more exercise and gets happier. Dogs are incredibly active creatures who need a lot of physical exercise and movement to burn their energy to remain healthy both mentally and physically. Insufficient physical activity is detrimental to their health. But while my dog is playing outside, I'll put a GPS collar on to keep it from being stolen.

It depends on the weather. If the weather is pleasant, I usually let my cat out to play in the yard. But when it gets extremely hot or cold, I like to keep it inside, just for its safety, you know. The cat itself prefers to stay outside though.

sub_questions.content: What is the most popular animal in China？

answers.content: The most popular animal in China is the Giant pandas. I think "the looks of a teddy bear" are what makes pandas so popular. Pandas appeal to our "cuteness recipient" because they have large, front-facing eyes, and are extremely furry, so we therefore think of them as cuddly and cute.

It’s probably furry dogs, you know, such as huskies and Tibetan mastiffs. There are more and more single people in China nowadays and they love to keep dogs as companions. If you go out in the morning or evening, it’s almost impossible not to run into someone walking a small furry dog on the street.
 .

sub_questions.content: What's your favorite animal？Why?

answers.content: Well, cats are definitely my favorite. I think they bring out the kind of inter-species relationship that I am comfortable with which also reflects how I treat people. I am not the sort of person who wants to order people around or manage them. I much prefer to mind my own business, so I really enjoy being around cats.

sub_questions.content: What pet would you like to have?

answers.content: As I have mentioned, I don't actually want pets anymore. All these small creatures are so delicate that I don’t think I can take good care of them. But if I had to make a choice, I think a turtle might be a nice option, since it can live very long and I wouldn’t have to experience sadness any time soon.

sub_questions.content: Did you have a pet when you were younger?

answers.content: Yes! I had a small goldfish when I was a child with a very unique color. This tiny creature had two giant bubbles around its eyes and I loved it very much. But its life only lasted for a few months and when it died, I felt awful. Since then, I have never had any other pets because I am afraid of facing their death.

sub_questions.content: Do many people keep pets in China?

answers.content: I am not sure about the exact percentage of pet owners, but I have to say that pets are really common in my country. Some of my neighbors have puppies while others have cats, you know, what you might call normal pets. However, I know that more and more people nowadays are becoming interested in other types of pets like snakes and piglets.

questions.content: Will you spend a lot of money on shoes?
Do you like buying shoes? How often?
Which do you prefer, fashionable shoes or comfortable shoes?
Which do you prefer, expensive shoes or comfortable shoes?
How much money do you usually spend on shoes?
Do you spend a lot of money on shoes?
How often do you buy shoes?
Have you ever bought shoes online?
Do you know anyone who likes to buy a lot of shoes?
What are your favorite type of shoes?

sub_questions.content: Will you spend a lot of money on shoes?

answers.content: It depends on how you define a lot of money. I think it’s ok for me to spend one thousand or so for a pair of shoes. I will think twice if the price exceeds one thousand RMB. Once I saw a pair of leather boots that’s really just my type. It was about five thousand RMB? I persuaded myself finally and bought one because I think I can wear it for at least several years. So, as long as I think it's worth it, I will buy shoes at a high price.

sub_questions.content: Do you like buying shoes? How often?

answers.content: I like buying shoes of course. I will never get tired of new shoes. Generally, I will buy new shoes at the turn of the season because I need new sandals or boots for the coming summer or winter. Also, it is a period for shoe brands to launch their latest products. In addition, I may also buy new shoes before Christmas since everything gets cheaper by then.

sub_questions.content: Which do you prefer, fashionable shoes or comfortable shoes?

answers.content: Under most circumstances, I prefer comfortable shoes. It will be a disaster for me to wear some fashionable high heels to hang out with friends. I don’t want to have such a torture for my feet. However, when I need to go see a client or meet on some formal occasions, it’s really necessary to have a pair of fashionable shoes that go with my fancy clothes.

I think health is more important than appearance. I mean, I like outdoor activities and I often go hiking, mountain climbing and fast-walking. If I wear shoes that aren’t suitable for the activity, I will suffer and, even worse, I may hurt my feet. It would be very unwise to go for fashionable shoes rather than comfortable ones.

sub_questions.content: Which do you prefer, expensive shoes or comfortable shoes?

answers.content: I prefer comfortable shoes, but I think many expensive shoes are also comfortable. I think PRICE is not the most important thing for shoes. As long as it wears comfortable and looks great, I will buy it. I don’t care much about the branding and some expensive shoes are expensive simply because of their brands. Then, I will not pay for that.

Well, definitely comfortable shoes. Honestly, why would anyone pick expensive shoes over comfortable ones? Unless it‘s for some commercial event, I guess. I‘m not super rich, so I really wouldn't go for the expensive ones. I think it‘s just a waste of money.

sub_questions.content: How much money do you usually spend on shoes?

answers.content: Usually, I spend about 1000 yuan on each pair of shoes. I think that’s an affordable price for me since I only pay for classic designs that I may wear on multiple occasions. However, if I long for some shoes of limited edition or from a luxury brand, of course, I need to have more budget for that.

Well, I think those that cost 200 RMB or so are perfect. You know, for everyday use, that price is not too high or too low. I can afford them. Also, at that price, I can easily find shoes that are comfortable and durable.

sub_questions.content: Do you spend a lot of money on shoes?

answers.content: Well, I guess it depends how do you define a lot of money. For me, I‘m from a middle class family, so spending anything over 500 yuan on a pair of shoes is too much, as shoes are consumer goods and go out of style quite quickly. Therefore, I don’t think it‘s a good idea to spend too much on them.

sub_questions.content: How often do you buy shoes?

answers.content: I don’t buy shoes that often as I don’t need to have a lot of shoes in my daily life. I usually have around three pairs for each season, including flat shoes and sports shoes. Although it’s hard to say how often I buy shoes, last month was the summer sale, so I bought three new pairs and got a great bargain.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever bought shoes online?

answers.content: Many of my shoes were brought online. For some brands, they don’t have offline stores in China. Once I saw a pair of sandal on Instagram and found out that I could only buy it through its website online. It took me roughly two months to finally get my new shoes. I also buy sneakers online because there are often better discounts on shopping apps compared to stores offline.

I am a big fan of online shopping, however, shoes are one of the few things I don’t.
buy online often. Shoes come in many shapes and styles but only very few look good on me. I did buy a pair recently from a European e-commerce website called Selfridges during the sales season. The price was great although they haven’t actually arrived yet so I hope they’ll fit!

sub_questions.content: Do you know anyone who likes to buy a lot of shoes?

answers.content: I have a few female friends who buy a lot of different types of shoes, such as high heels, ballet flats, sneakers and loafers. I think the reason is that they want to match their shoes with their different outfits. I mean, jeans can be paired with sneakers, and if you are dressed formally, you can put on leather shoes.

sub_questions.content: What are your favorite type of shoes?

answers.content: Canvas sneakers are my favorite type of shoes, which I wear on many occasions such as going to the grocers, chilling at the park, travelling out-of-town or even working at the office. I work in a school and I am all about being active and staying on my feet, so I prefer wearing something comfortable to walk around in. They are also easy to clean.

questions.content: What's your favorite color?
What's the color you dislike? Why？
What colors do your friends like most?
What color makes you uncomfortable in your room?
Have you ever bought something because of its color?
Do you like dark colors?
Are there any colors you dislike?
Do you usually wear clothes in your favourite colour?

sub_questions.content: What's your favorite color?

answers.content: That’s quite a hard question for me to answer. I like all colors, although I would say my favourite color is red. I enjoy the feeling when my mind and eyes are stimulated when I see something red, which I think is the case for a lot of people. That’s why I like all shades of red, even burgundy and pink.

My favorite color is blue. I like blue because Blue is a non-threatening color that calls to mind feelings of calmness or serenity. While seeing the color blue, I feel like being surrounded by the sea and sky. Both are enormous and placid. I once read an article that said people who like blue are reliable and trustworthy. This is exactly who I am.

sub_questions.content: What's the color you dislike? Why？

answers.content: Well, to be honest, I'm not a big fan of the color black. It just doesn't appeal to me, you know? I prefer more vibrant and colorful hues that uplift my mood. Black, on the other hand, seems dull and gloomy. It lacks the energy and liveliness that I appreciate in other colors. So yeah, black is definitely not my cup of tea when it comes to choosing a favorite color.

I dislike the color white because it triggers emptiness to me, a feeling I don't like. In addition, throughout Chinese history, the white color has been tied to death as opposed to red which often symbolizes good luck in our culture. So the white color often reminds me the death of my beloved ones and evokes strong feelings of anger, aggression, fear, and sadness.

sub_questions.content: What colors do your friends like most?

answers.content: My friend Amy likes pink color so much so that it occupies a majority of clothes in her closet. I guess the main reason is that many people immediately associate the color with all things feminine and girly, and my friend likes to be seen as feminine and girly. Moreover, the color might also bring to the mind romance and holidays such as Valentine's Day. And who don’t like romance?

To be honest, my friends have different preferences for colors. Some of them like dark colors such as black, red, blue and so on, while others prefer odd colors like neon green or complementary colors. I feel like there is no good or bad preference. Instead, this actually shows their aesthetic appreciation.

sub_questions.content: What color makes you uncomfortable in your room?

answers.content: Red color makes me uncomfortable in my bedroom. I did some research and it is said that the human brain has a visceral response to the color red because it's the color of blood, and we associate it with danger, so it gets the blood running and the heart beating faster. That explains why the red color makes me uncomfortable while I'm trying to fall asleep at night.

I don’t like dark colors like deep blue or dark red, because these colors usually make the room look dingy and make me feel scared. When I’m not in a good mood in particular, these colors actually make me feel moody and depressed. In contrast, I prefer some warm and lighter colors, and canary-yellow is my favorite.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever bought something because of its color?

answers.content: Yes, I have. However, I tend to prioritize functionality over color when making purchases. For instance, when buying a new phone, I would consider features such as camera quality, battery life, and storage capacity instead of solely focusing on its color. Nonetheless, I understand that color can play a role in influencing purchasing decisions for many people.

Yes, I have bought something because of its color. For instance, I once purchased a vibrant red dress for a special occasion. The striking color caught my attention immediately, making it stand out among other options. The boldness of the color made me feel confident and energized when wearing it, which was exactly what I was looking for. The unique shade of red added an extra charm to the dress and enhanced my overall appearance.

sub_questions.content: Do you like dark colors?

answers.content: Yes, I do like dark colors, like black, dark blue, and so on. I think generally, items with dark colors are more lasting, especially with clothing. That’s why I always go with darker shades as they coordinate well with different items of clothing. What’s more, I always choose a dark outfit at work to highlight my profession as a teacher.

sub_questions.content: Are there any colors you dislike?

answers.content: Again, that’s quite a hard question for me to answer. I like all colors, but they have to be associated with different things like clothes, flowers, furnishings, etc. I would say my least favourite colors are yellow and green. They’re quite bright so I don’t own many things in those colors, and you’ll rarely see me wearing yellow or green. To me, they’re just not that appealing.

sub_questions.content: Do you usually wear clothes in your favourite colour?

answers.content: Not really. You see, my favourite color is blue, but it’s not suitable for all occasions. For example, If I’m going for an important interview, it’s not appropriate to wear casual blue clothes, so I’ll wear a formal black suit with a white shirt. However, when I go shopping with my friends or family, blue is always my first choice because it’s the color of the sky and puts me in a good mood.

questions.content: Do you know anything about growing plants?
Do Chinese people send plants as gifts?
Did you grow any plants when you were young?
Do you keep plants at home?

sub_questions.content: Do you know anything about growing plants?

answers.content: I know a little bit about growing plants. I understand the basics like providing sufficient sunlight, watering regularly, and using proper soil. I also know that different plants have specific needs, so it's important to research and care for them accordingly.

Yeah, I know quite a bit about gardening. I know how to grow aloe vera(芦荟). The first step in growing aloe vera is to realize that this plant grows best in a dry environment. Aloe plants cannot tolerate standing water. Another important thing about caring for Aloe Vera potted plants is that they need proper light. Growing an aloe vera home pot plant is not only easy, but it also provides the family with a plant that helps treat minor burns and rashes.

sub_questions.content: Do Chinese people send plants as gifts?

answers.content: Yes, Chinese people do send plants as gifts, especially during special occasions like housewarmings or Chinese New Year. Plants such as lucky bamboo and bonsai are popular choices because they symbolize good fortune, prosperity, and longevity.

Yes, we do. In ancient China, a young man would use the peony as a gift to show his affection for a woman. When seeing someone off, a poet, scholar or official would pick up a willow branch and give it to the person who is leaving. Nowadays, influenced by western culture, people will send roses to express their love for others.

sub_questions.content: Did you grow any plants when you were young?

answers.content: Yes, when I was young, I grew some plants. I remember planting sunflower seeds in our garden with my parents. Watching them grow and bloom was a rewarding experience that taught me patience and responsibility.

Yes. When I was young, I used to have two kinds of potted plants in my home: an aloe plant and a peace lily. The gel of the aloe plant has a number of healing properties. Not only does it soothe skin burns and cuts, it can also help to monitor the air quality in your home. Also, the beautiful peace lily is a wonderful flower to keep in the home.

sub_questions.content: Do you keep plants at home?

answers.content: Yes, I do keep plants at home. I have a few potted plants in my living room and bedroom. They add a touch of nature to my home and help create a relaxing atmosphere. Taking care of them is also a nice hobby for me.

Yes, I do. Visually, plants make my home look more beautiful. They are the best decorations. Also, it’s not just about improving the indoor aesthetic. Even if I don’t normally notice a plant’s presence, knowing one is there can subconsciously make me feel calm and relaxed.

questions.content: Would you want to be a celebrity in the future?
Do you think, to be famous, a person needs to have some special talent (or ability)?
What kinds of people become famous?
In general, how do people become famous?
Who is your favorite celebrity in China?

sub_questions.content: Would you want to be a celebrity in the future?

answers.content: No, that’s the last thing I want. Well, I am pretty satisfied with being someone in the crowd. So I get to choose how I want to live my life. As a celebrity, you basically have to exchange your freedom for fame. You would be watched everywhere you go, and every move you make could be made on the tabloids. The unmeasurable stress could really tear me apart.

sub_questions.content: Do you think, to be famous, a person needs to have some special talent (or ability)?

answers.content: Well, normally yes. If you look around, most famous individuals are exceptionally good in their field. But still it doesn’t speak for all. Some people were born with fame. For example, if you are Bill Gates’ children, you can’t help being watched by the media and the public, can you?

sub_questions.content: What kinds of people become famous?

answers.content: Ah, people who are particularly talented in some area like sing、acting or dancing could easily get admirers in the first place. And I think people who can grasp the opportunities can also get success. Being good at something and being famous depend on good opportunities, I think. So if you know how to take advantage of the situation, you are more likely to get famous.

sub_questions.content: In general, how do people become famous?

answers.content: I think these days, many celebrities get known by attending talent shows. There are all sorts of contests out there, you know, beauty contest, singing contest or even painting contest. Whatever the program is, as long as it attracts audience, the top candidates will receive fame.

sub_questions.content: Who is your favorite celebrity in China?

answers.content: Jay Chou, of course. That's a household name in China. You know, he is incredibly famous in pop music. Oh, actually, he’s more than a singer, he produces his own music and he also produced a few movies that won prestigeous film awards in the country. But even with great fame, he’s a down to earth guy who’s humble and gentle. That’s why he’s my idol.

questions.content: What apps have you recently used？
What kinds of apps are you usually interested in？
How do you find out about different apps？
What kinds of apps would you like to use in future?

What was the first app you used?
What’s the most popular app in your country?
What kinds of apps would you like to see in the future?
What kinds of apps do you often use?

sub_questions.content: What apps have you recently used？

answers.content: Recently, I’ve been using a few apps quite frequently. For example, I use Duolingo to practice Spanish because I’m trying to learn a new language. I also use Spotify to listen to music and podcasts during my commute. Another app I’ve been using a lot is Baidu Maps, especially when I’m exploring new places or trying to avoid traffic.

I recently used Bilibili, a video sharing like YouTube. But what's special about it is its real-time captioning system that displays user comments as streams of scrolling subtitles overlaid on the video playback screen. Such subtitles are simultaneously broadcast to all viewers in real-time, creating a chat room experience in which users feel like watching and playing together with others.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of apps are you usually interested in？

answers.content: I’m usually interested in apps that are either educational or make life more convenient. For instance, I love language-learning apps like Duolingo or Memrise. I also enjoy productivity apps like Notion, which helps me organize my tasks and notes. On the other hand, I sometimes download fitness apps, like Nike Training Club, to follow workout routines at home.

I'm interested in memory training apps because improving memory for names, faces, information and other data can be helpful for my daily life. Yet not only can these apps train my brain to concentrate, relax, learn, accomplish goals, they are themselves interesting to play with. I find them a great way to kill time.

sub_questions.content: How do you find out about different apps？

answers.content: I usually discover new apps through recommendations from friends or online reviews. For example, my friends often suggest useful apps they’ve tried, like budgeting tools or travel planners. I also check the “Top Charts” or “Editor’s Choice” sections in the App Store to see what’s trending. Sometimes, I come across apps through social media ads or blogs that review tech products.

I usually get word-of-mouth recommendations from my best mates. For example, last weekend when we took a selfie together, I found a really cute filter on my friend’s photo app which applied a baby blue mask on all of our faces. It covered all the blemishes and brightened our eyes. My friend told me the app name, so afterwards I downloaded it from the app store.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of apps would you like to use in future?

answers.content: In the future, I’d like to explore apps that focus on mental health and well-being, like meditation or mindfulness apps. I’ve heard great things about Headspace and Calm, so I’m curious to try them. I’m also interested in apps that use AI to personalize experiences, like fitness coaches or language tutors. Lastly, I’d love to try more AR (augmented reality) apps, especially for travel or education, as they seem really innovative.

A “slack for families”. An app that tracks all family members and keeps everyone updated (without them doing anything). Notifications such as “X left home”, “Y is arriving at work”, “Z is at Safeway” keep the family automatically updated on where they are, and let them have conversations around it “While you’re at Safeway get some milk”.

sub_questions.content: What's the most popular app in your country?

answers.content: The most popular app in my country is WeChat. WeChat is China's most popular messaging app with a monthly user base of more than 1 billion people. But it offers so much more than messaging, allowing its users to do everything from payments to the ability to book flights and hotels.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of apps are you generally interested in？

answers.content: I’m interested in apps that can provide me with insightful and factually correct content, such as Quora and Reddit. Members of these apps often answer questions willingly, and from their own perspective, instead of for commercial benefits. I find their answers really interesting.

sub_questions.content: Which apps have you used recently？

answers.content: I downloaded some educational apps for children called ABC Mouse, Starfall and Khan Academy only last week because my niece, who turned six years old last month, asked me to help her with learning English. Since I didn’t know much about kids’ English, I downloaded these apps to help me out.

sub_questions.content: Do old people in your country like to use apps?

answers.content: -No, based on what I've observed, old people in my country don't like to use apps. Take my grandma for example, she is 74-years old but can only use the smartphone to make calls and texts. She told me that she still prefers to get her news from the newspaper and her friends will text her old Nokia phone to let her know if there's a good film on TV.

Surprisingly, yes! Thanks to the widespread use of both the Internet and smartphones, vast numbers of old people have become app users. My grandfather, for example, uses apps even more often than me! He enjoys chatting with his friends, playing online card games and doing online shopping. I think these apps make their lives more colorful.

sub_questions.content: What was the first app you used?

answers.content: The first app I used is a game app on Nokia called snake. I guess it is indeed an entire generation’s obsession. I remember playing Snake II when I was as young as six or seven. I would play it on my mom’s old Nokia during car rides home from school. Snake just challenges the most primal part of you. You see it growing with each thing it eats and you can’t help but keep going.

The first app I used was a music app. I still remember when my father bought me my first smartphone when I was 15 years old. I downloaded this music app as soon as I got the phone and started to listen to my favorite pop songs. I still use this app today and I actually pay the membership fee so I can listen to music in higher quality.

sub_questions.content: What’s the most popular app in your country?

answers.content: I think the most popular app in my country is Alipay. People enjoy using it because it means they don’t have to carry their wallets around with them anymore. By simply scanning a QR code, we can pay for almost everything through the app. As has been mentioned in the media, the app is so popular and successful that it has completely changed the consuming habits of the whole country.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of apps would you like to see in the future?

answers.content: I would love to see an app in the future which could monitor the health conditions of people. Concerns about the health of society are being voiced more and more frequently online. However, most people are too busy to go for checkups at hospital, so if there was an app which could monitor our health, it would save us lots of time and ensure our well-being.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of apps do you often use?

answers.content: -I often use mobile game apps because they put me in a different mindset — and apparently a much better mood — than social apps like Facebook and Twitter. When playing mobile games, I feel relaxed and focused. A huge chunk of all the games available on the AppStore and Google Play are free-to-play. That means I don't have to spend a dime but can have all the fun.

I often use apps to communicate with friends and family such as Wechat and QQ. Since I live in a school dormitory during the week, I keep in touch with my parents using these apps. It is really simple and convenient and doesn’t cost a thing. I like to share every moment of my life with my parents, so I usually send them lots of photos. I think these apps keep all family members closely connected.

Do you think handwriting is important?

What impression does a person’s handwriting have on other people?

How did you learn to write?
When do children begin to write in your country?

How do most people write nowadays?
Do you usually write by hand or by using a computer / typewriter / word-processor?（Which do you prefer, handwriting or typing?）

sub_questions.content: What's the differences between handwriting and typing?

answers.content: It is more efficient to type on a computer compared to writing with a pen. If you want to produce a long article, and maybe some edits are required when you finish, typing on the computer is recommended. However, when it comes to creative writing, some visual aids are necessary, which are not convenient to complete on computer.

sub_questions.content: Which do you prefer, handwriting or typing?

answers.content: Well, as I mentioned earlier, I would rather type than write by hand. For one, I’m a fast typer. It’s always easier for me to type things. And for another, my handwriting can be clumsy. I really don’t want to show that to people only when I have to.

sub_questions.content: Do you think handwriting is important?

answers.content: Well, on some occasions, yes. Say if you are writing a Thank You card, it’s better to write by hand to show your sincerity. Also, handwriting today is more of a form of art that all students should practice. So it’s definitely an important skill.

sub_questions.content: What are the differences between handwriting and typing?

answers.content: There are so many distinct differences between them. The first that comes to mind is that writing by hand is more time-consuming while typing is more efficient. Besides, handwriting varies from person to person, but when inputting on a laptop, if we choose the same font, we can produce the exact same thing. Another difference is that typing does not consume ink and paper but writing by hand does.

sub_questions.content: Do you like handwriting?

answers.content: Not really. I only write by hand when I have to, ‘cause honestly my handwritten characters aren’t very elegant. They even look clumsy. I especially don’t like to sit paper exams because of my awkward handwriting, you know. So, yeah, I really don’t like it.

Yes, I do. I write with a pen every day because I have a habit of journaling. Personally, I am a visual person, so handwriting gives me freedom when I want to draw. Also, writing by hand can help me avoid distractions, giving me some precious “ME” time.

sub_questions.content: How can children today improve (or practice) their handwriting?

answers.content: I think it is necessary for children to attend some handwriting courses. With the help of teachers, children can cultivate their interest and improve their handwriting effectively and systematically. Besides, as the saying goes, practice makes perfect. Children should draw up a daily plan and insist on practicing their handwriting every day so that they can make progress little by little.

sub_questions.content: What impression does a person’s handwriting have on other people?

answers.content: It can be considered that handwriting is the outward expression of inner quality. Clear and neat handwriting can be a better introduction than any type of business card. If someone’s handwriting is messy and hard to interpret, it can be concluded to some degree that the person is not well organized in their life.

sub_questions.content: Do you think handwriting is very important nowadays?

answers.content: With typing becoming the trend in modern society, people don’t have many chances to pick up their pens in daily life anymore. But as far as I am concerned, handwriting is so crucial that nothing can replace it. On the one hand, it is the outward expression of inner quality. Clear and neat handwriting is like a professional name card. On the other hand, it is a type of art instead of merely a tool.

sub_questions.content: How did you learn to write?

answers.content: As far as I can recall, it was in primary school that I began to learn to write. There was a handwriting course which was designed to teach kids to write. I remember starting with some basic Chinese characters like ‘one’, ‘big’, ‘sky’. The teacher would show us pictures before teaching us how to write the words.

sub_questions.content: When do children begin to write in your country?

answers.content: Great significance has been attached to writing in the traditional Chinese education system. And good handwriting is the symbol of good-quality education. As a result, children in my country start to write from an early age. Even children in kindergartens will be taught to write some basic Chinese characters like ‘one’, ’big’ and ‘sky’.

sub_questions.content: Do you think computers might one day replace handwriting?

answers.content: Typing on computers has been the trend in the 21st century and an increasing number of corporations are participating in the digital reformation. Be that as it may, handwriting is so significant for all of humankind that it could never be replaced by any other thing. On the one hand, it is more than just a tool. It is art. On the other hand, the precious skill of handwriting has been passed down from our ancestors, so in no way should it be abandoned.

sub_questions.content: How do most people write nowadays?

answers.content: The most common method of writing nowadays is poles apart from what it used to be. As time has gone on, more and more people have turned to digital tools, and the concept of a ‘paperless office’at the start of 21st century has now become a reality, with computers becoming a common tool in modern companies. Very few people other than students still write on paper.

sub_questions.content: Do you usually write by hand or by using a computer / typewriter / word-processor?（Which do you prefer, handwriting or typing?）

answers.content: Given the choice, I would say that I prefer typing to writing by hand. For one thing, computers can process various forms of media, so I can easily insert pictures, sounds and even videos into my digital documents. Also, it is more environmentally friendly to type than to write on paper. Last but not least, it is so much more convenient storing data on a computer, and the risk of losing documents is minimized through backing up.

questions.content: Do you want to live on another planet?
Do you like to see the stars at night?
Is there a good place to look at the sky where you live?
Do you prefer the sky in the morning or the sky at night?
Can you see the moon and stars at night where you live?
Do you like to look at the sky? Why?

sub_questions.content: Do you want to live on another planet?

answers.content: To be honest, I don't want to. Our planet, the earth, has so many breath-taking places that I haven’t explored yet, and I love my current life with my family, friends and my loved ones around me. I definitely do not want to move to another planet and lose all the fun on the earth.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to see the stars at night?

answers.content: I love it! It’s a shame that in most cities, it’s barely possible to see the stars at night. But in some rural areas, people can still enjoy the beauty of the stars at night. I remember when I travelled to New Zealand and we stayed in a camper van on a star-gazing site in the South Island. We were watching the stars in the sky and even the Milky Way was clearly visible. That kind of beauty is really hard to express in words.

sub_questions.content: Is there a good place to look at the sky where you live?

answers.content: Yes, I suppose so. I’ve got a tiny but clean balcony in my room where I think it is a perfect location to appreciate the sky. Standing on the balcony and staring towards the sky, I always find it mysterious and attractive. It’s like an exquisite masterpiece of Vincent van Gogh. So, whenever I have time, I go to this small balcony to enjoy the appealing view.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer the sky in the morning or the sky at night?

answers.content: Actually, I don't have a preference for either of them, but if I have to make a choice I would say the sky in the early hours. The reason is that, in the morning the sky is still covered in mist and I am really into this kind of vague view. Also, the rising sun in the morning sky represents the brand-new beginning of another day, which I think is a positive symbol.

sub_questions.content: Can you see the moon and stars at night where you live?

answers.content: Well, to be honest, I believe it is a pity that I can hardly see those celestial bodies clearly in the evening these days. Since the air pollution has influenced the visibility at night greatly. The moon and stars can be spotted sometimes when the weather is nice enough, but I have to say it is becoming less and less frequent for me to see them.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to look at the sky? Why?

answers.content: Yes, I like to look at the sky, especially when the weather is good. I like it because I enjoy the blue of the clear sky with some snow-white clouds. When there is a breeze, you can see some thin clouds moving slowly. All this makes me feel like I’m in the quietest fairy tale. That’s why I like to look at the sky.

questions.content: What is your favorite festival?

sub_questions.content: What is your favorite festival?

answers.content: My favorite festival is the Mid-Autumn Festival. I have two reasons for that. First of all, I love mooncakes and there are a variety of fresh mooncakes to choose from during the Mid-Autumn Festival. Secondly, the moon is always super bright and round during the night. I can’t help but think of all the fairy tales about the moon, like Chang’e, which is absolutely super romantic.

sub_questions.content: How do you celebrate Spring Festival?

answers.content: For me, I always gather together with my family and friends. For family, we always prepare a scrumptious dinner and everyone stays in front of the TV and watches the Spring Festival Gala. For friends, we gather during the Spring Festival as well. We always go out to eat and then I like to go to karaoke afterwards, or we just stay in the teahouse to play cards.

sub_questions.content: What special food and activities do you have for these festivals?

answers.content: It depends on what kind of festivals. For example, for Spring Festival, Chinese people always go home no matter where they are because for us, this is the time for family reunion. For Mid-Autumn Festival we have the habit of eating mooncake. For Qing Ming Festival normally people go tomb sweeping to express their love for the ancestors.

sub_questions.content: How do you celebrate festivals in your country?

answers.content: Well, in my country we celebrate different festivals differently. For example, during the Dragon Boat Festival all the Chinese people eat rice dumplings. Some places even have the tradition of making rice dumplings at home with family members. At the same time, in some coastal cities in the south, the custom of dragon boat racing is used to celebrate this festival.
