sub_questions.content: Would you prefer an exciting holiday or a peaceful holiday?

answers.content: I would lean towards an adventurous holiday filled with thrilling activities like scuba diving or bungee jumping. The rush of excitement and adrenaline would make the vacation unforgettable. However, I also appreciate the tranquility of a peaceful holiday, such as a relaxing retreat in nature or a calm beach destination, allowing a break from the hustle and bustle of everyday life. Ultimately, a mix of both can provide a perfect balance for an enriching holiday experience.

I would definitely choose an exciting holiday! As a guy, I love the thrill of adventures like bungee jumping and exploring new places. The adrenaline rush from exciting activities keeps me energized and excited. Peaceful holidays are nice for relaxation, but the excitement of trying new things and pushing my boundaries is what truly makes a holiday memorable for me.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer a leisurely or a busy holiday?

answers.content: I personally prefer a leisurely holiday as it allows me to unwind and rejuvenate from the daily grind. With a relaxed itinerary, I can fully indulge in activities that bring me joy and tranquility, such as reading, exploring nature, or simply lounging by the beach. It provides me with the opportunity to disconnect from work and stress, enabling me to return refreshed and ready to take on challenges. A leisurely holiday is the perfect time for self-care and self-discovery.

Personally, I am inclined towards a busy holiday. I find satisfaction in exploring new places, engaging in thrilling activities, and immersing myself in the local culture. By having a packed itinerary, I can make the most of my time and create lasting memories. The adrenaline rush of trying new things excites me and fuels my passion for adventure. A leisurely holiday might be relaxing, but I crave the excitement and sense of accomplishment that a busy holiday offers.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to spend your day at home?

answers.content: Not really, I prefer being out and about. Spending my day at home can get a bit monotonous and dull for me. I like exploring new places, meeting people, and experiencing different environments. It helps me grow and keeps me motivated. On the contrary, being home all day can sometimes make me feel confined and unproductive. So, I prefer to make the most of my time by engaging in activities outside of the house.

Not really. I prefer to go out and explore. Staying at home all day can be quite boring for me. I enjoy being outdoors, trying new activities, and meeting new people. It helps me relax and rejuvenate. Plus, there's so much to see and experience in the world that I wouldn't want to miss out on.

sub_questions.content: What do you do on your holidays?

answers.content: On my holidays, I like to engage in a variety of activities depending on the destination and my mood. This could include sightseeing, trying out local cuisine, outdoor adventures like hiking or swimming, or simply relaxing and reading a book. The key is to make the most of the time off and enjoy every moment.

Normally I would make a trip overseas with my family or my friends. I love travelling but I always work a lot. However, holidays provide a perfect time for me to forget about everything and just enjoy myself. But of course with the epidemic now, I can’t travel, so I like to spend some quality time with my family.

sub_questions.content: Which public holiday do you like best?

answers.content: Among public holidays, I particularly enjoy the Chinese New Year festival. It's a time of celebration, tradition, and reunion with family and friends. The festive atmosphere, delicious food, and vibrant cultural activities make it a truly special occasion.

I like Spring Festival the most and I have two reasons for that. Firstly, people get the longest time off during Spring Festival. I think at least one week and some companies provide even longer holidays. Also family and friends gather together during Spring Festival, which means I get to see all my relatives and close friends.

sub_questions.content: Do you like holidays? Why?

answers.content: Yes, I absolutely love holidays. They provide a much-needed opportunity to relax, recharge, and explore new places or experiences. It's a chance to break away from routine, spend quality time with loved ones, and create lasting memories.

I love holidays! I mean, who doesn't? Holidays allow me to forget my work and studies, and I can have some time to myself. I always go abroad for my holiday so I get to explore more of the world. I also think holidays can recharge my energy levels so when I come back from a holiday I feel like I have more power to work.

sub_questions.content: Where did you go on your last holiday?

answers.content: For my last holiday, I went to visit my grandparents in the countryside. It was a refreshing break from the city life, surrounded by nature and tranquility. Spending time with family and reconnecting with my roots was truly enjoyable.

I went to Fiji with my boyfriend for my last holiday before the epidemic. It was a beautiful holiday and we totally enjoyed ourselves with the clear water and the nice sun. We went to the beach everyday, had cocktails in the bar and went on a whale-watching cruise. We saw one huge whale and several dolphins swimming around our cruise ship. God, it was amazing! I wish I could go again soon.

questions.content: Do most of your friends use a wallet?

sub_questions.content: Do most of your friends use a wallet?

answers.content: Well, I don't think so. I think most of my friends just use their phones to pay when they go out. I have seen some people carrying wallets, but they are either business men or they are the same as me, using wallets as card holders. So, I think most of my friends don’t use wallets.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever sent a wallet to someone as a gift?

answers.content: No I have never sent a wallet to someone as a gift. I think wallets are like perfumes, they are quite personal. Some people like them in black while others like them in gold. Each person’s requirements about wallets are different. Some people need big ones because they need to put a lot of cash inside while others prefer just a card holder. It’s hard to guess, so I don't send wallets to others.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever lost your wallet?

answers.content: No I don't think so. I think probably because I am a very cautious person, when I get in the car I always lock the door first. If I have to take a bus or a taxi, I hold my wallet in my hand so that I don't forget to take it with me later on or so that someone can’t steal it. When I walk on the street I always make sure the zip of my bag is not facing outwards. Therefore, so far I have never lost my wallet.

sub_questions.content: Do you use a wallet?

answers.content: Yes, I have a wallet. My previous wallets were all big ones so that I could put cash inside. But a few years back I changed my wallet to a small one. It has three compartments but you can only put cards inside. You know nowadays in China people don't really use cash. Instead we use our phone to pay everywhere. However, since I travel a lot, I always take my ID card and credit cards with me in the wallet.

sub_questions.content: What did you like to do the most when you were in primary school?

So, before the term started, students got to choose which sports they wanted to do, and on sports day, which was every Thursday afternoon, students could go to the playground and play sport the whole afternoon. I chose volleyball so I liked playing volleyball the most when I was in primary school.

I can take pictures or videos whenever I want and it offers more leg room. Also, the back seats are always bumpier, especially when the road conditions are bad. That’s why I prefer front seats more than back seats when travelling by car.

answers.content: Well, that’s hard to answer. I have travelled by car in North America, in Oceania and in Europe. But I would say the longest non-stop drive I made by car was from Frankfurt to Berlin, which took me almost 6 hours. In China, the furthest place I have travelled to by car is Urumqi. sub_questions.content: When do you travel by car?

answers.content: I travel by car all the time when I have a vacation. Even when I go abroad, I rent a car at the airport so that I can get started quickly. Like I said before, I don't think taking public transportation is convenient when someone needs to go to their desired destination somewhere in a rural area or in the mountains. So I take cars all the time when I go travelling.

sub_questions.content: Do you like traveling by car?

answers.content: Yes, I love to travel by car. In fact, I have done quite a lot of self-driving tours with my family and friends. I think that traveling by car is more convenient than traveling by buses or trains. Firstly, you can make stops whenever and wherever you want. Secondly, the routes of buses or trains are fixed, which means you cannot go to your desired destination without some walking, which is super inconvenient when your destination is somewhere in a rural area or in the mountains.

sub_questions.content: Is there any education about environmental protection at school?

The content was some of the most common issues, such as the harm caused by plastic products to the environment, or the environmental damage caused by thermal power generation. I reckon the content could be more up-to-date and maybe more specific.

sub_questions.content: Do you think you’ve done enough to protect the environment?

answers.content: I am trying but I think I can do more. For example, I like to watch TV while sleeping and sometimes the TV is still left running after I fall asleep. Also, I always order take aways and this involves a lot of plastic products. I know these are not good ways to protect the environment and I will correct these behaviors.

sub_questions.content: How can we protect the environment?

answers.content: Well, I think the most urgent thing is to say no to plastic products. As we all know that plastic products are difficult to decompose naturally and some can even take thousands of years to completely decompose. Scientists have discovered plastic even in the deepest ocean floor. After fish eat plastic pellets, these plastics cannot be digested by them. Furthermore, when humans eat these fish, plastic particles will continue to be deposited in the human body. What a terrible thing this is!

sub_questions.content: Would you like to work for a company related to environmental protection?

answers.content: Yes, why not? As we can see, environmental pollution is getting more and more serious nowadays, but the good aspect is, people are more aware of the importance of environmental protection. If I had the chance to work for a company related to that, I would know of more ways to protect the environment. I think it would be a fun experience.

sub_questions.content: Do you think students need more relaxion time?

Providing students with sufficient relaxation time not only improves their mental well-being but also enhances their overall productivity and academic performance. Balancing study with relaxation is essential for their holistic development.

Yes, I do think so, especially for high school students. You know the high school students are always under huge stress because of all the tests and the university entrance exams. The students always look so tired so I think they really need more relaxation time.

It's a precious opportunity to rejuvenate and recharge my energy for the upcoming challenges.

Yes, absolutely. I love vacations and I love to travel abroad during my vacations. I live in an inland city, but I am a huge fan of the beach and the sand. Just thinking about lying on the beach and drinking a cocktail or some cold beverage makes me feel relaxed.

Well, for some people maybe the answer is yes, but seeing as I am not a big sports fan so I will say for me it’s a no. I rarely do sports even though I always tell my friends that I should. sub_questions.content: Do people in your country often send flowers to others?

answers.content: Certainly, in my country, exchanging flowers is quite common among friends and family as a gesture of affection or celebration. Flowers are often sent on special occasions like birthdays, anniversaries, or to convey congratulations or condolences. They symbolize a variety of emotions and are considered a thoughtful way to express feelings without words. It's a beautiful tradition that adds color and fragrance to relationships.

In my country, sending flowers is a common gesture on special occasions like birthdays or anniversaries. However, it's not limited to just those events. We also send flowers to friends or family members as a symbol of gratitude or support during challenging times. Flowers are seen as a thoughtful way to express emotions and brighten someone's day, making them a popular choice for various occasions.

Yes, I do enjoy taking photos of flowers in my daily life. I find the vibrant colors and intricate patterns fascinating. Whenever I see a beautiful flower, I can't resist capturing its beauty through my lens. It's a peaceful and rewarding hobby that brings a sense of calmness and appreciation for nature in my day-to-day routine.

Yes, I have a dear friend who adores flowers. She has a beautiful garden at her home where she spends hours taking care of various blooms. Her favorite are peonies, and she cultivates them with such dedication that her garden looks like a floral paradise. Her love for flowers is truly inspiring and she often shares her gardening tips with me, which I find really helpful.

sub_questions.content: Do you love flowers?

I deeply appreciate the vibrant beauty of flowers. Their delicate petals and enchanting fragrances never fail to uplift my spirits. Whether it's a bouquet of roses symbolizing love or a colorful array of wildflowers brightening up a room, flowers bring joy and serenity into my life. They are nature's exquisite gift that never ceases to captivate me.

sub_questions.content: Have you sent flowers to other people?

Ah, the only person that I’ve ever bought flowers for is my mother. Her favourite flower is Lily so I bought Lilies for her on Mother’s Day. She kept them in a beautiful glass vase and changed the water regularly. Under her care, they can last for two weeks or even longer!

sub_questions.content: Have you planted any flowers?

answers.content: Yes, I have planted flowers before. I enjoy gardening as it allows me to connect with nature and beautify my surroundings. Planting flowers also brings a sense of satisfaction and joy as I witness their growth and bloom over time.

sub_questions.content: Are there any flowers that have special meanings in China?

answers.content: Yes, there are several flowers that have special meanings in China. For example, peonies symbolize prosperity and wealth, while chrysanthemums represent longevity and good fortune. Additionally, the lotus flower is often associated with purity and enlightenment in Chinese culture.

Absolutely! Peony has been popular in China for hundreds of years. You would find many traditional Chinese artworks of it. Actually, it used to be our national flower back in Qing Dynasty. And it always carries special meaning with it. Actually, you would find its print at the back of our one-yuan coin! Many people relate peonies with patriotism in my country.

sub_questions.content: What kind of flowers do you know?

Did you try any special costumes when you were young?
Do you like to wear special costumes?

sub_questions.content: Do you ever buy special costumes?

answers.content: Like I said, I did buy the Michael Jackson dancing costume once. And honestly, that costume was not cheap! I almost used up my savings for that! My mother was pretty upset that I bought something that looked completely ridiculous! But anyway, I had a good time wearing it. So, it’s all worth it, I guess.

sub_questions.content: When was your last time you wore a special costume?

answers.content: That’s probably two years ago when I went to a Halloween party with a bunch of friends. I wore zombie make-up and dressed in a black zombie robe. I tell you, I looked pretty scary! At least kids preferred not to play their little tricks with me. Yeah, good costumes!

sub_questions.content: Did you try any special costumes when you were young?

answers.content: Well, I think I did. My favorite pop star was Michael Jackson. Honestly, he was a legend! Well, I so wanted to imitate him dancing, so I went to a costume store and bought his costume. I did have a lot of fun dancing in the bling-bling suit. But then the enthusiasm faded. That costume is buried deep in my closet now.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to wear special costumes?

answers.content: I wouldn’t say so. Well, I don’t mind putting on special costumes on special occasions, like at a Halloween party. Oh, and also, when visiting some ancient tourist sites of costume themes. But then again, I only wear costumes so that I am not the odd one out in a group activity. To be honest, I never feel particularly comfortable in those costumes.

questions.content: Do you often forget people's names?
How do you feel when people can't remember your name?
How do you remember people's names?
Is it easy for you to remember people's names?

sub_questions.content: Do you often forget people's names?

answers.content: Not really. As I mentioned before, asking about the spelling and how to write the name is what I do when a new friend is introduced to me, so that I have less trouble remembering. Also, I put them in my contact list as well to help me avoid forgetting it.

sub_questions.content: How do you feel when people can't remember your name?

answers.content: To be honest, I may feel a bit embarrassed initially because I think it’s essential to remember each other’s names since this is an important way to show respect. However, I do understand that sometimes my Chinese name can be a bit hard for them to remember, so I will just remind them with patience.

sub_questions.content: How do you remember people's names?

answers.content: Actually, whenever I ask someone their name, I always ask them how to spell it. I suppose in this way, people will sometimes explain their name in detail or even tell me the meaning behind it. So, it will be quicker for me to learn their names by heart.

sub_questions.content: Is it easy for you to remember people's names?

answers.content: Well, it really depends. For Chinese people, I find it easier to remember while for foreigners, it may be a little bit more challenging. I guess the reason behind this is probably because Chinese is my first language and I’m familiar with those common names.

questions.content: (4)How do you feel when you can't see any beautiful views from your window?
Do you want to live in a house with beautiful window views?
Do you like to watch the scenery from your window?
What scenery can you see from the window of your home/dormitory?

sub_questions.content: (4)How do you feel when you can't see any beautiful views from your window?

answers.content: Well, I feel a little bit depressed and suffocated if all I can see in my place is just white walls, especially if I’m already slightly upset. I may feel like I’m living in a cage or being confined in a tiny place, deprived of freedom.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to live in a house with beautiful window views?

answers.content: Absolutely, yes! Who doesn't? Personally, I believe that living in a place with a nice view can actually help with both our life and work/study. I mean, our mood can be lit up by the beautiful views, so we can enjoy our time better with friends and family. Also, when we get home from work/studying, seeing a delightful view can relieve our pressure.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to watch the scenery from your window?

answers.content: I definitely do. I feel like I can spend hours in front of the window every day, appreciating the gorgeous view with a cup of tea in my hand. Whenever I’m standing in front of the glass, I just feel a deep sense of tranquility, as if the time was slowed and my life was extended.

sub_questions.content: What scenery can you see from the window of your home/dormitory?

answers.content: Well, I’m lucky enough to be living in a high-rise flat equipped with a top-to-bottom glass window, so when I get up, I can always see the sun high above the skyline of the city. And in the afternoon, the view I can enjoy is the stunning sunset and sometimes birds flying across the sky.

questions.content: Will you stay in China in the future?
Do you know the history of your country well?
What makes you feel proud of your country?
Which part of your country do you want to live in?

sub_questions.content: Will you stay in China in the future?

answers.content: Quite likely, yes. I do want to go overseas to experience exotic cultures and meet different people, but in the long run, I still plan to come back to China because I’ve got family and friends here, who are very important to me.

sub_questions.content: Do you know the history of your country well?

answers.content: I suppose I could say yes. I always love reading about Chinese history, especially in feudal society. You’d be amazed by how some emperors managed to take over the throne. I learnt a lot about ancient Chinese wisdom from reading about history.

sub_questions.content: What makes you feel proud of your country?

answers.content: Well, I’ve talked to a few foreign friends about this before and with no exceptions, they all praised China for its splendid history and sophisticated philosophies, like Daoism and Confucianism. I’m telling you, it feels good to hear people speak proudly about my culture.

sub_questions.content: Which part of your country do you want to live in?

answers.content: I would like to move down to the south in the future. You see, the city I come from gets freezing cold in winter. People don’t really go out in winter, so the streets and even shops are usually empty. You get a bleak feeling sometimes, which I really don’t like.

questions.content: How do you feel after taking a nap？
Do you take a nap when you are taking your rest?
What do you usually do when you are resting？
How often do you take a rest?

sub_questions.content: How do you feel after taking a nap？

answers.content: Taking a nap really helps me to unwind after a busy day at work, and although it’s just a short nap, it really helps to refresh my mind. Even though it’s just a short nap for 20 minutes or so, pausing your work and study for a short time definitely makes our work and life happier and healthier.

sub_questions.content: Do you take a nap when you are taking your rest?

answers.content: Yes. I like to take a nap for 30 minutes at noon, which is a very common habit for most Chinese I believe, since we think it's good for our digestive system and we feel refreshed afterwards. What’s more, our work efficiency will significantly improve as well.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do when you are resting？

answers.content: I usually just stay alone, close my eyes and take a nap. Sometimes, when I’m under great pressure, I prefer to listen to some soft music or play video games to release the stress, or just order a cup of milk tea and some desserts to treat myself.

sub_questions.content: How often do you take a rest?

answers.content: Not frequently. Actually, I only take a rest twice a day during my working hours. I usually take a nap after lunch at noon and sometimes I order a cup of milk tea in the afternoon and take a break for 15 minutes. This is quite useful for me because taking a rest is a good way to recharge and then when I go back, my work efficiency will be significantly improved.

questions.content: Do you talk with your friends about the programs you’ve watched?
Do you like watching the same kind of programs all the time?
What kind of TV programs do you like?
Do you prefer to watch programs on the TV or on your cellphone？

sub_questions.content: Do you talk with your friends about the programs you’ve watched?

answers.content: Yes, I do. To be honest, programs are one of the most common topics among us. We tend to talk about the TV programs and movies that we’ve recently watched when we’re together. Most of the TV programs and films that I’ve seen were recommended by my friends.

sub_questions.content: Do you like watching the same kind of programs all the time?

answers.content: Of course not. For me, watching TV is a good way to relax and kill time, so watching the same kind of program over and over again would make me feel bored and drive me crazy. I prefer to watch different types of programs and most of the time I just watch reality shows and series. But sometimes I also watch the news and documentaries, and when there are some good movies online, I’ll also watch them.

sub_questions.content: What kind of TV programs do you like?

answers.content: I’m really fond of reality shows and series. Reality shows are usually quite funny and creative, and series, especially ones with a lot of action, move along at a brisk pace and I’m easily captured by their complicated plots. I really enjoy watching TV with my family and friends and discussing the programs together.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to watch programs on the TV or on your cellphone？

answers.content: I prefer to watch programs on my mobile phone because it’s just convenient. Compared with watching programs on TV, there are more TV series and films online and they have a quick turnover. Moreover, I’m not the only one who does so. The people around me also choose to watch TV on their cellphones and it’s quite common in China now.

questions.content: What's your favorite color when decorating your home?
Do Chinese people like redecorating their homes？
What kind of decorations do you prefer?
What's the decoration like in your home?

sub_questions.content: What's your favorite color when decorating your home?

answers.content: For me, the first choice must be white because white is pure and it’s my favorite color. The walls at my home are all in white. Some people think white looks boring, but I think when everything is white at home, different layers of white decorations can create different shades of beauty.

sub_questions.content: Do Chinese people like redecorating their homes？

answers.content: As far as I’m concerned, most Chinese would say no since redecorating their homes is kind of difficult and time-consuming. But for those who have been living in the same place for more than several decades, they might consider redecorating their homes so as to pursue a better quality of life.

sub_questions.content: What kind of decorations do you prefer?

answers.content: I prefer simple decorations without any colorful patterns and complicated textures on them. For instance, single-colored photo frames and glass vases are my favorite. In addition, there are a variety of green plants in my room. They reduce dust and keep the air fresh in the room, and because of them, my room looks lively and cozy.

sub_questions.content: What's the decoration like in your home?

answers.content: Well, the decoration in my home is quite simple, and actually there's no particular style. I prefer minimalism, so I try to buy all furniture and decorations in white and grey. I think it makes my room look more spacious and cleaner. And most of all, I like this style.

questions.content: Do you learn maths now? Why/why not?
What can people do with maths in their daily life?
Do you think maths is difficult?
Do students learn maths in secondary schools in China?

sub_questions.content: Do you learn maths now? Why/why not?

answers.content: I’m afraid not. Now I’m an art student in college and I don’t have to learn maths anymore. Maths is not the foundation in this area. What we learn in art school is quite artistic and theoretical, and I’m glad that I don’t have to do calculations now.

sub_questions.content: What can people do with maths in their daily life?

answers.content: I suppose maths is a necessary part of life and we need to do some simple equations almost every day, such as when we make a shopping list with a budget or when we need to calculate our GPA at school. That’s where simple maths comes in.

sub_questions.content: Do you think maths is difficult?

answers.content: Well, maths is actually quite hard and I would say it’s like rocket science for me! I find it really hard to memorize all the principles and theories in textbooks, so I failed most of my maths tests when I was a high school student.

sub_questions.content: Do students learn maths in secondary schools in China?

answers.content: Well, I suppose most students have to learn maths in my country because it’s a compulsory subject. That means we have to learn it in order to graduate. Maths is the basis of many science subjects and it’s quite important. We learn about algebra, geometry and so forth.

questions.content: Do you like planting trees? Why?
What kinds of trees do you plant?
Have you ever planted a tree?
What kind of trees do people usually plant in your country? How about your hometown?

sub_questions.content: Do you like planting trees? Why?

answers.content: Well, although I don’t have many opportunities to actually plant trees as a city girl, I don’t mind doing it. What’s more, I believe that he who plants a tree plants a hope and a great wish for the future. And of course, it not only does good to our physical health but also helps to protect the environment.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of trees do you plant?

answers.content: We planted a sweet-scented Osmanthus tree in a yard behind our dormitory last spring. Toilsome as the process is, I think planting trees and waiting for them to blossom cultivate our patience and develop a sense of achievement. I hope I can smell the intoxicating fragrance once the tree has grown and florescence has come.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever planted a tree?

answers.content: Well, actually I do have experience of planting trees. It was part of our college’s labor project in which every class had to plant a tree together on campus. Not until that experience did I realize planting is exhausting and laborious work. Fortunately, after a long process of shoveling and sowing, we managed it with the joint effort of my classmates.

sub_questions.content: What kind of trees do people usually plant in your country? How about your hometown?

answers.content: Well, the trees people plant vary in my native land. For instance, in northern areas people prefer to plant hardy trees like birches and white pines to live through cold winters whereas people in the south tend to grow trees such as palms and willows which can grow best in moist climates. Take my hometown as an example. Chinese parasols are the most common kind of trees and are planted along both sides of the street in my city.

questions.content: Do you like to read by yourself or with other people?
In what places do you think it is difficult to read?
Do you like to read at home or in other places?
Do you read electronic books?
Do you like reading?
What kind of books do you read?
Do you read books related to your profession?

sub_questions.content: Do you like to read by yourself or with other people?

answers.content: I reckon I would prefer the former. I mean, as I’ve mentioned before, when reading by myself, I can just do whatever I want and react to the story naturally without thinking about how it may affect others around me. But after I finish reading, I enjoy sharing what I have learned with a bunch of friends to exchange ideas and communicate our thoughts.

sub_questions.content: In what places do you think it is difficult to read?

answers.content: Well, generally speaking, I can concentrate on reading anywhere if the content is intriguing enough, no matter if it is loud or quiet. However, when I need to put some effort into understanding the book, probably a noisy place with a lot of distractions will make it even more challenging for me, like on a bus where we have lots of people coming and going.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to read at home or in other places?

answers.content: Actually, I don't really mind. Reading during my commute is what I normally do because in that way I feel as if the time spent on public transport is shortened. However, when reading at home, I can take my shoes off and snuggle in bed and laugh or cry as much as I want to. So, I guess either will do for me.

sub_questions.content: Do you read electronic books?

answers.content: No, I don't use electronic books personally, because compared to real books, I think electronic books such as Kindles fail to give me a real sense of reading. Also, I prefer reading paper books because I’m a fan of collecting different ones, while I wouldn’t be able to collect electronic books.

sub_questions.content: Do you like reading?

answers.content: Of course, because I always believe that books are the ladder of human progress, especially for young students. Reading is crucial for teenagers, because the characters described in the books can be models for young readers. What’s more, reading makes humans wise and full of knowledge.

sub_questions.content: What kind of books do you read?

answers.content: As a student who’s majoring in economics, I prefer to read books that can broaden my horizons, especially interdisciplinary study books, which can take my knowledge to the next level and make me further understand my major.

sub_questions.content: Do you read books related to your profession?

answers.content: Yes, I do. Reading books related to my profession is the only way to get the information I need when I’m studying major-related courses. Also, reading these books is an indispensable way to understand the meaning of new concepts.

questions.content: Are you a fan of any sport teams? Why?
Do you watch sport matches? Why?
What’s your favourite sport? Why?
What sports did you do when you were a kid?
What kinds of sports would you like to try in the future?
Do you like to watch sports on TV?
Do you have a favorite sports star?
Do you play any sports?
What kinds of sports are popular in China?

sub_questions.content: Are you a fan of any sport teams? Why?

answers.content: Actually, I absolutely love the Lakers, the basketball team from the NBA. I love how all the team members work together seamlessly towards the same goal. Their team spirit is really inspiring. However, the recent passing of Kobe Bryant is really a mournful story and I know a lot of us love the Lakers because of him, but I still admire the team as a whole.

sub_questions.content: Do you watch sport matches? Why?

answers.content: I seldom do. I mean, actually, I don’t spend much time in front of the screen in general because my eyes get easily sore, so watching games on TV has never been part of my life. However, I do go to the sports court sometimes to watch real life matches because being there physically gives me a stronger sense of participation.

sub_questions.content: What’s your favourite sport? Why?

answers.content: I guess my old favorite was swimming as I have mentioned earlier. But now personally I prefer to jog. It is probably because there are no swimming pools around my neighborhood but we do have a quiet park where I can run a little bit. After taking up running, I’ve found that it is a really relaxing exercise and you don't need any specialist equipment to do it, so it has become my first choice when it comes to doing sports.

sub_questions.content: What sports did you do when you were a kid?

answers.content: Well, for the first few years of my life, I was passionate about going swimming. I don’t know why most of the children at my age were a little bit scared of water but I just found it exciting, especially during the summer time, when I would finish dinner and rush to the nearest pool. Thanks to my enthusiasm, I can now swim like a fish!

sub_questions.content: What kinds of sports would you like to try in the future?

answers.content: If I had the opportunity, I would like to try rock climbing and bungee jumping. I'm not a risk taker, and both are extreme sports, which is exactly why I haven’t had the chance to try them. My parents are too protective over me as I’m their only child, but intrinsically I am pretty adventurous, so I still want to experience them one day.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to watch sports on TV?

answers.content: Actually, our TV at home is often on the sports channel. My parents and I like to sit in front of the TV after dinner and watch football games and sometimes table tennis competitions. We comment on the players and make jokes about the games. Besides, I regard it as a great way to strengthen our family bond.

sub_questions.content: Do you have a favorite sports star?

answers.content: That would be Cristiano Ronaldo. Actually, I don’t play football myself, but I love watching the World Cup. Seriously, I’ve never missed one game since I started watching it. Ronaldo is the best football player on the field. He’s fast as light and incredibly quick-minded. To be honest, sometimes I only watch the game because I want to watch him play.

sub_questions.content: Do you play any sports?

answers.content: Yeah, I am a really sporty person. I am particularly good at badminton and volleyball. My parents are both great badminton players so I started playing at an early age. Then they also got me to participate in volleyball lessons after I entered middle school, so thanks to my parents, I’m great at both of them now.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of sports are popular in China?

answers.content: A lot of Chinese people like ball games, like table tennis, badminton, or basketball. Also, other activities like swimming or jogging are also pretty common. Sports are actually gaining greater popularity these days because people have started to realize the benefits to their health.

questions.content: Do you write many emails?
How often do you write an email?
What are the differences between emails and letters?
What kind(s) of email do you think is (are) the hardest to write?
How do you feel when you receive emails?
What kinds of emails do you receive that make you feel excited?
How do you communicate with others at work by email or is it more convenient?

sub_questions.content: Do you write many emails?

answers.content: Yes, I do need to write many emails, and I mainly send emails to my professor (上班族colleagues) for handing in my assignment, and for asking questions related with my major(daily work), I also need to write to my friends abroad, since facebook is banned in China, email is necessary for me to keep long-distance friendship.

sub_questions.content: How often do you write an email?

answers.content: I write emails around 3 times a week, not very frequently, although it is necessary for my study, still I prefer other types of communication tools, like Wechat, which is more entertaining, because I can also send vocal messages through it, also most of the people only check email inbox once a day probably, so sending emails is not that timely and efficient in some way.

sub_questions.content: What are the differences between emails and letters?

answers.content: Letters can convey more feelings compared with emails, when writing thank-you notes or sending a romantic message, an email really doesn’t do it. A typed letter is ok, but even better, a hand-written note/letter is so unusual that it makes it much more personal and/or romantic.

sub_questions.content: What kind(s) of email do you think is (are) the hardest to write?

answers.content: I suppose the email written to HR for job application is the hardest for me, since I have written several emails for internship application before, I had to pay close attention to the tone, as well as the wording, I have to double check before sending it also.

sub_questions.content: How do you feel when you receive emails?

answers.content: It really depends on which kind of email I would receive, if it is an email from my friends abroad, I would be very glad to hear from him/her, since there would be many photos attached in this email, but if it is just an email from my professor (business email), I guess I can’t feel a thing.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of emails do you receive that make you feel excited?

answers.content: As I said before, the emails from my friends would keep me in a good mood, also some emails that convey good news will also make me thrilled, such as emails of internship (job) offer, event invitation, and so on.

sub_questions.content: How do you communicate with others at work by email or is it more convenient?

answers.content: If the co-workers are in the same office with me, I would just speak to him/her face to face, instead of writing an email, this is more efficient. But sometimes I need to send work related emails to colleagues in our branch offices.

questions.content: Do you think people these days are as punctual as they were in the past?
How do you remind yourself to be on time?
What will you do if you are waiting for someone?
Why are some people always late?
Do you think it is important to be punctual?

sub_questions.content: Do you think people these days are as punctual as they were in the past?

answers.content: I’m afraid not. Yes, we do have solid technological tools to tell us the accurate time nowadays, but our life-space is so much faster. We are always running from one place to another. It’s inevitable to be late sometimes. However in the past, people would start to get ready long time before the appointment. So they were probably more punctual than we are now.

sub_questions.content: How do you remind yourself to be on time?

answers.content: Normally I keep it on my phone calendar if there are important events that I should remember. The alarm will ring when I should get ready for it. But to be honest with you, I always lose the track of time. So I often end up rushing towards the avenue in order to be punctual.

sub_questions.content: What will you do if you are waiting for someone?

answers.content: As a book worm, I often have a book in my bag when I go out. So when there’s an appointment and I have to wait, I’d just fish out my book to pass time. Sometimes I just browse on my phone to read the latest news, or maybe take advantage of the time to catch up with an old friend.

sub_questions.content: Why are some people always late?

answers.content: When a person always gets late, I think we could basically say that this person doesn’t value other people’s time or know how to respect people. He/she probably doesn’t even realize that punctuality is a courtesy. And when you are not even aware of it, of course, you are never on time.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important to be punctual?

answers.content: Positively yes! If you want to earn a good reputation or people’s trust, being on time is like one basic quality that you should have. Imagine someone stands up on you a few times, what kind of impression would you have of this person? So, yeah, punctuality is absolutely important.

questions.content: How do you feel when you receive a letter?
Who do you usually write to?
Do you think people will still write letters in the future?
Is it hard to think of what to write?
What do you usually write about?
Do you write many letters?
Do you prefer to write letters by hand or to use a computer?

sub_questions.content: How do you feel when you receive a letter?

answers.content: To be honest, I feel happy when receiving a letter. I think it is very nice of someone to be willing to sit down and write a letter to me. At least it means that you are special to someone so that he or she would actually write a letter to you. Therefore, I always feel pleased and I am thankful for the letters I’ve received.

sub_questions.content: Who do you usually write to?

answers.content: I write letters to my best friends. Actually, we do not communicate merely through letters. If we want to talk with each other, we just pick up the mobile phone and say “hello”. However, I will write a letter to my friends and give it to them, along with their gifts.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people will still write letters in the future?

answers.content: Well, I am not sure. Maybe fewer and fewer people in the future will choose to write letters in the future, but I think letters will still be a special part of our society. You don’t have to count on the letters to communicate with someone. However, the special feelings that letter brings to you will always remain.

sub_questions.content: Is it hard to think of what to write?

answers.content: For me, it's not. If I decide to write a letter, the words just come naturally. Today, we live in a world where everything is changing so fast. If you want to reach to someone, you can call, e-mail, send short message, or follow them on social media. So when I write a letter, I just calm down and express my true feelings.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually write about?

answers.content: When I write a letter, I write down my feelings. It’s all about how I really feel. And I usually write about my gratitude towards them for always being there for me and for backing me up. Hmm, I would also say that I will always be their side and send my best wishes.

sub_questions.content: Do you write many letters?

answers.content: Actually, I don’t quite often write letters now. In an era where communication is instantaneous, I talk with my friends, family, co-workers via short message, WeChat or Facetime. You know technology really makes communication a lot easier. However, if I want to create some special memories, I would write a letter to someone.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to write letters by hand or to use a computer?

answers.content: I prefer to write letters by hand. I actually enjoy the act of writing. When I am writing a letter, thoughts and feelings begin to flow with the ink in the nib. Well, I guess the recipient will better understand my true feelings by reading a hand-written letter than an e-mail.

questions.content: What's the best time for you to study?
What time do you like to study?
Who do you usually study with?
Do you like to study alone or with your friends?

sub_questions.content: What's the best time for you to study?

answers.content: I think the best time is from 8:00 to 11:30, on weekdays. If I study in the morning, I can remember the things for a long time and also learn it in a quick time. Usually, I don’t study during weekends. I need to combine exertion and rest. A relaxing weekend makes me energetic for next five weekdays.

sub_questions.content: What time do you like to study?

answers.content: I usually study in the morning. A quiet environment to study is important indeed. You know, the early morning is a tranquil and quiet time. As for me, my mind is fresh and clear that I am refreshed in the morning after a good night's sleep and should have just had an energy-filled breakfast that will sustain me well in to the day.

sub_questions.content: Who do you usually study with?

answers.content: I study with my classmates when a group discussion is needed. In college, teachers assign a lot of group works. Therefore, to finish the homework or a project, we have to team up. And we will book a reading room in the library in advance. Then we meet there and study together.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to study alone or with your friends?

answers.content: I like to study alone. I find it hard to stay on track when my group gets together. The conversation shifts from course material to current events. Studying independently allows me to concentrate, to pace myself and minimize the disruptions that can keep me from focusing on study. Ok, in a word, it’s easier to avoid temptations when I work alone.

questions.content: How often do you do outdoor activities?
How much time do you spend outdoors every week?
What outdoor sports do you like? (why?)
How often do you do that?
What outdoor activities do you like to do most?
Do you like outdoor activities?
What do you do in your spare time?

sub_questions.content: How often do you do outdoor activities?

answers.content: Hiking is my favorite outdoor activities and I go hiking every now and then. But the nature of hiking limits the frequency of hiking to some degree. For one thing, a destination should be chosen. Many factors should be taken into consideration during this process, like the weather, the distance and the natural environment. For another, professional equipment needs to be prepared like alpenstocks and climbing robe.

sub_questions.content: How much time do you spend outdoors every week?

answers.content: As a fanatical hiking amateur, I spend about twenty hours a week going hiking. Every weekend, I will depart with my hiking friends to the nearby destinations. All the routes are well designed by the leader and professional equipment is prepared in advance. I can really have a good time hiking during the weekend.

sub_questions.content: What outdoor sports do you like? (why?)

answers.content: There are a great variety of outdoor activities at choice. And I will try sorts of outdoor activities like jogging, horsing and golfing in my leisure time. But to tell you the truth, hiking is always my cup of tea. For one thing, fresh air taken in during hiking benefits our health. For the other, hiking is a good way of social contact. We can have a cordial talk with other hikers during the tour, which will add to the friendship.

sub_questions.content: How often do you do that?

answers.content: Hiking is my favorite outdoor activities and I go hiking biweekly. First, a destination should be chosen. Many factors should be taken into consideration during this process, like the weather, the distance and the natural environment. Second, professional equipment needs to be prepared like alpenstocks(登山杖) and climbing robe. As a result, it is not practical to go hiking weekly.

sub_questions.content: What outdoor activities do you like to do most?

answers.content: There are a wide range of outdoor activities at choice. And I will do outdoor activities like jogging, horsing and golfing, every now and then. But to tell you the truth, hiking is always my solid choice. My choice is not out of no reason. First, fresh air taken in during hiking benefits our health. Second, hiking is a good way of social contact. We can have a cordial talk with other hikers during the tour, which will add to the friendship.

sub_questions.content: Do you like outdoor activities?

answers.content: As to the topic of activities, some prefer indoor activities like yoga while others prefer outdoor ones like hiking. Given the choice, I would say that I like outdoor activities better. On the one hand, we can take in fresh air while doing outdoor activities. As a result, jogging or hiking outside benefits our health. On the other hand, participating outdoor activities is a good access to socialize. We can come across many friends who share the same hobby.

sub_questions.content: What do you do in your spare time?

answers.content: As to the activities in spare time, nothing can match hiking. If I had a vacation that was more than one day, I would call up my friends and leave for mountains in nearby cities. If the time was limited, we would choose to have a city tour. From my point of view, it is a healthy choice to go hiking in the leisure time.

questions.content: Do you use the internet (very much)?
When was the first time you used the internet?
Is the internet very important (or useful)to you?

sub_questions.content: Do you use the internet (very much)?

Sometimes, I feel I am like a cave people if I am in some place without wifi or data.

sub_questions.content: When was the first time you used the internet?

answers.content: Actually I can’t recall clearly, since it was long time ago, but I guess it was when I was around 10 years old, my family had the first computer and I used QQ chat for the first time, I felt it was very novel for me, like a magic.

sub_questions.content: Is the internet very important (or useful)to you?

sub_questions.content: Do you think you could live without the internet?

questions.content: Do you prefer electronic map or paper maps?
Do you ever ask people for directions instead of using a map?
Who taught you how to use a map?
Do you often use maps?

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer electronic map or paper maps?

answers.content: I definitely vote for electronic map which is so convenient and useful. For example, my father loves road trip. Before electric map becomes a common thing, we should read the map carefully before we set out. Sometimes, we even went the wrong way. Now, the electronic map can instruct you while you’re driving. I love it.

sub_questions.content: Do you ever ask people for directions instead of using a map?

answers.content: Only if I'm really lost. Whenever I ask for directions, I find it difficult to remember what the person said. So most of the time ，I prefer to find my own way.

sub_questions.content: Who taught you how to use a map?

answers.content: My father did it. When I was a child, a giant world map was hung on my wall. And my father used to teach me what it is and what does those images and words stand for. I used to stare at the map and wonder what does the outside world look like.

sub_questions.content: Do you often use maps?

answers.content: Yes, I often use electronic map. You know, I live in Beijing, one of the biggest cities in China, so it is impossible for me to remember all the routes, places, bus lines or subway lines. Whenever I go to somewhere I haven’t been, Google Map can tell me exactly how I can get there.

questions.content: What is the most popular animal in China?
Do you like to see animals in the zoo?
Have you ever had a pet before?
What kinds of animals do you like?
Why do some people keep pets at home?

sub_questions.content: What is the most popular animal in China?

answers.content: As to the most popular animal in China, panda is the winner without any doubt. Pandas are endangered animals, most of which live in the nature protection area in Sichuan Province. Panda is named as the symbol of China and it always plays the role of precious gifts in diplomatic activities.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to see animals in the zoo?

answers.content: To tell you the truth, I don’t fancy seeing animals in the zoo at all. From my point of view, it is merciless to prison animals in cages for they may lose their life skills as a result. As to me, animals should be released back to their habitat and human should not disturb them for a bit.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever had a pet before?

answers.content: I have always been keeping pets ever since I was a child. I had a variety of pets from dogs, cats, and birds, to rabbits, fish, even lizards. Because I was the only child in my family and there was no siblings accompanying me. So my parents give me pets as a friend as well as a family member.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of animals do you like?

answers.content: There are a wide range of animals at choice as a pet, like dogs, cats, rabbits, fish, even lizard. But given the choice, I would say that cats are my favorite. For one thing, I like the soft fur of cats. Touching a cat arouse my sense of warmness and safety. For another, a cat can be very independent. Unlike dogs, cats don’t need to be accompanied all the time. Besides, cats are agile and elegant in my mind.

sub_questions.content: Why do some people keep pets at home?

answers.content: Pets are playing an essential role in the structure of modern family. An increasing number of people are tending to keep a pet at home. For one thing, many people are living alone. A pet can eliminate their loneliness to some degree. For another, a pet arouse the sense of love and care. Many people enjoy the feeling of being needed. Third, a strong pet like a hunting dog can guard the house, which is especially beneficial to young women living alone.

questions.content: Do you read magazines?
Do younger people or older people prefer to read magazines?
Have you ever read online magazines?

sub_questions.content: Do you read magazines?

answers.content: 女生Yes, I am fond of reading magazines, especially fashion magazines, my favorite one is vogue, an American fashion magazine, I have learned some useful make-up skills from it. 男生 Actually I am actually not a big fan of magazine, maybe I prefer newspaper, which contains more useful information, but sometimes I read sports magazine like the New Sports or magazine about politics, like The New Yorker.

sub_questions.content: Do younger people or older people prefer to read magazines?

answers.content: I guess it should be younger people, since the content in magazines doesn't seem to cater to the senior community in particular, and actually seems to cater to a younger crowd. I guess my grandparents have never read magazines in their lives.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever read online magazines?

answers.content: Yes, I read online magazines, because for some magazines, like the New Sports, I can read them for free on my phone or ipad, which is more timely and money-saving, also cell phone is more portable, compared with magazines.

sub_questions.content: Did you read magazines when you were young?

answers.content: I did not have the chance to read magazines when I was young, because my parents did not allow me to do that, they thought magazines would distract me from studying, so I only had access to children’s book back then.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of magazine are popular in your country?

answers.content: It really depends, people have various tastes of magazines in China, for younger generation, we prefer to read entertainment or fashion magazines, these are very enjoyable for us, for mid aged people, they might find magazines about politics or business more entertaining.

questions.content: Do you think parents should teach children to save money?
Have you ever given money to other children?
Do parents give children pocket money in China?
Did you save money when you were young?

sub_questions.content: Do you think parents should teach children to save money?

answers.content: Yes, of course. Parents should make sure that children don’t take the money for granted. Money is earned by hard work. By saving money, children form an appropriate value of money. Saving money early means more opportunities in the future. The kids learn to save money so that they don’t waste money when they grow up.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever given money to other children?

answers.content: Yes, I have. I still remember that I lent money to my classmates when I was in primary school. Unfortunately, He did not repay me the money. I was on duty that day, so after I cleaned up the classroom, many students have left. I met a classmate at the school gate. He looked very upset. He said that he had lost the money for bus. Luckily, I did not buy snack that day, so I just gave him 5 jiao.

sub_questions.content: Do parents give children pocket money in China?

answers.content: Yes, I think so. My mother gives me pocket money every week. It’s not too much money, 15 yuan a week. She wants me to learn how to create a budget and make sensible spending plans. Well let me show you my spending plan was that: 5 yuan for my favorite comic book, 5 yuan for bus, and 5 yuan for every day’s snacks.

sub_questions.content: Did you save money when you were young?

answers.content: Yes, I did. In China, children get new year lucky money from elders during the Spring Festival. It is a huge amount of money for a child. Personally, I seldom use that money. My mom opened a bank account for me to save that money. Therefore, every year, I save my lucky money in the bank. I used that money to buy a computer when I went to college.

questions.content: What kinds of exercises are popular in your country?
Do you like extreme sports?
What was your favorite sport when you were young?
Do you think children should play sports regularly?
What kinds of exercises do you do?

sub_questions.content: What kinds of exercises are popular in your country?

answers.content: As to the most popular exercises in China, table tennis is the winner without any doubt. For table tennis is regarded as national sport in China, many children will start to play table tennis from an early age. Also, the Chinese table tennis team has always been taking the lead in the competitions which is an positive incentive for the popularization of table tennis in China.

sub_questions.content: Do you like extreme sports?

answers.content: To tell you the truth, although I enjoy the exciting feeling of watching extreme sports, I can’t collect my courage and do it myself. I would be scared to death doing extreme sports. For I have so severe acrophobia that I could never step onto a high place, let alone doing bungee jumping. Also, I think extreme sports are too dangerous to me. I am not willing to risk my life.

sub_questions.content: What was your favorite sport when you were young?

answers.content: As far as I can recall, riding bicycles was my cup of tea when I was a child. For riding a bike can break the limitation of walking speed and I could ride to everywhere. Riding bike gave me a sense of freedom and happiness. And as a result, it is still my favorite sport up till now.

sub_questions.content: Do you think children should play sports regularly?

answers.content: As far as I am concerned, it is beneficial for children to play sports regularly. First, a strong body, which is the base of everything, can be achieved through sports. Second, they can learn to cooperate and share with others during this process, and it is a crucial lesson in life.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of exercises do you do?

answers.content: Considering the limitation of time and space, yoga is always my first choice. For one thing, unlike some outdoor exercises like soccer or basketball, yoga can be done in a narrow and small space. For another, it is safer to do yoga for we don’t have to compete with others during this process. As a result, many injuries can be avoided.

questions.content: What kinds of new food have you tried recently?

sub_questions.content: What kinds of new food have you tried recently?

We tried Chicken with Dried Fruit and Shrimp Curry. Chicken with Dried Fruit is a stew cooked with an abundance of spice. But honestly, I do not like the flavor of spice, but I enjoy the curry.

sub_questions.content: Do you like any food from the countries near China?

answers.content: I'd say that Korean kimchi and Japanese sushi are always my favorite foreign food. Simply because I like eating kimchi with meat, rice and vegetables. It tastes good, and it is healthy. What makes sushi great is the simplicity of the food and complexity of the flavor. Sushi rice is prepared in a different manner from the rice that I'm used to eating. It has an almost sweet taste and sticks together.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of foreign food are popular in your country?

answers.content: In China, Korean food is popular. Ever since the hit Korean TV drama “Dae Jang Geum” was showed in China, Korean food have been popular among Chinese people. The spread of Korean culture makes Korean food popular in China. Moreover, a nice meal of Korean food doesn’t cost much.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever tried foreign food?

answers.content: Yes, of course. I have tried many kinds of foreign food, such as Korean kimchi, sushi, Thai food, and Italian spaghetti. I think that variety is the spice of life and is healthy for us. Personally I like spicy food. When eating Korean food, we keep kimchi on hand at all times since it is a nutritional essential that goes well with meals of any origin.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to try new food?

answers.content: Yes, I do. Every time I find something that tastes good, I feel excited as well. Life is too short to only have apples and peanut butter and jelly. It sometimes means I'm stuck with food not appealing to my tongue, but trying is better than not trying. It’s good to get out of your comfort zone.

questions.content: Do you know your neighbours?
Do you like your neighbours?
