sub_questions.content: Do you know your neighbours?

answers.content: Honestly speaking, I am not acquainted with my neighbors, I do not even know their names actually, I only talked with one family once who lived next door, they are couples from north part of China with a new born baby. But normally we only say “hi” to each other when we meet in corridor.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your neighbours?

answers.content: They seems nice, they were quiet all the time, I never hear loud noises coming from their apartments, also they are polite, since they would hold the door for me every time when I was about to take the elevator.

sub_questions.content: What do you think of your neighbours?

answers.content: As I mentioned before, I think they are courteous people, but since I am not acquainted with them, normally I do not think too much about them, as long as we can live peacefully and harmoniously together in the same building, this is perfectly fine for me.

sub_questions.content: How often do you see (or talk to) your neighbours?

answers.content: Not very often, around once a week, probably we have different daily routines and schedules, some may have nine to five job, some might be housewife, I meet them occasionally in the elevator or in corridor.

sub_questions.content: When do you meet your neighbours?

answers.content: Sometime I run into my neighbors in the elevator, sometimes in the corridor, like I said before, but we just nod and smile to each other without conversation, it could be awkward sometimes if we are in the same elevator without any communication.

questions.content: Do you think young people are more likely to get bored?
When you were young, what would you do if you felt bored?
What will you do when you feel bored?
What kinds of things are boring to you?

sub_questions.content: Do you think young people are more likely to get bored?

answers.content: No, I do not think so. Actually, I do not think elder people are more likely to get bored, either. It depends on who they are. A curious mind finds everything interesting, and thus avoids boredom at every turn. Wonder about the architecture around your town. How was that building designed? It has nothing to do with the age.

sub_questions.content: When you were young, what would you do if you felt bored?

answers.content: When I was a kid, every time I felt bored, I watched interesting cartoons. My parents bought me a lot of cartoon discs. You know, those vivid cartoons characters were creative, entertaining and fun to watch. They offer easy-to-catch jokes, educational but interactive messages and have a karmic ending which appeals to me.

sub_questions.content: What will you do when you feel bored?

answers.content: I have multiple choices when I feel bored. If the weather is good, I would like to stroll in a park and enjoy beautiful scenery. Sometimes, I will play mobile games. There are a lot of mobile games on the shelf. If I concentrate on the games, I won’t feel bored.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of things are boring to you?

answers.content: Lengthy speeches make me feel bored easily . I think every Chinese students has tolerated the lengthy speeches made by headmasters on different occasions. As long as there is a ceremony, the headmaster is going to give us a lecture. Actually, nobody is listening to him or her. We all wanted the speech to be finished quickly.

questions.content: What is your favorite restaurant?
Do you like to eat at home or outside?
Do you often eat out?
Who do you often have meals with?
When do you have meals?

sub_questions.content: What is your favorite restaurant?

answers.content: My favorite restaurant is a small seafood restaurant near our school. The food there is of a good quality and well prepared. And the waiting staff is always friendly, polite and helpful. What I mean to say is that they will advise you on any specials on offer, and they tend to any need you may have swiftly and efficiently. Therefore, I never feel embarrassed to invite my families, friends, or foreigners there for a meal.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to eat at home or outside?

answers.content: I prefer to eat out. Maybe just because that I am not a qualified cook， I can’t cook delicious food like that in restaurants by myself. But for the food safety problem, which the media always mentions, now I am studying cooking and trying to eat out less.

sub_questions.content: Do you often eat out?

answers.content: Yes, about twice a week I guess. Although eating out costs much than eating at home, it saves me a lot of time. When I am too tired to cook by myself, I would choose to eat out. There are so many choices, so sometimes it also takes me time to decide what to eat.

sub_questions.content: Who do you often have meals with?

answers.content: I often have meals with my classmates. After the class we go to the cafeteria together. When we are having meals, we also enjoy chatting and gossiping. That is a good way for us to relax. And at weekends I prefer to have dinner with my parents. Since we are busy with working on weekdays, the dinner gives us the chance to talk with each other freely.

sub_questions.content: When do you have meals?

answers.content: I have breakfast in the morning at about 7 o’clock before I go to school. The lunchtime in the cafeteria is about 12 o’clock. I am used to having dinner later, at about 8 o’clock, since I always go to bed very late. If I feel hungry before the dinner, I would eat some fruit or chocolate.

questions.content: Who is your favorite pop star?
Do you want to be a pop star?
Do you like to go to concerts?
Do you prefer live music or recorded music?

sub_questions.content: Who is your favorite pop star?

answers.content: There are so many pop stars that I admire, if I have to choose one, I guess it would be Tailor Swift, she is a very talented American singer and songwriter, famous for her narrative songs about her personal life, her “love story” and “you belong with me” are my favorite ones.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to be a pop star?

answers.content: I dreamed of being a pop star when I was a little boy/girl, but I do not want to be a celebrity now, because I can imagine the stress shouldered by these pop stars, also I do not want be judged by the public, private space and freedom are very significant for me.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to go to concerts?

answers.content: As a music lover, concerts are one of my biggest indulgence and have been for a long time. Because there’s an indescribable feeling that comes from the interactions between the performer and the audiences, also it’s a moment in time that can never be replicated: musicians are performing live on stage.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer live music or recorded music?

answers.content: Although I love the performance in live music, still I’m in the recorded camp. Because recorded music is massively edited, tweaked and perfected in various ways; also sometimes the singer might even forget the lyrics when he/she is performing on the stage.

questions.content: Would you like to have more free time in the future?
Which day do you have more free time on, Saturday or Sunday?
Do you often have free time?
What do you and your family like to do in spare time?
Do you think you will spend more time with your family?
Do you like to spend time with your family?
What do you usually do when you are free?

sub_questions.content: Would you like to have more free time in the future?

answers.content: Yes, I would definitely appreciate having more free time in the future. Having extra time would allow me to explore hobbies and activities that I am passionate about, such as painting and hiking. It would also provide me with the opportunity to spend quality time with my loved ones and enhance my overall well-being. More free time would undoubtedly contribute to a better work-life balance and promote self-care, ensuring a more fulfilling and rewarding lifestyle.

Absolutely, having more free time in the future would be fantastic as it would allow me to pursue my hobbies and interests more actively. Engaging in activities like hiking, playing musical instruments, and spending quality time with loved ones can enhance my well-being. Additionally, having extra time could also provide opportunities for further learning or relaxation, thereby contributing to a balanced and fulfilling life.

sub_questions.content: Which day do you have more free time on, Saturday or Sunday?

answers.content: On Saturdays, I typically have more free time to relax and enjoy various activities. I often use this day to catch up on reading, baking, or spending time outdoors. Sundays, on the other hand, I tend to devote to self-care, meditation, or meeting with friends. Overall, I find both days equally important for balancing work and leisure activities in my schedule.

I find myself having more leisure time on Sundays compared to Saturdays. On Sundays, I usually enjoy sleeping in, having a leisurely breakfast, and then spending time outdoors playing sports or hiking. It's a day when I can relax and recharge for the upcoming week. Conversely, Saturdays tend to be busier with errands and social commitments, leaving me with less free time to unwind.

sub_questions.content: Do you often have free  time?

answers.content: As a person with a busy schedule, I treasure the limited free time I have. Balancing work and personal life can be challenging, but I make it a priority to indulge in my hobbies during my spare moments. I enjoy reading novels, exploring new cafes, and practicing yoga to unwind and recharge. Having some free time allows me to rejuvenate and focus better on my responsibilities.

As a young professional, my schedule is fairly packed with work commitments and personal responsibilities. However, I do make it a point to carve out some free time for activities that I enjoy. In my case, I find relaxation in playing the guitar or engaging in outdoor activities like hiking. These moments of leisure not only help me unwind but also provide a refreshing break from my routine.

sub_questions.content: What do you and your family like to do in spare time?

answers.content: We will go for a walk in the park together. Because there is a beautiful wetland part nearby a lake in my hometown. So strolling after dinner is like a great family activity for us. By the way, the scenery is beautiful and we can chat while walking.

sub_questions.content: Do you think you will spend more time with your family?

answers.content: I would like to spend more time with my family. Having quality time with family is important for everybody. However, as I grow up, I spend less and less time with my parents. My college is in a city far away from my hometown. I can only go home during summer and winter vacations.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to spend time with your family?

answers.content: Of course, I do. Nowhere is better than home, right? Since I am the only child in my family and I have a very close relationship with my parents. I always feel safe and warm when I am with my family. when I am with my family, I feel that I am complete.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do when you are free?

answers.content: During my free time, as a woman, I enjoy unwinding by reading novels, practicing yoga, and exploring new recipes to cook. I find pleasure in taking long walks in nature as it helps me relax and clear my mind. Occasionally, I also like to meet with friends for coffee or watch movies to entertain myself. These activities help me strike a balance between relaxation and personal growth.

As a crazy traveler, I want to travel when I have a long holiday. You know it's fun to explore the nature and the world. It’s not unusual to become a bit bored of everyday life. The daily routine: bills to pay; shopping to do; a big project at work; meals to cook; clothes to wash …I look forward to some ‘time out’ each year. It’s a time to switch off from everything back home and to leave our worries behind.

questions.content: Why do people who live in cities like public gardens?
What do you think are the benefits of having gardens in the city?
Do you think there are enough public gardens in your hometown?
Are there many public gardens in China?
Are public gardens very important in China?

sub_questions.content: Why do people who live in cities like public gardens?

answers.content: The living pace of modern people in cities is faster and faster. When people want to relax, the public gardens would be the first choice of most people, because the gardens offer them the place to slow down. People can get close to nature there. Besides, the free ticket of public gardens is also an advantage.

sub_questions.content: What do you think are the benefits of having gardens in the city?

answers.content: Firstly, gardens provide people the place to relax and take some exercise. Secondly, the trees and plants in the gardens make the air in the city fresher. Thirdly, as attractive scenery of cities, gardens help the city to be prettier. Because of these benefits, more and more gardens are built in cities.

sub_questions.content: Do you think there are enough public gardens in your hometown?

answers.content: Yes, I think so. In Shanghai, there are more than one hundred public gardens in different sizes. I have visited some of them. It is not crowded and all the public facilities are well protected. So I think the amount of public gardens in Shanghai can satisfy the need of people.

sub_questions.content: Are there many public gardens in China?

answers.content: Yes, almost in every city. Talking about Shanghai as an example, there are more than 100 public gardens in Shanghai. And since so many public gardens are so well decorated and organized, they become famous tourist interests. I have also visited several public gardens when I traveled. I think they helped me to know more about lives of local people.

sub_questions.content: Are public gardens very important in China?

answers.content: Yes, of course. By the fact that there are parks in almost every city in China, we can know the importance of parks. I think a park is like an oasis where people can get away from the hustle and bustle of city life. They are good places for people to have picnics, to take some exercise, or to forget about work for a while.

questions.content: How do you remind yourself to be on time?
Why are some people always late?
Do you think it is important to be punctual?
What will you do if you are waiting for someone?
Do you think people these days are as punctual as they were in the past?

sub_questions.content: How do you remind yourself to be on time?

answers.content: Generally I put a reminder in my phone, it reminds me about the upcoming event 1 hour in advance, so thanks to this useful tool, I have always been punctual, also I keep memos on my phone about my schedules, and I check my memos several times a day.

sub_questions.content: Why are some people always late?

answers.content: It might because these late people are a little big inconsiderate, they don’t take enough care over other person, so they keep other people waiting habitually. Also some girls tend to be late frequently, probably because they think being late is girls’ priority.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important to be punctual?

answers.content: Absolutely yes, being punctual means you respect others and you cherish their time. Sometimes being late can even cause intense friction between the two people. Also, in some occasions, if you are late, you might lose or miss some valuable opportunities, like in a job interview or an important meeting.

sub_questions.content: What will you do if you are waiting for someone?

answers.content: Unluckily, most of my close friends are late persons, they always keep me waiting, so I get used to it and normally I would download a movie or an e-book before our meeting, it is a really good way to kill time for me while waiting.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people these days are as punctual as they were in the past?

answers.content: Frankly speaking, as a young people, I do not know much about the habit of people in the past, but I guess probably people in the past are more punctual, because I have noticed that my grandpa, as well as my professor, who is in his late 60s, are always on time in any appointment, it is definitely not the same case for younger generations.

Would you like to have your own boat?
Is boat trip popular in your country

sub_questions.content: Have you ever travelled by boat

answers.content: Yes, I remember it was long time ago, when I was only 10 or 11 years old. I took a boat to Hangzhou during a family trip. It was just a small-sized boat, I was very excited because that was my first time to travel by boat, but I got sea sick and even vomited.

sub_questions.content: Do you like boats?

answers.content: Well, to be honest, now I am not a big fan of boat, I mean I was really into it when I was a little boy/girl, but now for me, ferry is more entertaining and intriguing, maybe boat is less exciting and even a little bit childish.

sub_questions.content: Would you like to have your own boat?

answers.content: Having my own boat sounds like a nice idea, but this is probably not on my wish list, since as I mentioned before, I fancy ferry, or maybe ship, I hope I can get my own ferry in the future and be a captain like Jack Sparrow in the movie Pirates of the Caribbean.

sub_questions.content: Is boat trip popular in your country

answers.content: It depends, in south part of China, the answer would be yes, since there are many coastal cities there, like Hangzhou and Xiamen, but it is not the same case in northern China, such as Gansu province and Shanxi province, there are not many boats available in these places due to water shortage.

questions.content: Do you remember your friends in primary school? Why?
What do you do with friends?
How do you make friends?
Do you have many friends
Do you like chatting with friends online?

sub_questions.content: Do you remember your friends in primary school? Why?

answers.content: Well, it is a shame that I’ve forgotten most of them, since I have not seen them for almost 10 years, maybe I can recognize them if we have the chance to meet. But I still keep in touch with 2or3 friends in primary school, since we are living in the same city currently.

sub_questions.content: What do you do with friends?

answers.content: There are so many ways for us to have fun together, sometimes, we spend time in library, it may sounds a little bit nerdy, because my friends and I are all big fans of reading, so would go to the library together, read novels and drink coffee there. At Friday night, we would play pool or grab a bottle of beer in the pub.

sub_questions.content: How do you make friends?

answers.content: I make new friends in some activities, like in dancing club or sports team, because this is a great way for me to find other people who have common interests, we can talk a lot about the things we both love, like jazz music or sports stars.

sub_questions.content: Do you have many friends

answers.content: Yes, I have many friends, but only a few close friends, the circle of my friends are getting smaller as we move on in life, and we tend to grow up in separate ways. I have like 5 or 6 reliable constant friends now, we can share happiness and sorrow with each other, they equal to a world to me.

sub_questions.content: Do you like chatting with friends online?

answers.content: Yes, I like chatting with friends online, through Wechat or QQ, popular communication tools in China, because I can formulate my thoughts when we are texting each other, also I keep all the chatting records, these are valuable memory for me.

questions.content: How often do you meet with your family?
How do you spend the time with your family
Do you want to live with your family in the future?
Are you close to all of your family members?
How has your family influenced you?

sub_questions.content: How often do you meet with your family?

answers.content: I haven’t been meeting with my family very often after attending university, since I am studying in Shenzhen, which is quite far away from XX, I only come back home twice a year during summer and winter holidays, so I really cherish the time spent with my parents.

sub_questions.content: How do you spend the time with your family

answers.content: We love to spend time playing outdoors together. We camp, we swim, we ride, we run. We jump in muddy puddles, we play games and we garden. It keeps us strong and healthy in body and mind. Doing it all together makes it easy and fun, happy time with my family.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to live with your family in the future?

answers.content: Although it would be lovely to live with my family, I would not choose to do that, I believe that I can be more independent by living on my own, instead of relying on my parents, also I will study abroad soon in UK, and I plan to stay abroad for several years to explore more foreign culture.

sub_questions.content: Are you close to all of your family members?

answers.content: I am close to most of them, but not all of them, because I have a big family with many uncles, aunts, cousins, and so on, so I even haven’t met some of them.

sub_questions.content: How has your family influenced you?

answers.content: My family has influenced me greatly, in terms of my personality, my parents are very outgoing and hospitable, when I was young, they always invited my friends and classmates to my place for dinner, that’s why I am very sociable now, I love making new friends.

questions.content: Have you had full-time or part-time job before?
What kinds of jobs are popular in China?
What do you plan to do in the future?
Have you changed your mind on your dream job?
What was the dream job for you when you were young?

sub_questions.content: Have you had full-time or part-time job before?

answers.content: Yea I tried some part-time job in my sophomore year. I’m pretty good at writing, you see, so I sometimes write articles and hint fictions for the magazines and websites. I’m in no way famous, so it’s not actually that serious as a job. Oh I think I made myself sound like a freelancer this way, didn’t I? Ha-ha!

sub_questions.content: What kinds of jobs are popular in China?

answers.content: I think the answer will be “civil servants”. If you managed to pass the exam and get into the government, then all your family members and friend would be happy for you. Cuz in China, being a civil servant means decent payment, high social status, and impeccable healthcare. I myself, however, dislike the kind of life where you can easily see what you can become in your 40s.

sub_questions.content: What do you plan to do in the future?

answers.content: Um…I’m still in college now, but if fact I’m try to become an economist in the future, like analysing financial trends and stuff. I’m quite into figures, and I’m pretty good at playing with them, I guess that could be a pro for my career plan. I hope that I can manage to get an internship from some great companies, so that I can learn and improve myself.

sub_questions.content: Have you changed your mind on your dream job?

answers.content: Oh um definitely. Actually it’s not very hard to find out that even if you own a computer company, it doesn’t necessarily mean that you can always get all the edge devices and games for free. It’s a business, you see, so it’s more like cost-benefit balance and stuff. Just like the case of shopkeeper, I have plenty of friend who dreamt to be shopkeepers, you know, they thought they could just eat all the jellies and ice creams for free, which is, unfortunately, not the truth.

sub_questions.content: What was the dream job for you when you were young?

answers.content: I used to dream to be a computer company owner, cuz I was a complete computer aficionado. And you know, in a child's eyes, the boss of a computer company should be able to possess all kinds of top- rank work stations, and should be able to play all those fantastic games. I suppose that’s why I had this dream.

questions.content: Do you like your high school?
Which subject you like most? And dislike?
Do you still contact with your friends in high school?
What is your high school like?
What happened on your first day of high school?

sub_questions.content: Do you like your high school?

answers.content: I love my high school, I miss it. Because it not only taught me knowledge but also gave me an unforgettable memory. The teachers in high school cared much about our students both in study and life, and classmates were always friendly to each other. We built deep friendship together.

sub_questions.content: Which subject you like most? And dislike?

answers.content: My favorite subject in high school was English actually, since I was really into American movies and tv series, I had a dream in high school that one day I would be able to speak to foreigners fluently like native speakers in those movies. So I put lots of efforts in English and I got really good grade. Speaking of the subject that I like least, it must be history, those ancient stories were really tiresome for me.

sub_questions.content: Do you still contact with your friends in high school?

answers.content: Yes, I have been keeping in touch with several friends in my high school, but not all of them, we keep each other posted about our lives, and we plan to get together to have a reunion in this coming winter vacation.

sub_questions.content: What is your high school like?

answers.content: My high school has several large buildings and enough space for about 2, 500 students. Every student is given a locker. When students first arrive at school, they go straight to their lockers to put away or get their textbooks and to hang up their outdoor clothes.

sub_questions.content: What happened on your first day of high school?

answers.content: I will never forget that day, we had military training on the first day of high school, since it is a tradition in China to have military training at the beginning of high school, as well as University, it was baking hot outside and we did lots of exercise, I guess the principle wanted us to have strong bodies and will.

questions.content: Do you like (to learn about) history?
What historical event do you find most interesting?
What historical event do you think was most important?
Do you think history is important?
Do you like to watch programs on TV about history?
How do you get information about history?

sub_questions.content: Do you like (to learn about) history?

answers.content: Yes, I do. Actually when I was in senior high school, every student had to choose one extra subject to take College Entrance Examination. And my choice was history. So you can see, I like history since I was in senior high school. I also like reading some books about history.

sub_questions.content: What historical event do you find most interesting?

answers.content: As far as I am concerned, I think the most interesting event is the invention of paper in Han Dynasty, before which we used bamboo or wood to record information. While after the invention of it, we are able to convey message and record history event more conveniently through paper.

sub_questions.content: What historical event do you think was most important?

answers.content: I think it will be the World War II, during which a whole new world was totally established. And most important, the United Nations was founded after that, meaning the world would be in order and safety.

sub_questions.content: Do you think history is important?

answers.content: Yes, exactly. In Chinese there is a proverb that, through the history you can know about the future. We can learn a lot from the history and thus correct our own mistakes in case they will happen again.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to watch programs on TV about history?

answers.content: Of course I like it. Compared with books, which maybe mundane, I prefer TV programs such as some documentaries, which will contain a lot of videos and images, bringing me back to the time when the event took place.

sub_questions.content: How do you get information about history?

answers.content: I frequently read books and documentaries shown on TV to get information about history, especially those about the economy or civilizations. That is where most of us got our information.

questions.content: Does it rain much in China?(Where?When)
Is there any part of China where it doesn’t rain much?
When (in which month/season)does it rain most in your hometown?
In which season does it rain most in other parts of China?
Can you remember any time when it rained particularly heavily in your hometown?

sub_questions.content: Does it rain much in China?(Where?When)

answers.content: It depends on different parts of China, it rains frequently in some coastal cities, like Xiamen and Shenzhen, residents may even suffer flood due to the heavy rain, but in northern China, such as Shanxi Province, it is quite dry with less rain during the year.

sub_questions.content: Is there any part of China where it doesn’t rain much?

answers.content: Yes, as I said before, in north part of China, especially those cites far away from the ocean, like Xian and Gansu, the lack of rain may cause drought occasionally, so Chinese government has been trying to deliver water from the south to the north these years.

sub_questions.content: When (in which month/season)does it rain most in your hometown?

answers.content: My hometown is located in northeast part of China, it is quite dry there with during most of the time of a year, i is a little bit humid with more rain, some fruits like grapes, can’t be planted in my hometown due to the dry weather.

sub_questions.content: In which season does it rain most in other parts of China?

answers.content: I suppose it rains most during summer, around July or August, so there might be flood occasionally in some parts of China due to the heavy rain, for example, the flood damaged many villages in Guangxi province in last summer, many people became homeless, this was really a disaster.

sub_questions.content: Can you remember any time when it rained particularly heavily in your hometown?

answers.content: Yes, although it has rarely happened, I remember that when I was a high school student, once I was on my home after class, it was showering suddenly, thundershower, and I was totally drenched, but it only lasted for no more than 10 minutes before the rain stopped.

questions.content: Do you like to spend time with teenagers
Do you know anything about the kind of fashion that teenagers like?
What are the best things of being a teenager?
How do teenagers entertain themselves?

sub_questions.content: Do you like to spend time with teenagers

answers.content: To be honest, I do not like spending time with teenagers, since most of the teenagers that I’ve met are quite rebellious and impolite, they like fancy dresses and they even swear a lot on the street, also for me, they might be a little bit immature, so it would be hard for me to find some common topics that I can talk with them.

sub_questions.content: Do you know anything about the kind of fashion that teenagers like?

answers.content: I guess fashion for teenagers is the result of the desire to be like a celebrity. They would buy some cool shirts that they saw their idols wore on TV, celebrities are perhaps the greatest influences on teenagers in the modern world, and they can have a huge impact on a teen's ideas about fashion and its importance.

sub_questions.content: What are the best things of being a teenager?

answers.content: Being a teenager is stress free, they do not need to worry about taxes or pay the bills of electricity and your home, parents do that, they are carefree. Also they are innocent and they have many expectations and dreams of their lives, but many adults have fears after difficulties in their lives. .

sub_questions.content: How do teenagers entertain themselves?

answers.content: I guess visiting malls, watching movies and hanging out with friends have become the favorite pastime for teenagers. Take my cousin as an example, she is a 16-year-old Chinese girl, she enjoys spending time with her friends in cinema or in shopping malls.

questions.content: Do you do housework at home?
Do you like to do housework? Why?
What kinds of housework do you do?
What kinds of housework you dislike to do?
Did you help your parents to do housework when you were young?
Do you think children should do housework?
Do you think men and women should share housework?

sub_questions.content: Do you do housework at home?

answers.content: Yes, I do. I am not a big fan of housework, but since I live by myself. I have to do housework at least once a week.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to do housework? Why?

answers.content: No, I don’t like to do housework at all. Cleaning the floor and doing laundry are always tiring. It takes a lot of time and energy. I will only do housework when I have to.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of housework do you do?

answers.content: I do all the housework I can do by myself. For example, I will sweep the floor and mop it at least once a week. I will do laundry whenever my basket is full. Also I will wash the dishes after I finish a meal.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of housework you dislike to do?

answers.content: Frankly speaking I dislike all kinds of housework! If I have to choose one of the most, I would have to say cleaning the dishes after meal. I like cooking a lot and I usually use different plates and bowls to prepare for the cooking. So after I cook, there will be various kitchenware waiting for me to wash. It is annoying sometimes.

sub_questions.content: Did you help your parents to do housework when you were young?

answers.content: When I was little, I used to be assigned to do different kinds of housework by my mother. Since both of my parents were busy working every day, they would ask me to do some of the housework. For example, my mum would ask me to go grocery shopping after school and then help her with cooking. Also, I had to wash my plate after meal and wash my underwear every day.

sub_questions.content: Do you think children should do housework?

answers.content: Yes, I strongly believe so. From my own experience of doing housework, I learnt a lot as a child. I learned more about how to live alone and solve all kinds of problems in life. Thanks to this experience, I am now living by myself and my parents won’t be worried about me at all. I think it is pathetic for some young people who do not even know how to wash their own clothes.

sub_questions.content: Do you think men and women should share housework?

answers.content: Of course yes. It is not about the gender. Everyone should do their part of housework at home. In China, in most of the families, women work outside as much as the men. It is unfair to put the burden of doing housework only on the shoulder of one person no matter men and women.

questions.content: What do you think is the purpose of advertisements?
Are there many advertisements in your country?
Why do you think there are so many advertisements now?
What are the various places where we see advertisements?
How do you feel about advertisements?
What kind of advertisement do you like the most?
Do advertisements (ever) influence your choice about what to buy?
Where can we see advertisements?
Do you like advertisements on TV?
Do you prefer advertisements on TV or those in magazines?
Do you think advertising plays a very important role in today's world?
What sorts of advertisements leave the deepest impression on people?
What do you think about the developments in advertising in China today?
Why do you think companies advertise themselves (or, their products)?

sub_questions.content: What do you think is the purpose of advertisements?

answers.content: I think the advertisements aim mainly at publicizing the use of the products and attracting customers. For example, a company wants to improve the sales of their product, so they decide to make an advertisement. They may hire a superstar as their spokesperson or write an interesting song for the product. After being broadcast, the advertisement could attract more customers to buy the product.

sub_questions.content: Are there many advertisements in your country?

answers.content: Yes. With commercialism running rampant in today's china, we can see advertisements almost everywhere we go, the posters on which shining stars are promoting exquisite products, the flyers with appealing models of products, you just name it.

sub_questions.content: Why do you think there are so many advertisements now?

answers.content: I think the emergence of all these advertisements is attributable to mainly two reasons: an increase of people's purchasing power and an increasingly wider range of products. Advertisers have to employ every means they are capable of to make their products stand out, to attract those potential buyers.

sub_questions.content: What are the various places where we see advertisements?

answers.content: It seems advertisements are able to insinuate themselves into every nook and cranny of our life. When you walk on the street, you may get loads of flyers. When you go to canteen, you may see large posters. When you get on the bus, you may see banners on the bus. It is true that advertisements are almost everywhere.

sub_questions.content: How do you feel about advertisements?

answers.content: It's a mixed feeling. When you see elaborated designed advertisements, it's really an enjoyment. Besides, they can help you to decide which products to buy. However, when your life is inundated with advertisements, sometimes you will get tired of them and wish to get rid of them.

sub_questions.content: What kind of advertisement do you like the most?

answers.content: I like advertisements about food the most. I like to see different kinds of foods with different colors advertised on the screen or in the posters. I think such advertisements are really a feast to the eye. They add spices to my daily routine life and make me believe that life is full of beauty.

sub_questions.content: Do advertisements (ever) influence your choice about what to buy?

answers.content: Yes. I think I'm liable to be influenced by what is called celebrity effect. I still remember that once I went to a supermarket to buy a bottle of juice. The moment I saw my favorite star's face on the label, I picked that bottle without hesitation, thinking that if she liked drinking it, it must be the best.

sub_questions.content: Where can we see advertisements?

answers.content: In the past, advertisements were mainly shown on TV. But now you can see advertisements almost everywhere. There are large posters in the subway station and the shopping mall. Besides, you can see different kinds of advertisements when you are surfing the internet.

sub_questions.content: Do you like advertisements on TV?

answers.content: Not really. Because when I'm watching a program or a movie, I don't like to be interrupted by advertisements. Just imagine how annoying it is when you are coming to the end of a movie when suddenly an advertisement pops up. You know, I would not turn on the TV to specifically watch advertisements.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer advertisements on TV or those in magazines?

answers.content: I prefer those in magazines. I think magazines offer a wider variety of advertisements and they are more exquisite and elaborately designed. It is very easy for me to be attracted by those shining pictures coupled with neat descriptions.

sub_questions.content: Do you think advertising plays a very important role in today's world?

answers.content: Yes. Sometimes I think advertising is a kind of art and it makes the world more colorful. Different products are like different arts. Besides, to some degree, it is of some help for us to distinguish poor quality products between good quality products.

sub_questions.content: What sorts of advertisements leave the deepest impression on people?

answers.content: I think it is the public service advertisement. Those advertisements are so moving that they are likely to leave an indelible impression on you. Usually the topic of such advertisements is love and you know how powerful love is and how easily it will move people to tears.

sub_questions.content: What do you think about the developments in advertising in China today?

answers.content: To some degree, I think the developments in advertising should be put under some control. It is obvious that advertising in China runs so rampant that it has already exercised a negative influence on our daily life. Too many advertisements are really a nuisance and a disturbance.

sub_questions.content: Why do you think companies advertise themselves (or, their products)?

answers.content: I think fierce competition in the market has compelled every company to resort to advertisements. Since buyers have a lot of choices, companies have to think out a way to convince buyers to trust their products or services, to tell buyers why their products or services are most suitable to them.

questions.content: What part of your day do you like best?
What’s your daily routine?
What is the busiest part of the day for you?
What’s the difference of routine between you and your teenager’s times?

sub_questions.content: What part of your day do you like best?

answers.content: I like the part before going to bed. Not only because it is quite quiet and I enjoy staying alone but also because I like reading and that is almost the only time I have got that enables me to read.

sub_questions.content: What’s your daily routine?

answers.content: Usually I will get up at 6 and do some jogging for some while before breakfast and then go to school. At night, my routine is to read some books or listen to some music before going to bed.

sub_questions.content: What is the busiest part of the day for you?

answers.content: I guess it will be the afternoon, after my lunch. There will be a lot of class to attend and some of them are rather far away, which means I have to walk hurriedly to go to different classrooms one after another.

sub_questions.content: What’s the difference of routine between you and your teenager’s times?

answers.content: As for teenager’s time, I was always busy doing my homework, going to school. It was quite monotonous and boring. While now I have more time to dominate all by myself and do something I really like.

questions.content: Do you have a mirror at home?
Do you use the mirror every day?
How often do you look at yourself in the mirror every day?
Can mirrors be decorations at home?
Have you ever bought clothes without looking in the mirror?
Will you put many mirrors at home?
Do you think mirror is necessary?
Have you ever bought mirrors?
Do you like to buy mirrors?

sub_questions.content: Do you have a mirror at home?

answers.content: Yes, I do. There are several mirrors in my apartment: one on the wall of my bathroom, one in my wardrobe and also a small one on my desk. My family and I use them every day to check if we look OK.

sub_questions.content: Do you use the mirror every day?

answers.content: Absolutely. Like I mentioned before, I use mirror mainly to check if I look nice. Also, before I go out for work, I would like to look into the mirror and check if I have everything with me.

sub_questions.content: How often do you look at yourself in the mirror every day?

answers.content: Actually, I love to check how I look in the mirror. That is to say, every time when I see a mirror, I will look at myself in it. So it is hard to count. Sometimes, I even use my cellphone as a mirror to see if I look good.

sub_questions.content: Can mirrors be decorations at home?

answers.content: Sure, mirrors can be decorations at home. If we live in an apartment that is not big enough, it is a good way to put big mirrors on the wall so that the room will seem to be bigger. However, I heard from my grandma, that in China, it was a taboo to put a mirror at the end of our bed.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever bought clothes without looking in the mirror?

answers.content: Frankly speaking, yes. I always buy clothes without looking in the mirror. Since I am busy with working/studying now, I seldom have time to go shopping in the shopping malls. Most of my clothes are bought from the internet. You know, I cannot try it on unless I buy it and receive it at home.

sub_questions.content: Will you put many mirrors at home?

answers.content: Well, I guess yes, but not so many, maybe 4 to 5 will be enough. Let’s see: one in the bathroom, one in the wardrobe, one in the hallway of the entrance and one on my dresser. I can use them whenever I am in need.

sub_questions.content: Do you think mirror is necessary?

answers.content: Yes, definitely. The appearance of a person matters a lot and mirrors help us to make sure that we are in the appropriate look. Also, if there are some skin problems that we cannot see directly with our eyes, like acnes on the face or back, mirrors are necessary for help.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever bought mirrors?

answers.content: Let me see, yes. When I first entered the university/high school, I lived with 3 other girls in a small dorm. There was no mirror in that dorm so I bought a small one and put it on my desk. Also, I have bought a mirror as a souvenir when I travelled to Japan two years ago.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to buy mirrors?

answers.content: Well, it is hard to say if I like it or not. I buy mirrors only when I need to. And sometimes, if I see some pretty and exquisite mirror, I would love to buy it and give it as a gift to my friend. But for myself, I only buy them when I have to use them.

questions.content: Who teach you to be polite
How do Chinese people show politeness
What rules of politeness change in China?

sub_questions.content: Who teach you to be polite

answers.content: My parents first and then I was taught to be polite in primary school. They told me how to behave myself in front of strangers and how to express my thankfulness to other people.

sub_questions.content: How do Chinese people show politeness

answers.content: Basically, when we need help we will say ‘excuse me’ and when we get help we will say ‘thank you’. Moreover, Chinese people like to shake hands with others to express their politeness.

sub_questions.content: What rules of politeness change in China?

answers.content: When we meet someone for the first time in the past, we would take a bow and even bow low to show our politeness and respect to the one we meet with. Now the things have changed in China, to show our politeness, we would like to shake our hands and nod our heads.

questions.content: Do you like sunshine?
Do you like a place full of sunshine?
Are there any technologies with sun in nowadays? For example?
Do you like sunny days
What do you like to do when it’s sunny days?
Are there many sunny days in our hometown?

sub_questions.content: Do you like sunshine?

answers.content: Yes, I do. I love sunny days and I enjoy the sunshine. Some others may think sunshine will get them tanned while I think I will get warmth and I hope I can see sunshine every day.

sub_questions.content: Do you like a place full of sunshine?

answers.content: Yes exactly! I love going to the beach and of course I will use sunblock in advance in case I get too tanned and get hurt by sunshine.

sub_questions.content: Are there any technologies with sun in nowadays? For example?

answers.content: The one I would like to mention is solar system. The solar energy has been widely used in many fields; the most famous one is the electricity generation. It is also environmental-friendly.

sub_questions.content: Do you like sunny days

answers.content: Yes, I like sunny days. While there cannot be too many sunny days because it will be too hot. Generally, I prefer sunny days to other days.

sub_questions.content: What do you like to do when it’s sunny days?

answers.content: I like to go to some outside coffee bars and order some coffee. And I will also enjoy reading my favourite books, staying there the whole afternoon.

sub_questions.content: Are there many sunny days in our hometown?

answers.content: As far as I am concerned, there are different kinds of weather in my hometown and the most common one is sunny day, especially in autumn and winter.

questions.content: What kinds of teachers do you like best?
Who was your favorite teacher when you were young?
Would you want to be a teacher in the future?
Have you ever had bad teachers before?

sub_questions.content: What kinds of teachers do you like best?

answers.content: There are all kind of teachers in my life. But I can tell you that I like the easy-going teachers the most, just like everyone else. The reason is that easy-going teachers wouldn’t do anything to restrict your imagination. And imagination is the secret of leading a funny life.

sub_questions.content: Who was your favorite teacher when you were young?

answers.content: In my primary school, I met about 10 teachers. My favorite one was a female teacher who taught me Chinese. I totally forgot her name, but in her class, I developed a huge interest into Chinese. I am really grateful to have her in my life.

sub_questions.content: Would you want to be a teacher in the future?

answers.content: Such thing is already on my to do list. Probably you don't know it. But in China, teacher is well-respected job. Although they do not get a good salary, people look up to them. And since China is getting more international, I want to become an English teacher if possible.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever had bad teachers before?

answers.content: Well, that's the part of my memory that I really don't want to bring up. I had a really bad teacher in my middle school time. He was really strict to the students. And whenever my classmates made any mistakes, no matter how tiny they were, he would carry out severe punishment. He was a nightmare in my life.

Is growing vegetables popular in your country?

sub_questions.content: Should schools teach students how to grow vegetable?

answers.content: Absolutely, teaching students how to grow vegetables in schools is an invaluable idea. Not only does it promote a sustainable and healthy lifestyle, but it also instills a sense of responsibility and connection to nature. By engaging in practical activities like gardening, students can learn important life skills, appreciate the environment, and even develop a passion for agriculture. Overall, this hands-on experience can truly enrich their educational journey.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it’s easy to grow vegetables?

answers.content: Yes, I believe that growing vegetables can be both challenging and rewarding at the same time. Cultivating plants requires patience, dedication, and a good understanding of horticulture. It involves tasks such as preparing the soil, maintaining proper watering schedules, and protecting the plants from pests and diseases. Overall, the process of growing vegetables can be fulfilling as it allows us to be more connected to nature and enjoy the satisfaction of harvesting our own fresh produce.

sub_questions.content: Do many people grow vegetables in your city?

answers.content: Yes, in my city, a significant number of residents are keen on growing vegetables at home. It's a trend that has gained popularity due to the desire for fresh and organic produce. Many households have dedicated spaces like balconies or small gardens to cultivate plants such as tomatoes, herbs, and lettuce. This practice promotes sustainability and healthy eating habits among urban dwellers.

sub_questions.content: Is growing vegetables popular in your country?

answers.content: Absolutely! In my country, the trend of growing vegetables has gained significant momentum in recent years. With a rising awareness towards sustainable living, many urban dwellers have started cultivating their own produce, even in limited spaces like balconies or rooftops. This not only promotes healthy eating habits but also reduces carbon footprint by minimizing food miles. Consequently, it has become quite popular among the environmentally conscious population.

sub_questions.content: Are you interested in growing vegetables and fruits?

answers.content: I find the idea of cultivating vegetables and fruits quite appealing. The thought of nurturing plants and witnessing the gradual process of growth is fascinating to me. Not only does it provide a sense of accomplishment, but it also connects us to nature in a meaningful way. Gardening can be a therapeutic and rewarding experience that allows us to appreciate the wonders of the natural world.

sub_questions.content: What kind of fruit do you like?Why?

answers.content: I like those fruits which are juicy and sweet, such as oranges, mangoes. Because when I am thirsty, they can solve the problem and according to the scientists, the sweet things can really make people happy.

sub_questions.content: Did you like to eat fruit when you were a child?

answers.content: Yes, I did. I guess every child likes the food which is sweet. I can still remember my favourite one is watermelon. Every summer, our family members will sit around and eat watermelons together.

sub_questions.content: Is it important to eat fruit?

answers.content: I think so. My parents always tell me that fruit is good for health as it contains a lot of vitamin C and minerals, which are quite important for people to stay healthy. Apart from that, having different kinds of fruit is also very significant.

questions.content: What do street markets sell?

Do you often go to the supermarket?

sub_questions.content: What do street markets sell?

answers.content: As I’ve mentioned, in general, street markets tend to sell cheaper goods compared to the supermarkets, and the things they sell are less known, since they usually have less known and inferior brands. However, the quality of the goods are not necessarily inferior and some of them even have better quality than those sold in the supermarkets.

sub_questions.content: What are the differences between street markets and supermarkets?

answers.content: There are many differences between these two, just like comparing apples and oranges. You see, generally, supermarkets are richer in their variety of goods available, which enables us to better compare goods with one another. However, street markets, on the other hand, are more competitive when it comes to their prices, as they sell cheaper goods.

sub_questions.content: (Version2)Do you often go to the supermarket?

answers.content: Yeah, I go to the supermarket on a weekly basis. Actually, there’s a supermarket nearby my neighbourhood, so it’s pretty convenient for me to purchase groceries at a reasonable price. Besides, I’m a premium member of this supermarket, so I can enjoy a good discount whenever I buy things, at least 20 percent off in fact.

sub_questions.content: Do you often go to the supermarket?

answers.content: Yes, I do! While I enjoy street markets very much, I also go to supermarkets quite often, especially during weekends or holidays. I often go with my family, and whenever we go, we spend a whole afternoon exploring them!

sub_questions.content: Are there many street markets in China?

answers.content: I’m not entirely sure about the exact number of street markets in China, but I have to say that there are indeed a great many of them in almost every Chinese city. Take my hometown as an example. You can easily find one in a random corner of the city, where there are a variety of goods at very favorable prices.

questions.content: Are you interested in watching TV advertisements or internet advertisements?
Do you want to work in advertising in the future?
Do you share advertisements with others?
What kinds of advertisements do you dislike?
Are you more interested in watching TV adverts or internet adverts?

sub_questions.content: Are you interested in watching TV advertisements or internet advertisements?

answers.content: I can’t really say which one I prefer, ‘cause I don’t pay much attention to any types of ads. If I have to answer this question, I suppose I watch more internet advertisements than TV commercials though, since I hardly ever watch any TV. Those internet ads that are interesting are often those with a few comedy plots in them.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to work in advertising in the future?

answers.content: That sounds like a promising proposal! I would if I could! I ‘m fairly interested in psychology, especially behavioral psychology. I’m intrigued to find out people’s inner desires and what pushes them to make shopping decisions. So if I have the opportunity to work in this industry, I don’t mind giving it a try.

I definitely think that advertising is an interesting industry. Yet, I’ve never thought about working in this field. To succeed in this line of work, you have to know enough about psychology to be able to manipulate the consumers’ minds. I’m afraid my ability isn’t adequate for that.

sub_questions.content: Do you share advertisements with others?

answers.content: Only occasionally. I am kinda interested in nutrition supplement products. So sometimes when I see a relative ad, I may share it with my family, especially the ones related to Calcium, as both of my parents often get sore knees and are advised by the doctors to take Calcium supplement.

I’ve shared some shopping links with my friends and family, but not adverts, I’m afraid. You see, I don’t see the need to share ads because usually, my friends and family are likely to have seen the same ads. Besides, most adverts flatter the product, so what they say in the ad isn’t necessarily true. That’s why I don’t see the need to share them.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of advertisements do you dislike?

answers.content: I don’t like ads that promote computer games. For some reason, I just find computer games such a waste of time. The game planners design games to manipulate the players’ minds and behaviors. I don’t think highly of that at all. So I don’t like internet ads which involve inviting people to play games.

I don’t like ads that target children, especially ones that advertise children’s drinks. As we all know, drinks contain so much sugar which can be harmful for children’s health. Even worse, sugar can make people become addicted to the unhealthy drinks. I just don’t see any good in these ads.

sub_questions.content: Are you more interested in watching TV adverts or internet adverts?

answers.content: I’m afraid I’m not particularly interested in either of them. But if I have to choose, I would rather watch TV commercials. That’s because TV commercials are usually of higher picture quality than the ones online. Also, most TV commercials hire entertainment or sports stars to endorse the products, which can be enjoyable to watch.

questions.content: How many public holidays do you have in China?
Do you think people need more public holidays?
How do you usually spend your holidays?
Which holiday is your favorite?

sub_questions.content: How many public holidays do you have in China?

answers.content: That is a tough question because I’ve never counted the number of them. I suppose the figure may be around 10 and most of them last only three days so we can only take very brief rests. But actually, the number of holidays used to be even smaller so I think the situation is being improved.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people need more public holidays?

answers.content: Yes, definitely! The reasons are simple: people’s pressure is growing quickly nowadays, so taking more time off is both mentally and physically beneficial to them. Also, I believe that the advances of technology have increased the efficiency of people’s work so it might be a great choice to give them more spare time, so that they may come back with more energy.

sub_questions.content: How do you usually spend your holidays?

answers.content: Well, the way to spend my holidays depends on the length of them. If there is only a three-day vacation, staying at home with video games and the latest movies or reality shows would be my first choice. But if time permits, I will be more than willing to have a short trip to some cities next to mine. A long journey is something that I will never choose since it can be very exhausting.

sub_questions.content: Which holiday is your favorite?

answers.content: I prefer the Spring Festival, which is actually a traditional Chinese festival. I love it for two reasons. One is that I can take a long holiday, which might be a fantastic chance for me to relax. Another reason is that the Spring Festival is when we family members gather together. Spending time with them, I think, is sweet and pleasant.

questions.content: Are there any differences between your favorite meeting places in the present and in your childhood?

sub_questions.content: Are there any differences between your favorite meeting places in the present and in your childhood?

answers.content: Of course! When I was a child, my favorite place to go was the ice cream shop. If somebody was taking me to the ice cream store, I’d be thrilled! And I could totally fall in love with this person. However, now, I like to visit cafes or tea houses, as these places are comfortable to sit down for a chat.

sub_questions.content: Do you think there are some places more suitable for meeting with others?

answers.content: Absolutely. Even though many people like to meet with friends or colleagues in a restaurant, I really think the tea house, cafe, and some small clubs can be better places for such meetings. You see, products in these places aren’t very expensive. Also, the atmosphere is often great for socializing.

sub_questions.content: Why are some meeting places better than others?

answers.content: Well, I guess that’s because some places have a better atmosphere that’s more suitable for socializing. You know, a pleasant environment can contribute to quality interactions. However, in places that are noisy, smelly or dirty, like the seafood market, I imagine people wouldn’t even want to stay for long, let alone sit down for a chat.

For me, a convenient location, and relaxing atmosphere contribute to a perfect meeting place. For the location, it’s best if the meeting place is near a subway station or a bus stop so people can easily get there. In terms of atmosphere, soft music and caring staff are both essential for my ideal meeting place as well.

sub_questions.content: Are there any differences between your favorite meeting places in the present and those from your childhood?

answers.content: Yes. When I was a child, in particular, 7 years old, it was the park near my apartment that was a really attractive place to hang out with friends. I can still remember playing hide-and-seek there after school. However, the number of parks in my town has dropped sharply in recent years, and our busy lives have left us with less free time. Therefore we haven’t met in a park for about 5 years.

sub_questions.content: Do you think there are some places that are more suitable for meeting with others?

answers.content: There are a variety of western restaurants in an iconic tower which dominates the skyline in the center of my city. During the meal, we can not only share our daily lives with each other quietly but can also enjoy the stunning views of the whole city to help us unwind.

sub_questions.content: Where is your favorite places to meet with people?

answers.content: Ah, I think the cafe is a good place for appointments, whether with friends, family, or classmates. The atmosphere in the cafe is often relaxing. The coffee smell can calm people down, and make people want to talk. I think it’s a good place for a chat, yeah.

Personally speaking, the café close to the seashore in my hometown is my favorite spot to meet with my friends as it offers us a relaxing and agreeable atmosphere to chat with each other and play cards. In the afternoon, the comfortable ocean breeze and the glory of the setting sun also fill us with joy.

questions.content: How do you keep your photos?

sub_questions.content: How do you keep your photos?

I usually take photos with my iphone and that’s where I keep them, but I always keep a backup of all the important ones on my computer in case I lose the data.

sub_questions.content: In what situations do you take photos?

answers.content: The main purpose for my taking photos is to capture the precious moments in my life, such as the first day or last day at work, a family trip, a sweet moment with my boyfriend, or something I’m surprised to see and want to share with others.

sub_questions.content: (3)Do you prefer to take photos yourself or have other people take them?

answers.content: If I need to choose between the two, I would probably go with taking photos myself. I feel that different people have different characteristics, so the style of the photos will be distinguishing. sub_questions.content: Do you like taking photos?

answers.content: I would say so. I especially like taking photos of the outdoors because I really enjoy the amazing natural scenery such as early spring, flowers, sunsets, seashores, and so on. I also like taking photos of my family when we travel around, to capture our happy moments.

sub_questions.content: How often do you take photos?

answers.content: First of all, I’m not a professional photographer , I’m just someone who really loves and admires good views. Sometimes I only take photos a few times a week, but other times, I take a lot of photos when I go on a trip, when I have a wonderful meal or when I hang out with friends.

questions.content: Why is it that some people don't make plans?
What are the benefits of making plans?
Do you use paper or your cellphone to make plans?
Do you like making plans?
Are you good at managing your time?
Do you make plans every day?
What is the latest plan you made?
What is the hardest part about making plans?

sub_questions.content: Why is it that some people don't make plans?

answers.content: Some people choose not to make plans because they prefer spontaneity and flexibility in their lives. For instance, they might feel that schedules limit their freedom to explore new opportunities or follow their instincts. Additionally, some individuals find planning overwhelming or stressful and prefer to go with the flow. This approach allows them to adapt easily to unexpected circumstances and challenges that may arise, leading to a more relaxed lifestyle.

Some individuals may prefer living spontaneously rather than making plans due to their adventurous nature or desire for flexibility in life. They might believe that unpredictability brings excitement and opportunities for new experiences. On the other hand, some people might find planning overwhelming or restrictive, preferring to let life unfold naturally. Personalities, lifestyle choices, and cultural influences can all play a role in why some individuals choose not to make plans.

sub_questions.content: What are the benefits of making plans?

answers.content: Planning helps us stay organized and focused on our goals. It allows us to allocate our time and resources effectively, reducing stress and ensuring successful outcomes. For instance, planning a trip in advance can help save money and ensure we visit all the places we desire. In academics or work, making plans helps in achieving desired results within set deadlines. Overall, planning is a valuable tool for productivity and success.

Making plans provides a clear direction and helps in achieving goals efficiently. It enhances time management skills and reduces stress as tasks are organized. Moreover, planning enables effective resource allocation and prioritization of work, leading to increased productivity. For instance, planning a daily schedule can ensure better utilization of time and prevent procrastination. Overall, having a strategy in place can positively impact success and overall well-being.

sub_questions.content: Do you use paper or your cellphone to make plans?

answers.content: I prefer utilizing my cellphone to organize my schedule. The convenience of digital planning tools allows for better time management and on-the-go adjustments to my commitments. For example, apps like calendars and reminders help me stay efficient and well-prepared, ensuring smooth transitions between appointments. Thus, I find my cellphone an indispensable companion in shaping my day.

I prefer using my cellphone to make plans. It helps me stay organized with reminders, calendars, and apps for scheduling. Having everything in one place makes it convenient, especially when I need to juggle work, studies, and social activities. Moreover, I can easily make changes or updates on the go, ensuring flexibility in my plans.

sub_questions.content: Do you like making plans?

answers.content: Yes, I do enjoy making plans as it gives me a sense of organization and direction in my life. Whether it involves scheduling a weekend trip or outlining my goals for the upcoming month, planning allows me to stay focused and motivated. For instance, I recently created a study timetable to prepare for my IELTS exam, which has helped me manage my time effectively and track my progress.

Yes, I enjoy making plans because they help me stay organized and focused. For example, I usually plan my week ahead to balance work, hobbies, and social activities. It gives me a sense of control and allows me to achieve my goals effectively. Planning also helps me anticipate obstacles and find solutions in advance, which makes me feel more prepared for whatever comes my way.

sub_questions.content: Are you good at managing your time?

answers.content: Generally speaking, I’m literally an expert when it comes to time management. As my friends always say, unlike most of them, I can always distribute my time properly when finishing my homework, and I'm able to make both long-term and short-term plans, which helps me to monitor and evaluate my progress in both work and study.

sub_questions.content: Do you make plans every day?

answers.content: No, I don’t. However, I do make plans every week and every month. For example, when I was preparing for my exams last semester, I made weekly and monthly plans to help me schedule my timetable.

sub_questions.content: What is the latest plan you made?

answers.content: The most recent plan I’ve made is for my upcoming graduation holiday. I’ve planned to travel to Russia after my graduation and to go to Moscow and some other cities for around two weeks where I will meet my friends.

sub_questions.content: What is the hardest part about making plans?

answers.content: Well, I think the hardest part is that when you make plans, you have to think in advance about what to do and when to do it, and that’s very challenging. For instance, when I make plans for my exams, I have to decide which chapters are the most important and settle on a date to review them.

questions.content: Which place left the deepest impression on you when travelling?

sub_questions.content: Which place left the deepest impression on you when travelling?

sub_questions.content: Which cities have you travelled to?

answers.content: Well, I’ve traveled to many North American cities as well as Asian cities, including New York, Washington D. C., Boston, Toronto, Montreal, Bangkok and so on. I visited these cities during my holidays and also my exchange semester two years ago.

sub_questions.content: Do you like travelling?

answers.content: Yes, I’m a fan of traveling, especially backpacking, which is a kind of traveling characterized by low expenses and high degrees of cultural immersion. I enjoy traveling very much mainly out of my eagerness to know more about different cultures.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of cities do you like to travel to?

answers.content: As a nature lover, I’m always fascinated by cities with gorgeous natural beauty and authentic cuisine. Compared to metropolitans, I rather prefer traveling to small towns that are close to nature, especially those adjacent to mountains or lakes.

questions.content: Are you more patient now than when you were a child?
Does your job require you to be patient?
What is it that makes you feel impatient?
Are you a patient person?
Were you patient when you were young?
How do you feel when other people are not patient?
Are you less or more patient when you are angry?

sub_questions.content: Are you more patient now than when you were a child?

answers.content: Certainly. As a child, impatience often got the best of me. However, with age, I've developed a greater ability to remain patient in various situations. I find myself better equipped to handle delays and challenges without becoming frustrated. These days, taking a more calm and composed approach comes more naturally to me, which I believe is an essential skill to navigate life's ups and downs effectively.

sub_questions.content: Does your job require you to be patient?
What is it that makes you feel impatient?

answers.content: One thing that can make me feel impatient is waiting in long queues at crowded places. Whether it's at the grocery store or a busy event, standing in line for a long time can test my patience. I find myself feeling irritable and restless when I have to wait in line for an extended period. It's the feeling of time slipping away that makes me most impatient in such situations.

What makes me feel impatient is when I have to wait for others who are consistently late despite setting a specific meeting time. It can be frustrating when punctuality is not valued, as it shows a lack of consideration for other people's time. Keeping others waiting can disrupt schedules and cause unnecessary stress, which is why I find it difficult to cope with such situations.

sub_questions.content: Are you a patient person?

answers.content: In terms of patience, I consider myself quite composed. For instance, whenever faced with challenging situations, I always strive to remain calm and collected. This quality has proven to be beneficial in my personal and professional life, allowing me to handle difficult circumstances with grace and composure. Patience, I believe, is not merely a virtue but a practical skill that helps navigate life's complexities efficiently.

Yes, I would consider myself a patient person. For example, when faced with challenging situations, I always try to remain calm and think things through before reacting. Patience is an essential quality that helps me navigate through life's obstacles with composure and resilience. By being patient, I believe I can handle various tasks efficiently and make thoughtful decisions in different circumstances.

sub_questions.content: Were you patient when you were young?

answers.content: Actually, I have been very tolerant and easy-going since I was very little. I think that is because my parents were both good-tempered. All my childhood friends liked to hang out with me because I always listened to them attentively. My mom often says that being a teacher or psychiatrist will be a perfect job for me since I have great patience.

sub_questions.content: How do you feel when other people are not patient?

answers.content: Well, in this kind of situation, I get very nervous and wish to bring an end to this situation as soon as possible, since under such circumstances, I wonder if I have just bothered others or have brought inconvenience to them. But when I was younger, I might have got very annoyed when others showed an impatient attitude because at that time I was not mature enough.

sub_questions.content: Are you less or more patient when you are angry?

answers.content: Definitely less patient! In a fit of anger, I may totally lose control and say whatever comes into my mind first, no matter if it is suitable or not. At the same time, I might talk more rapidly and never want to repeat what I have said again. I know that is terrible, but that is what will happen when I get mad.

questions.content: Do you like to try new food?

What kind of foreign foods are popular in your country?

sub_questions.content: Do you like to try new food?

Every time I have meals in a restaurant that I have never been to, it is like having an adventure. Also, as a real foodie, I’m fond of cooking with different ingredients, hoping that I can invent my own recipe one day.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of new food have you tried recently?

answers.content: Well, I am always willing to try new things and last month I saw a newly-opened Mexican restaurant, so I decided to have a taste. I ordered nachos and tacos, which are two types of Mexican food. I thought it might take me some time to get used to the food but surprisingly I fell in love with it from the first bite! The spicy flavor is exactly my cup of tea.

sub_questions.content: What kind of foreign foods are popular in your country?

answers.content: I suppose the so-called “junk food” in the western country has gained a lot of popularity in China. There are a large number of restaurants serving hamburgers, pizzas and French fries. People, especially youngsters, are really enthusiastic about them probably because eating this kind of food is time-saving and also very fashionable. But personally, I don't regard it as a positive trend in our society.

sub_questions.content: Do you prepare tea or coffee for guests at home?

answers.content: Definitely coffee! The reasons are simple. Personally, I’m not interested in drinking tea, so you can find anything but tea in my apartment. Besides, the flavor of coffee can be changed according to guests’ preferences. You can add a dash of milk or sugar if you don't like the bitter taste, but the flavor of tea is not changeable. So I am not sure it is suitable for every visitor.

sub_questions.content: Do Chinese people like to drink tea or coffee?

But since coffee was introduced into China and it is regarded as a modern beverage, the number of coffee lovers is also growing. Especially among the young people, drinking a cup of Americano has become a habit in the morning.

sub_questions.content: When was the last time you drank tea or coffee?

answers.content: Well, actually I am a big fan of coffee and I have the habit of drinking this kind of beverage every day. You know, I just had a cup of espresso this morning. I am really partial to it because just a sip of coffee can keep me refreshed and energetic. As for tea, it is not a common thing in my daily life.

questions.content: ln what conditions would it be difficult for you to use a computer?
