When was the first time you used a computer?
When do you use a computer?

sub_questions.content: ln what conditions would it be difficult for you to use a computer?

answers.content: Oh, I’ve never thought about this. As long as I can access a computer, it’s fine using it. But there were times when I got sore eyes. That’s because I stared at the screen for too long. Yeah, well, when my eyes aren’t dealing with the screen, I find it hard to use the computer.

sub_questions.content: In what conditions would you use a computer?

answers.content: Well, unlike a lot of young people who play games on the computer, I only use the computer for watching shows, and writing essays. I know these things can be done on the phone as well, but I just can’t stand looking at the small screen for a long time. So I choose the computer instead.

sub_questions.content: In what conditions would it be difficult for you to use a computer?

answers.content: Without electricity, of course. My laptop would die out in an hour without a charger. Also, I think computers are less useful if they’re not connected to the Internet. Technically you can do some writing or editing offline, but at times you’d still need to go online to double-check something or do some research.

sub_questions.content: What would your life be like without computers?

answers.content: Ah, I don’t know. I’m sure it would be much less convenient than it is now. Not that I am an Internet-addict, but it does great help to my study and work. Without it, I think I wouldn’t be as efficient as I am now. So yeah, my life would be difficult without it, I suppose.

If you’d asked me this question five years ago, I would probably have said my life would be a mess without computers. But things are so different now, because mobiles and tablets are taking over. People don’t need computers as badly as they used to. So I guess I’d be fine, as long as I could keep my phone and iPad.

sub_questions.content: When was the first time you used a computer?

answers.content: That’s when I was about 15 years old, my mother signed me up for a summer course where they taught me how to type fast on the computer. To be honest, that was the first time I had a chance to use the computer.

It was in first grade. My dad bought a computer, and back then they were still big and heavy. sub_questions.content: When do you use a computer?

I also play video games on it even though there are more than enough mobile games on the market. The thing is, some classic games like LOL are only available for PC.

questions.content: How do you feel after taking a nap?
Do you take a nap when you are taking your rest?
What do you usually do during a break?
How often do you take a break?
Do you prefer a long break or several short breaks?
Why do you need to take a break?

sub_questions.content: How do you feel after taking a nap?

answers.content: Taking a nap leaves me feeling refreshed, like a blossoming flower after a gentle rain. The quiet embrace of sleep awakens me with a renewed energy, inspiring creativity and lifting my spirits. It's a brief escape from the hustle and bustle of the day, like a mini-vacation for my mind and body.

sub_questions.content: Do you take a nap when you are taking your rest?

answers.content: I do find that taking a short nap during my rest period helps me recharge. It allows me to regain focus and energy, especially when I have a busy schedule. Napping is a rejuvenating experience that can improve productivity and enhance overall well-being. So, yes, I do take a nap when I need to during my breaks.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do during a break?

answers.content: The first thing I do is picking up my phone and checking if I’ve missed any messages. In order not to distract me, I always put my phone on silent before I start my business, so I’ve got to check it afterwards. Then, jumping into bed and closing my eyes would be the next thing I would do, because I can fully relax.

sub_questions.content: How often do you take a break?

answers.content: For me, I usually work or study for forty minutes and then take a twenty-minute break. I think forty minutes is about my attention span and that break can keep me energetic. Also, there’s some scientific data showing that human brains may start to lose concentration after focusing for half an hour, so I think my decision is appropriate.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer a long break or several short breaks?

answers.content: Actually, I think a long break would be my preference. Personally speaking, I cannot get focused immediately. I mean, it takes me a few minutes to really get down to my business, so if the breaks are too frequent, the chances are, before I really concentrate again, it’s time to take another rest! So a long break at the end might be more ideal.

sub_questions.content: Why do you need to take a break?

answers.content: Well, I guess taking a rest is the best way to maintain our efficiency. Keeping concentrated for a long period of time can be extremely exhausting and I become really impatient if I work or study for hours without breaks. At that point, even a small distraction can take my concentration away, so having a bit of rest can restore my energy and help me stay focused later on.

questions.content: Do you always have good sleep?

sub_questions.content: Do you always have good sleep?

sub_questions.content: What time do you usually go to bed?

answers.content: I often go to bed before 11pm, but occasionally I have to burn the midnight oil for exams or assignments. I try not to stay up too late though, as it affects my energy levels the next day. So even when I do have to stay up, I make sure I go to bed before 2am at the latest.

answers.content: No, not as far as I’ve noticed. In fact, most elders sleep only a little. Take my grandparents as an example. They are often up at 5 or 6 in the morning, and the older they get, the less sleep they seem to need. I’ve never heard elderly people complain about not getting enough sleep even they don’t sleep much at all.

answers.content: Generally around 6-8 hours. Quality sleep is important to me, so I make sure I get enough sleep at night, otherwise I’d be grumpy the next day and wouldn’t be able to keep up my energy. But when I have the pressure of assignments, well, I’ll just have to burn the midnight oil, and maybe end up not getting any sleep at all.

questions.content: Do you think maths is important?

Are girls generally good at maths?

sub_questions.content: Do you think maths is important?

answers.content: Absolutely, I believe mathematics is incredibly important. It serves as the foundation for many other subjects and practical skills. From understanding scientific principles to managing finances, mathematics plays a crucial role in our daily lives. Moreover, it fosters critical thinking and problem-solving abilities, which are essential for success in various careers. Whether it's in the field of finance, engineering, technology, or even art and design, a solid grasp of mathematics can open doors to countless opportunities. Overall, I see mathematics as an indispensable tool for navigating the modern world.

Yes, for sure. Although for some people, myself included, learning maths is difficult and challenging, it’s very important since the modern world is built on it. As you can see, it’s used in economics, scientific experiments and so on, which serve as the foundation of our civilization.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it’s difficult to learn maths well?

One thing that can make learning math easier is finding a good teacher or mentor who can guide you through the process and provide personalized support. Additionally, seeking out resources like textbooks, online tutorials, and practice problems can help reinforce the material and improve your understanding.

Definitely yes. Despite being a boy/girl and having always been expected to master maths well, I’m quite bad at it and have to spend way more time learning it and doing exercises than most people. So I do believe it’s difficult to learn, especially if you want to learn it well.

sub_questions.content: Are girls generally good at maths?

It's important to focus on providing an inclusive and supportive learning environment for all students to thrive in mathematics.

It varies from person to person, but there’s an old saying that goes: “Everyone is equal in mathematics”. I think maths is a subject which depends on how much effort you spend on it, rather than the gender.

sub_questions.content: Have you received a gift you didn’t like?

answers.content: Um… I have to admit I’m a rather picky person who’s a bit hard to please. So um I guess I don’t really like most of the gifts people gave me ha-ha! To be specific, my mom bought me a set of Complete IELTS as New Year gift, and I was like… “Seriously?” I mean, I love her and I know she really wants me to do well in the exam, but… You know, really? Are we giving each other textbooks now? As gifts? Ha-ha!

sub_questions.content: When was the last time you received a gift?

answers.content: Let’s see… Well I think that was the wine my best friends gave me in the New Year’s Eve. You see, young people in China always get together with their friends at this time, and exchange gifts. Sometimes we play “Secret Santa”. Well, perhaps we shouldn’t call it “Secret Santa” since it’s not really Christmas, right? You know, we just exchange gifts and guess who bought which one and blah blah….

sub_questions.content: When do you send gifts?

answers.content: Oh there are many occasions where you should prepare a gift, like the birthday party of an intimate friend, or maybe a housewarming party of your colleague. Nowadays, western festivals have become much more popular than before, so I also send my friends some presents when Halloween, Christmas or even Easter.

sub_questions.content: Why do people send gifts?

answers.content: I think it’s primarily for maintaining the relationship, whether it’s a friendship or a love relationship or work-relations. As cheesy as some people may see gifts are, they are a great way to show that we care about whoever we’re giving the gifts to. So they can certainly smooth the relationship. I suppose most people see it in the way that I do.

sub_questions.content: How often do you buy others gifts?

answers.content: Perhaps too often. Well, I am a people person and like to make friends. Gifts are a good way to maintain the friendship. So I end up having to buy gifts about every month because it’s always someone’s birthday. And then plus the festival gifts for my own family. I am actually starting to feel that I visit the gift stores too often.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of gifts are popular in your country?

answers.content: In China, for traditional festivals, the majority of Chinese still like to bring with them the fruits or snacks when they visit family or friends. But many people, especially the young generation, like myself, also shop jewelries or clothes as gifts. Some people even just send coupons or movie tickets. These are the common ones, I guess.

sub_questions.content: What do you give others as gifts?

answers.content: Well, as a student, I don't really have much budget so I prefer some meaningful but affordable stuff. I would just be frank with my friends and ask them what they need so that my gifts would not become a waste. Sometimes it’s just a cup. Sometimes it’s a scarf. But I will always have this present personalized by attaching a handmade card saying how much I appreciate my friends.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to send expensive gifts?

answers.content: No, I don’t like it. But sometimes I have to. I have some friends who are pretty wealthy and often buy luxury items, like LV handbags, or Huge Boss perfume. If what I buy for them is of a cheap price, they’ll probably feel insulted. So for them, I’d be ready to spend a huge amount of money on gifts even I wish I didn’t have to.

sub_questions.content: What’s the best present or gift you have ever received?

answers.content: To be honest, I once got an amazon voucher from one of my best friends and I consider it really useful. I mean sometimes I got very nice gifts but sometimes the gifts would just be sitting on the shelf forever, so with this voucher, I can just buy things that I really need.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to watch live sports games?

answers.content: I do, although I can’t always make time for live matches. Live sports are interesting because they show you what the game is really like! You know, it’s not always possible to go to a real game, so if you want to experience the atmosphere, watching live games on TV is the next best thing.

sub_questions.content: Do you like watching sports programs on TV?

answers.content: Yes, absolutely! I think sports games are exciting to watch! I especially enjoy watching basketball matches on TV. That’s how I know about big sports stars like Jordon, Yijianlian and Yaoming. They are my super idols and I love to watch them play on TV.

sub_questions.content: Do you usually watching sports programs on TV?

answers.content: No, I do not usually watch sports programs. However, I often watch NBA with my friends about three or four times a year because I really enjoy watching and discussing basketball games with my friends, enjoying snacks and beers. But when I am alone, I do not usually watch sport games because I am more into tv shows comparing to sports.

Yes, I often watch sports programs on TV, such as volleyball, football and basketball. I think it’s cheaper and more convenient to watch sports programs on TV. It’s simply impractical to fly all over the world to watch live games, especially now with the raging pandemic. Besides, I really enjoy staying with my family while watching the Olympics and chilling on my comfy sofa. I can also invite friends over and enjoy scrumptious food during the game.

sub_questions.content: Who do you like to watch sports games with?

We both love to watch sports games, so I especially like to watch with him for we could discuss the skills and strategies together, we support the same team and we often cheer for them on our own way.

As we all know, the more we connect and share with others, the more happiness we create. For example, I like sports games such as volleyball and baseball which are chock-full of team spirit and professional skills. I especially like to watch such sports with my family. Not only is it a kind of luxury to spend time with my family watching the game and shouting at the TV, but it also creates a natural space for coming together and communicating. It’s nothing but pure joy to share my own thoughts and elation with family members when my favorite team scores and wins.

sub_questions.content: Do you watch live sports games?

answers.content: I do watch live Olympic games. I prefer live games because it gives me excitement and nervousness at the same time. My favourite live sport game is diving competition because I really appreciate how beautiful the dives are. Also, the uncertainty brought by live games lures me into keeping watching.

I will choose to watch live sports games if my favorite team or player is there. For instance, during the FIFA World Cup or the European Championships, I’ll stay up late to watch live sports games or hang out with friends to watch the live games in a bar. Last year during the European Championships, I watched the match between Portugal and Germany with my friends at a German bar. It was a terrible choice since I’m a fan of Cristiano Ronaldo!

sub_questions.content: What kinds of games do you expect to watch in the future?

answers.content: As time goes by I find myself becoming more easily interested in those exciting sports such as diving. I think the more challenging the sports are, the stronger will players may get, so I decide to learn to dive in this summer. But before that, I expect to watch many diving sports games online in the future in order to study and imitate the basic postures.

I like watching different kinds of sports games, especially during the Olympics every four years. I spend nearly the entire day sitting in front of the television! Since I went skiing for the first time last year with my friends, I am now addicted, so I am looking forward to binging on the Olympic Alpine Skiing! Although it’s more dangerous, it ramps up the level of excitement for both players and the audience. It feels like the competitors are like birds flying in the sky. I am definitely keen to see more skiing in this winter Olympics in China.

questions.content: Why do people think New Year is a new beginning?
Do you have any ceremonies to celebrate the New Year in your country?
Do you remember a particular New Year that you celebrated?

sub_questions.content: Why do people think New Year is a new beginning?

answers.content: I think it’s actually a sense of ritual in life. We make a year-end summary to review our losses and gains throughout the year. These reviews symbolize a transitory end so we can therefore open a new chapter in life. Each day is just another day, but when the year resets, we feel like the slate is cleaned and we can turn over a new leaf.

sub_questions.content: Do you have any ceremonies to celebrate the New Year in your country?

answers.content: In the old days, we used to have ceremonies like firefighting, burning incense and making vows, or just watching the celebrations on TV. But with the growing richness of activities, people, especially the youth, are no longer confined to traditional ways of celebrating, yet what remains is essentially the same, which is to accompany the important people in your life when seeing in the New Year.

sub_questions.content: Do you remember a particular New Year that you celebrated?

answers.content: Yes, the most impressive one for me was when we went camping on the outskirts of the city to celebrate. It was totally new for me, pitching tents, building fires, and cooking in the open air. Despite the freezing weather, it was the ideal way to escape the hustle and bustle and return to the wonderful feeling of nature.

sub_questions.content: How do you celebrate New Year?

answers.content: Well, generally speaking, I celebrate New Year either with my friends or my family. If I stay at home with my parents and relatives, we will cook the dinner all together. It’s actually a bit hectic since everyone is busy with their dishes. However, if I hang out with my friends, it will be much more casual. We normally find a good bistro serving both dinner and alcohol and spend our night there drinking and talking.

questions.content: Can you tell if you like someone when you meet them for the first time? Why?

answers.content: Unfortunately, no. I always work/study with the same group of people during the weekdays, and the only time I can make to meet new friends would be the weekends. However, I really need a good rest on Saturdays and Sundays, so I don't really have chances to do that.

sub_questions.content: Do you like meeting new people?

answers.content: Well, I always regard myself as an extroverted person, who can get energized and motivated from meeting new friends. So, yes, I do enjoy going out to meet new people. Interacting with people that I haven’t met before can always get me excited.

answers.content: Honestly, it is very probable that I will have a mixture of feelings. I mean, for sure, their enthusiasm can warm my heart and I will definitely feel honored. However, I may also feel a little bit nervous and not know how to respond if the people are too passionate.

questions.content: How much sleep do you need every day?

sub_questions.content: How much sleep do you need every day?

answers.content: Well, you know I am quite occupied with my work, so normally I take a seven-hour sleep every day. I have to admit that the time is insufficient for the reason that I always feel a bit sleepy in the afternoon. So, I believe that I need more than seven hours like seven and a half or eight hours to take a rest.

answers.content: Yes, I do have the habit of reading before I go to bed. Latest news and articles written by famous people would be my preference for bedtime reading. Those exciting novels and hilarious jokes would not be my choice, since I need to calm down before sleep, so I am more into some less thrilling stuff.

sub_questions.content: Do you take naps at noon?

Even a very brief rest can make me feel refreshed at noon so that I can devote myself to serious things later with higher efficiency. But the truth is, I barely have nap time now because of too much work, so every day in the afternoon, I can feel slightly exhausted.

sub_questions.content: How have your sleeping habits changed since you were young?

answers.content: I suppose so. Back in my childhood, I used to share a bed with my parents since I was afraid of darkness and having one of my parents around could comfort me. However, now I prefer to sleep alone probably because I think that is what a grown-up person should do.

I did ride a bike to school for a period of time. It was an enjoyable experience as it helped me to not only save money on transportation but also stay active and healthy.

sub_questions.content: Did you ride a bike when you were little?

sub_questions.content: Did you have a bike when you were young?

questions.content: What is your daily study routine?
Have you ever changed your routine?
Do you think it is important to have a daily routine for your study?
What part of your day do you like best?

sub_questions.content: What is your daily study routine?

answers.content: On weekdays, I get up at about 7:30 in the morning and finish washing. If there is no class in the morning, I will go to the library to study by myself. After lunch, I will take time to review the contents learned in the morning and prepare the items needed for the afternoon course. I think a proper rest can make my brain cells more active, so I will arrange some relaxation time around dinner. From 8:00 to 10:00 in the evening is my most efficient time, so most of my homework will be completed within this time period.

On school days I have to wake up early and get washed and dressed. Then I usually rush to the university campus to attend a lecture. After hours of classes, I eat my lunch in the school cafeteria and then I take a nap in any quite classroom I can find. After the afternoon classes are over, I return to my dorm at around 7 pm. It’s normal for me to spend the evening with my roommates. We girls always have a lot to talk about. If there is a pile of homework hanging around, I will stay up late, and then I go to bed.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever changed your routine?

answers.content: I will change my habits periodically. There are two reasons. First, I think the invariable life will make people feel bored and even tired gradually. Life needs a certain freshness, which is more important for students. The dull study life needs some adjustments. Second, the courses of each semester are different, so the change of time management is also indispensable, especially for the schedule of extracurricular discussion and social communication, which is the focus that contemporary students need to weigh more.

I seldom change my routine. I’m a bit like Sheldon from Big Bang Theory, who is used to living an ordered life with certain rules. It’s not that I don’t do anything other than my daily routine, it’s just if one day my friend suddenly invites me to hang out with them, I’ll most certainly say no. But if they invite me a few days in advance , I’ll put it in my schedule, which makes me feel safe and comfortable. And then I’ll most probably say yes.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important to have a daily routine for your study?

answers.content: Yes. As far as I know, successful people of the past and present are known to have a daily routine. For example, Steve Jobs, Benjamin Franklin and Elon Musk. A daily routine easily eliminates distractions because people are focused only on those things that need to be done. As for students, the more they keep to their daily routine, the more automatic it becomes for them to perform all the activities throughout the day. As a result, their study becomes smooth and efficient.

Yes, of course I believe a daily routine is necessary for my study. Students who adopt a certain daily routine usually trive and live their best life, while students who don’t have a routine can barely keep up with their assignments. I believe that my daily routine makes me more productive, less stressed and emotionally overwhelmed, since I always know what’s next on my list. Then I can finish every task on time, thanks to my schedule .

sub_questions.content: What part of your day do you like best?

answers.content: There is an old Chinese saying goes: "work at sunrise and rest at sunset." People can make contributions to society, family and themselves only when they are sober. I personally like the moment when I get up in the morning, because it means that I want to start a day's study and work, which means that I will achieve a brand-new achievement for myself. Even if I just learn a little new knowledge or achieve a small goal, these are all footprints on the road of my life.

Probably the time when I lie down on my bed and haven’t fallen asleep yet. There is a reward mechanism in my daily routine. Which means if I finish all my tasks after working really hard in the daytime. I reward myself with a period of time playing games or watching drama. And that always take place before I go to sleep. I believe everyone needs some time each day to amuse themselves. Otherwise, there will be little motivation for them to work harder.

questions.content: Is it important to be happy?

sub_questions.content: Is it important to be happy?

questions.content: How can we protect wild animals?

Have you ever seen any wild animals before?

sub_questions.content: How can we protect wild animals?

answers.content: There are several ways to protect wild animals. As a normal person, we could stop buying anything made of the wild animals’ body parts, for example, fur products and ivory products. At the same time, our government could also take action to stop the relative manufacturing activities. And I believe it is urgent to build more nature reserves. We should put more money and manpower into it.

sub_questions.content: What wild animals do you like most?

answers.content: My favorite wild animals are sea otters. They just seem to get so much enjoyment out of life. Maybe we should try to be more like otters. I also think it's cute how sea otters balance shells on their chests and break them open with rocks. Wolves are really cool too. They are very loyal and devoted to their families. I guess there are quite a few lessons we could learn from wild animals.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever seen any wild animals before?

answers.content: Yes. I was so fortunate to see a yak in the plateau. They were large and hairy, ambling quietly. I was about to get out of the car and get a close-up of them, but my mom stopped me. The yak is not docile; they will run fast to escape once a stranger comes close. So, I could only watch them from a distance.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to go to the zoo?

answers.content: Yes. Because it is the only way I (and many others too) would see these animals in person. Zoos also educate people about animals, and tell us about the ones in danger of extinction. Some animals are so in danger that there are more of them in zoos than in the wild. Zoos also take in animals that would otherwise be killed, because there is nowhere else for them to go.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever kept pets?

answers.content: Yes. I had several ducklings when I was about 8 years old. I hadn’t wished to have my own pets. However, my mom had hoped to nurture my sense of responsibility, so she bought them and asked me to raise them. I started to love the little ducks, as they lined up after me every day, quacking for food. It made me feel that they depended on me. So I had to protect them.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of news do you share with our friends?

I also like sharing recipes, book recommendations, and fitness tips to inspire and bond with my friends over common interests. Sharing news is a way to connect and create meaningful conversations with those I care about.

sub_questions.content: Who is the first person you like to share good news with?

answers.content: The first person I would like to share good news with is definitely my close friend Jack. He's been my best buddy since childhood and has always been there for me through thick and thin. Whenever something exciting happens, he's the one I turn to for support and celebration. Sharing good news with Jack not only doubles the joy but also strengthens our bond as friends.

sub_questions.content: Did your parents teach you to share when you were a child?

answers.content: Yes, my parents taught me the value of sharing from a young age. They emphasized the importance of generosity, empathy, and cooperation, teaching me to share toys, snacks, and experiences with others. Their teachings instilled in me a sense of compassion and social responsibility that I still carry with me today.

When I was little, my mom was the one who was always telling me to share things. She asked me to take out my toys when my childhood friends came, she also required me to retell what had happened in the kindergarten when I got back home. I believe my mom made a wise decision because sharing things, no matter material stuff or daily stories, is really helpful to maintain a good relationship with others.

sub_questions.content: What kind of things do you like to share with others?

answers.content: I enjoy sharing positive experiences, knowledge, and resources with others. Whether it's recommending a good book, sharing travel tips, or offering support and encouragement, sharing enriches relationships and fosters a sense of community.

Actually, there are a lot of different kinds of stuff that I am willing to tell others, especially delightful things. When I pass an important test, I would tell my parents about it. When I see a hilarious story, I would recount it to my friends. Sharing, in my opinion, can double the happiness, so I always tell cheerful things.

sub_questions.content: What kind of things are not suitable for sharing?

answers.content: Some personal or sensitive matters, such as private family issues or confidential information, are not suitable for sharing with others. It's essential to respect privacy boundaries and be mindful of what information is appropriate to share.

Privacy is something that I think is absolutely unsuitable for telling others. For the ones who tell the personal things, they may put themselves in danger if the listeners are not trustworthy enough. For the listeners, they may not want to bear those secret things. So, sharing personal stuff is not so responsible for both the tellers and the listeners.

sub_questions.content: Do you have anything to share with others recently?

answers.content: Recently, I've been sharing some interesting articles and videos with my friends and family, especially those related to topics we're passionate about, like travel, technology, and culture. It's a great way to connect and exchange ideas with others.

Well, recently I have been working on this IELTS examination, so the things that I would like to talk to my friends are usually about this exam. After reading or listening, I would share what I have known with other people.
