And girls, you know, like romantic movies. Just like the boys, once sitting in front of the TV, they can’t be distracted by anything.

sub_questions.content: Do your friends like this movie?

answers.content: No, not really. Old movies are not their cup of tea. On the one hand, unlike the newly-shot types, old movies are in low-definition, not very clear. On the other hand, the characters are becoming very old-fashioned. You know, the young generations are trend-seekers; they love fashionable things. Movies like Top Gun are out of the question for their choice.

sub_questions.content: What’s your favorite movie?

answers.content: I guess my favorite movie should be Top Gun. I love it, first of all, because it is Tom Cruise’s first movie, and it made Tom an international star. He had great performances in many exciting scenes of air battles. Just like the song in the movie, my breath was taken away by the scenes.

questions.content: Which morning do you like best in the week?
Do you get up early on weekends?
What do you usually do when you get up early?
Do you often get up early in the morning?

sub_questions.content: Which morning do you like best in the week?

answers.content: Probably Saturday mornings are the best for me. Obviously, I don't have to work/study on that day so I can enjoy the morning in an unhurried manner, making myself a cup of tea and reading my favorite novels. Saturday mornings are the beginning of my hassle-free weekends, so when I wake up at that time, I feel very excited about my weekend plans.

sub_questions.content: Do you get up early on weekends?

answers.content: Yes, of course! And there are probably two reasons. One is that as I usually start my day at around 6:30 am on weekdays, it is hard for me to sleep in on Saturdays and Sundays. The other reason is probably because I find it relaxing to get up early on those days. I feel as if my spare time is extended in that way.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do when you get up early?

answers.content: Well, I feel whenever I get out of bed early, it seems like I have all the time in the world! So, preparing myself for the day leisurely and having breakfast slowly are two of the things I do because I have plenty of time. Also, I may go for a run if the weather allows since there will be fewer runners outside in the early morning.

sub_questions.content: Do you often get up early in the morning?

answers.content: Actually, yes. I know a lot of youngsters nowadays tend to sleep in a little but I‘ve developed the habit of getting up really early. I mean, when I have to study/work in the morning, 6:30 is the time when I wake up. And even if it's a weekend or when I have nothing scheduled for that day, I still get up at around 7:00 am.

questions.content: What kinds of gifts are popular in your country?
What do you give others as gifts?
Do you like to send expensive gifts?
What’s the best present or gift you have ever received?

sub_questions.content: What kinds of gifts are popular in your country?

answers.content: In China, for traditional festivals, the majority of people still like to bring fruits or snacks when they visit family or friends. But many people, especially the young generation, like myself, also buy jewelry or clothes as gifts. Some people even just send coupons or movie tickets. These are common, I guess.

sub_questions.content: What do you give others as gifts?

answers.content: Well, as a student, I don't really have a big budget so I prefer meaningful but affordable items. I would just be frank with my friends and ask them what they need so that my gifts would not become a waste. Sometimes it’s just a cup, sometimes it’s a scarf, but I will always have this present personalized by attaching a handmade card saying how much I appreciate my friends.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to send expensive gifts?

answers.content: No, I don’t like it, but sometimes I have to. I have some friends who are pretty wealthy and often buy luxury items, like LV handbags, or Hugo Boss perfume. If what I buy for them is of a cheap price, they’ll probably feel insulted. So for them, I’d be ready to spend a huge amount of money on gifts even though I wish I didn’t have to.

sub_questions.content: What’s the best present or gift you have ever received?

answers.content: To be honest, I once got an amazon voucher from one of my best friends and I found it really useful. I mean, sometimes I get lovely gifts but often they just sit on the shelf forever, so with this voucher, I could buy things that I really needed.

questions.content: How often do you go for a run?
Where do you usually go running?
Do you think running is a good way to stay healthy?
Do you like running?

sub_questions.content: How often do you go for a run?

answers.content: Well, now I go jogging just twice a week. The mornings on weekdays are my preference because there are fewer people in the gym or the park, compared with weekends. But I used to go four times a week before, because recently I’ve had to prepare for this test in the morning, so I had to cut down the number of times.

sub_questions.content: Where do you usually go running?

answers.content: Personally, if the weather is nice and the air is clear, I like to go jogging in an open-air area like a park, because I enjoy working out in a natural environment. However, if it’s a winter morning or a terrible day, to avoid inhaling too much cold or dirty air, I will go to the gym and run on a treadmill.

sub_questions.content: Do you think running is a good way to stay healthy?

answers.content: Actually, I suppose for young people, it’s definitely a fantastic method to keep healthy because you don't have to have access to any expensive facilities or fancy equipment. All you need is just a pair of running shoes, so it’s a really affordable workout. But for senior people, running too much may hurt their joints so they should just run moderately.

sub_questions.content: Do you like running?

answers.content: Well, honestly, I’m a big fan of jogging because as a type of cardio exercise, it can benefit our health immensely. But the environment here makes running less enjoyable due to the air pollution and very limited space. I think if I want to do this sport, I might have to pay some money and go to the gym instead.

questions.content: Do you think you are a good friend to others?
What kind of people do you like to make friends with?
Do you keep in contact with friends from your childhood?
What do you think makes a good friend?

sub_questions.content: Do you think you are a good friend to others?

answers.content: Yes, I can proudly say so. I am sociable enough to know at first, lovable enough to get along with, and judicious enough to give a piece of advice. Many friends of mine seek advice from me, and generally, they take it into consideration. I guess that shows how close we are.

sub_questions.content: What kind of people do you like to make friends with?

answers.content: I like to make friends with honest and straightforward guys. Cuz I’m the hot-headed type of person. Once I was told to do something, I would do it blindly. If an honest and straightforward friend came giving me wise advice, I could stop myself from acting stupidly and take a wiser move.

sub_questions.content: Do you keep in contact with friends from your childhood?

answers.content: Yes, of course. My BFF, Jane, was my kindergarten mate. Isn’t it unbelievable? I have a lot of friends, but it is her who I want to share everything with. When every happy or sad moment came, I would think of her. And then she would listen so attentively. I guess that’s the key to our friendship. Always share and listen.

sub_questions.content: What do you think makes a good friend?

answers.content: Well, for me, good friends have something in common. They share a common passion for certain things, even minor things. For example, my best friend Jane and I are crazy about knitting. Although we’re no longer living together, we still share photos of the knitting we have recently finished with each other. Every time we talk about it, we can’t stop, and it’s this passion that ties us closer together when we’re apart.

questions.content: Do you like to smile?
When do people smile at others?
Do you smile when people take pictures of you?
Can you recognize a fake smile?

sub_questions.content: Do you like to smile?

answers.content: Yes, I do, especially when I meet someone I know or encounter a professor at university. On the one hand, I smile to show my politeness, and on the other, I intend to show my respect. Smiling also makes me happier and more optimistic about life.

sub_questions.content: When do people smile at others?

answers.content: People smile at others in many different situations. One of these situations is when people greet each other. For instance, people smile when they encounter a friend in the morning in the park. Smiling enables you to get closer to each other.

sub_questions.content: Do you smile when people take pictures of you?

answers.content: Yes, I do. The reason for this is that I personally think that smiling is the best human expression, which represents kindness, happiness and joy. With this belief, I always smile when pictures are being taken.

sub_questions.content: Can you recognize a fake smile?

answers.content: Well, I think I can, because fake smiles are obviously different from real smiles, and are quite easy to recognize. When someone pretends to smile, for example, his or her lips act very unnaturally, which makes it easy to notice, so I think I can recognize whether someone is smiling from the bottom of their hearts.

questions.content: Do you like handwriting？
Do you like to receive cards with hand-written words?
Is your handwriting easy to read for other people?
Do you often write with a pen?
Can we tell someone’s personality from his or her handwriting?
What’s the differences of writing with a pen and typing on a computer?

sub_questions.content: Do you like handwriting？

answers.content:
Yes I do, I write with a pen every day because I have a habit of journaling. Personally, I am a.
visual person, so handwriting gives me freedom when I want to draw. Also, writing by hand can help me avoid distractions, giving me a precious “ME” moment.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to receive cards with hand-written words?

answers.content: Actually, I would be extremely grateful if I got a hand-written card, because these kinds of cards mean that the person really cares about you and is willing to spend time on you. However, I suppose an electronic card might be more environmentally-friendly and also more dynamic since you can add sounds and videos to it.

sub_questions.content: Is your handwriting easy to read for other people?

answers.content: I believe so. Keeping a diary gives me the chance to practice my handwriting more often than other busy young people. So relatively speaking, I think my writing is quite intelligible. Although typing has gradually replaced writing by hand in almost every area of life, I still think it is necessary to have clear handwriting.

sub_questions.content: Do you often write with a pen?

answers.content: When I was younger, pens were one of my necessities in daily life. However, honestly, nowadays I rarely use a pen to write because typing on my laptop or my smart phone is much more convenient. However, I have the habit of keeping a diary which requires me to handwrite , so whenever I open it, I write with a pen.

sub_questions.content: Can we tell someone’s personality from his or her handwriting?

answers.content: Well, I can’t say for sure but I reckon that to some extent, a person’s handwriting can be a reflection of their personality. When seeing illegible and messy handwriting, we often assume that the writer is a bit impatient and hot-tempered. But when neat handwriting is presented, we may think it comes from a careful and serious person’s hand.

sub_questions.content: What’s the differences of writing with a pen and typing on a computer?

answers.content: There are so many distinct differences between them. The first that comes to mind is that writing by hand is more time-consuming while typing can be more efficient. Besides, handwriting varies from person to person, but when inputting on a laptop, if we choose the same font, we can produce the exact same thing. Another difference is that typing does not consume ink and paper but writing by hand does.

questions.content: Are you tidier at work or at home?
Do you think people should be tidy all the time?
How do you keep things tidy?
Are you a tidy person?

sub_questions.content: Are you tidier at work or at home?

answers.content: I’m tidier at home. At home, I’m a tidy person, while at the workplace, I’m just like everyone else. For me, the workplace is a place to show your efficiency and proficiency; spending too much time on cleaning is definitely uneconomic. That doesn’t mean that I refuse to clean my work desk, because the last task in my daily schedule is to remove all the things I don’t need.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people should be tidy all the time?

answers.content: No. It is unnecessary to keep yourself or your place tidy all the time. You clean the place when you are in a good mood, but there are times in your life when you need to loosen yourself and you won’t have energy for room-cleaning. I believe that sometimes you have to embrace things like your anger or sorrow, and messing up your surroundings is one of the effective ways.

sub_questions.content: How do you keep things tidy?

answers.content: I keep things tidy by always putting things back where they belong. I know it is always tempting to keep things just where they are, but by doing so, things will just become messier in your room. A second habit I have is to create a holding zone which is an area dedicated to temporarily containing things so that you don’t mingle organized stuff with unorganized stuff.

sub_questions.content: Are you a tidy person?

answers.content: Actually, I’m a messy person by nature. Ever since I was a kid, I’ve never tried to be organized but I’ve always known where my things are - under the bed, in the corner, or on the top of my cat’s head. As I’m fully aware of the whereabouts of my personal stuff, I get really angry at my mom when she tries to organize my room and get me out of the chaos I am so used to.

questions.content: What desserts do Chinese people like?
Have you ever made cakes?
Do you like eating cakes?
Do you like dessert?

sub_questions.content: What desserts do Chinese people like?

answers.content: In my opinion, different from the super sweet cakes that we often find in Western countries, desserts with just a small amount of sugar would be more attractive to Chinese people. Nowadays, people in China have started to have an increasing awareness of the importance of having a healthy diet, so they will choose less sugary stuff instead of just tasty food.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever made cakes?

answers.content: Well, I’m afraid I’ve never really made a cake from scratch because I’m terrible at baking. But there was one time when I decorated a cake with a group of friends. I cut some strawberries in half and put them on top of the cake, and then I drew a few stars with yellow cream.

sub_questions.content: Do you like eating cakes?

answers.content: Definitely, yes. I don't think I can live without them! When I was little, I had a preference for cupcakes because they are tiny and nicely-decorated with cute patterns like daisies on them. No child can resist that. Nowadays, dark chocolate layered cakes would be my first choice since they taste smooth and rich without being too sweet.

sub_questions.content: Do you like dessert?

answers.content: Actually, I have a really sweet tooth. I mean, desserts are basically my life! I think I started to love sweet things like candy and cakes when I was very little. Every time I ate out, I would attach special attention to the desserts on the menu. And both Chinese desserts and Western-style pastries appeal to me.

questions.content: Will you learn other languages in the future?
Do you think it’s difficult to learn a new language?
What languages can you speak?
Why do you learn English?

sub_questions.content: Will you learn other languages in the future?

answers.content: I might. Yeah, it’s possible that I may have to learn new languages at some point, especially Japanese because my sister has moved to Japan. She has been granted permanent residency. We are very close, and I do plan to visit her often in the future. So, I may take Japanese lessons in the future, just so I can enjoy my stay there.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it’s difficult to learn a new language?

answers.content: I think it’s difficult to truly master a language, yeah. I say so out of personal experience. A couple of years ago, I signed up for Spanish lessons. The beginning of it was so easy and interesting. However, I couldn’t carry on after two months when I had to make that weird tongue-twisting sound. Yeah, I was embarrassed to make that sound. I quit it. So no, I don’t think it’s easy to learn a new language.

sub_questions.content: What languages can you speak?

answers.content: I am fluent in both English and Mandarin Chinese. English has been my primary language since childhood, but I grew up in a bilingual household where Chinese was also commonly spoken. I have continued to study Chinese throughout my education and have also lived abroad in Chinese-speaking countries to gain proficiency.

Well, I can speak Mandarin, Cantonese and some English. Yeah, only some English. It’s not that I’m not familiar with English. I actually started to learn English when I was four or five years old. I can understand English when it’s spoken to me, and I read English magazines. However, speaking is never something I’ve been good at. In fact, I only started to speak English in recent years.

sub_questions.content: Why do you learn English?

answers.content: I chose to learn English because learning a language gives me a lot of satisfaction. I enjoy learning it, and I know that every hour I spend gets me closer to perfection. Besides, by learning English, I also learn about other cultures. Few experiences will make me grow as a person more than learning the values, habits and way of life in a culture that is different from mine.

questions.content: What do you do when visiting relatives?
Why do people visit their relatives?
Do you often visit your relatives?
When was the last time you visited a relative?

sub_questions.content: What do you do when visiting relatives?

answers.content: Frankly speaking, when I visit relatives I usually find it awkward and I’m at a loss to decide what to do. However, my mother always encourages me to talk with my cousins and play with them, which to some extent makes it a bit easier to kill the time.

sub_questions.content: Why do people visit their relatives?

answers.content: Personally speaking, I believe people visit their relatives in order to cultivate their kinships with them. You know, after all, we share the same blood and sometimes we need to strengthen our relationships and maintain a relatively strong connection.

sub_questions.content: Do you often visit your relatives?

answers.content: As I’ve just mentioned, the last time I visited a relative was almost a year ago, so I don’t visit my relatives very often because actually my family lives far away from them, and paying frequent visits seems a little unrealistic and unnecessary, so we’ve drifted apart somewhat. I know it’s not a good thing.

sub_questions.content: When was the last time you visited a relative?

answers.content: Well actually, the last visit was last year when I paid a visit to my cousin Rain, because he got 7. 5 in his IELTS test, so I just wanted him to teach me his “secret weapons”, you know, some useful tips for the examination. I think his suggestions were quite helpful and made me feel encouraged.

questions.content: How often do you write an email?
What kind of email do you receive that makes you happy?
Who do you write to?
Do you think it’s important to reply emails quickly?

sub_questions.content: How often do you write an email?

answers.content: I do that nearly every day. When I was in high school, I barely wrote emails but since I entered college, emails have become probably the commonest way to convey messages. Every day after breakfast, the first thing I would do is check my inbox so it has really been an essential part of my life.

sub_questions.content: What kind of email do you receive that makes you happy?

answers.content: Because of the fact that I have to receive plenty of emails every day, I suppose it is those well-organized ones that can make me feel delighted. If an email is well-written, with very standard format and clear purposes, I will find it easy to comprehend and the important messages will not be missed so I prefer the emails with high readability.

sub_questions.content: Who do you write to?

answers.content: There are two kinds of people who I write to. One is those I work with and the other is my international friends. Writing emails is a quite formal action so it is used in my workplace. Also, when communicating with foreign friends, I find that we use different types of social applications so the emails are the common and most stable way for us to keep in touch.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it’s important to reply emails quickly?

answers.content: Well, I suppose the time of reply is not that significant when it comes to emails. Usually emergent messages which need to be replied immediately will be delivered through text messages or phone calls and only those official notices will be sent through emails. Sending emails means that we should write back carefully and seriously but not necessarily to be quick.

questions.content: Which do you prefer, reading magazines or newspapers?
Do you prefer to read news online or on newspaper?
Do you often read newspapers?
Are you very interested in the news?
Why do you want to know the latest news?
How important is it to you to get the news every day?
What sort of news are you most interested in?
What kinds of (types of) newspapers (or magazines) do you usually read?
How old were you when you first started to read newspapers?
Why do you think people read newspapers?
What different types of newspapers are there in China?
Do you care about the news?
Do you think it is important to read newspapers? (Why?/ Why not?)

sub_questions.content: Which do you prefer, reading magazines or newspapers?

answers.content: As far as I am concerned, magazines and newspapers bear a lot of similarities as well as differences. When it occurs to me, I enjoy magazines better than newspapers. First, magazines are richer in pictures than newspapers, which fit better to my reading habit. Second, magazines are more vivid in narration when newspapers are too serious and didactic sometimes. Besides, newspapers are published every day and magazines are usually published weekly or monthly. So it is more environmental friendly to read magazines instead of newspapers.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to read news online or on newspaper?

answers.content: Personally, reading online news would be my preference. I used to read from a newspaper but the advances of technology really have made my reading experience more diverse and dynamic. I mean online news are inexhaustible and usually contain links to other related information. So, for me it's a pleasure to retrieve news from websites.

sub_questions.content: Do you often read newspapers?

answers.content: I read newspaper every now and then. To tell you the truth, reading newspaper has been my habit, which was developed by my parents, ever since I was a child. My family subscribed to the newspaper and it was delivered to our door every morning. I usually read political column in the morning and entertainment column in the evening.

sub_questions.content: Are you very interested in the news?

answers.content: Absolutely. To be precise, I am an avid reader of news. For one thing, I am so curious about the changing world that I’m eager to know what is happening outside. For another, reading news bring on my reading and writing skills as well as my horizon. Last but not least, news can be a good topic when I chat with others.

sub_questions.content: Why do you want to know the latest news?

answers.content: Different people read news out of different purposes. As far as I am concerned, I want to know the latest news for three reasons. For one thing, the latest news is an access to the dynamic world. It breaks the limitation of location and time. For another, I can have my finger on the pulse in this way. Besides, news is shorter in length and it is fully fit for the tense pace of life.

sub_questions.content: How important is it to you to get the news every day?

answers.content: To tell the truth, it is crucial for me to get the news every day. For I trade stocks and make some other investments from time to time, it is essential for me to have my finger on the pulse. The news can reflect the macro economic environment to some degree and thus can be treated as predictive factors of the trend of the market. By having a close eye on the news, I can minimise my investing risks and get the best return.

sub_questions.content: What sort of news are you most interested in?

answers.content: There are different sorts of news, which cater to different target groups, in the newspaper, like political news, entertainment news, military news and economic news. As far as I am concerned, the local news jumps with my appetite. On the one hand, local news goes in hand with my daily life. I can know exactly what is happening around me. On the other hand, there is always plenty of jargon in economic news and political news that bewilders me every now and then. Besides, local news is practical, for there is always information of sales promotion in the neighbourhood.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of (types of) newspapers (or magazines) do you usually read?

answers.content: There are various kinds of magazines on sale, which target to different groups of customers, like travel journals, science brochures and novel collections. As far as I am concerned, fashion magazines are my cup of tea. First, they arouse my love of beauty. I can keep an optimistic attitude towards life via reading fashion magazines. Second, I learn from the models to pick and match clothes for myself. Besides, many friends of mine have a fancy on fashion which is a common topic in our conversation.

sub_questions.content: How old were you when you first started to read newspapers?

answers.content: It was ten years ago that I began to read newspaper when I just entered the middle school. It was a task arranged by our dean of students that every one should take turns collecting and classifying news and share it with all our classmates. This activity aimed to develop the habit of reading newspaper and sharing knowledge with others. And as a result, I began to read news from then on.

sub_questions.content: Why do you think people read newspapers?

answers.content: Different people read newspaper out of different purposes. From my point of view, their motivations can be divided into the following groups. First, some people regard newspaper as a connection with the outside world. Newspaper, which breaks the limitation of space and time, brings all the amazing and astonishing news to them. Second, some people read newspaper to civilize themselves. Newspaper is an access for the populace to promote their cultural literacy. Besides, some people just treat newspaper as entertainment tools, which are used to kill time after dinner.

sub_questions.content: What different types of newspapers are there in China?

answers.content: Newspaper has been an important method of spreading information and civilizing the populace. There are hundreds of kinds of newspaper published by various institutions in China, which can be divided into several categories. The first sort, political newspaper, usually published by governmental institutions, serves as the tool of passing down the will of the governors. There is also life service newspaper, which contains practical information in daily life. Besides, there is academic newspaper on which latest researches are published.

sub_questions.content: Do you care about the news?

answers.content: As to me, I care about the news considerably. For I am trading stocks and making some other investments, reading news can keep me alerted to the dynamic macroeconomic environment. Also, news can break the limitation of time and space and bring to me the adventure of the others. Last but not least, news is an important source of daily chats. It is easy to start a small talk with the latest news.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important to read newspapers? (Why?/ Why not?)

answers.content: As to me, reading newspaper is of great significance, especially for the young generation. In the digital era, traditional paper media is losing its leading position. An increasing number of people have switched to reading online. However, every coin has two sides. The scientific survey has showed that the young generation is suffering from reading and writing problems. Reading newspaper can not only improve this situation, but improve their awareness of social responsibility as well.

questions.content: Have you ever watched a live show?
Do you like to watch comedies?
Do your friends like to tell jokes?
Are you good at telling jokes?

sub_questions.content: Have you ever watched a live show?

answers.content: Yes, I have. I watched two Broadway shows in New York. Watching a Broadway show is practically a must-do for visitors there. The recent one I watched is a long-running favourite called The Phantom of the Opera. I had watched the movie version before but the live show is definitely much more enjoyable, with fantastic stage effects and great actors with good acting and good voices. The only shortcoming was that it was hard for me to see the facial expressions of the actors from my seat.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to watch comedies?

answers.content: I’m a great fan of American TV. “Friends” remains my all-time favourite, even 15 years after it ended. I’m a bit old school, so you may not appreciate a few of my choices. I also like “Two and a Half Men” although I haven't watched it after they dumped Charlie Sheen. In comedy movies, I’m an unabashed admirer of all Hugh Grant’s comedies. Notting Hill, Music and Lyrics and Nine Months remain my favourites.

sub_questions.content: Do your friends like to tell jokes?

answers.content: Some of them do, especially my friend Charlie. He is a hilarious guy. He can also be scary. We sometimes can’t tell if he’s trying to be funny or being unreasonable. I can figure him out pretty well after all these years we’ve spent together, and I sometimes give a two-man comic show with him to make fun of our friends.

sub_questions.content: Are you good at telling jokes?

answers.content: Yes, I guess so. I tell a great deal of jokes and try to remember the jokes I hear at any given time. Jokes can make you seem witty, boring, or even creepy depending upon what kind of joke you tell. You must know the audience and remember you’re trying to be funny, not offensive. I always avoid jokes about nationality, ethnicity or race. I never want to offend someone with a joke that is offensive, not funny.

questions.content: What do you think is the best age to learn driving?
How do you feel about getting older?
Should we treat people of different ages in the same way?

sub_questions.content: What do you think is the best age to learn driving?

answers.content: Personally, I learned driving after going to university because that was when I had more time. But if we don't take time into consideration, I guess 16 is probably an appropriate age to do it because that's when our bodies become mature enough to maneuver a vehicle. It’s never too early to learn a skill that is helpful for a lifetime.

sub_questions.content: How do you feel about getting older?

answers.content: Generally speaking, I suppose it is a positive thing to become more mature. I do understand the benefits of youth but I believe that what time can give us is not only wrinkles but also wisdom. Normally when people get older, they also get more experienced and financially independent, which means they can make sensible decisions and be responsible for their own life.

sub_questions.content: Should we treat people of different ages in the same way?

answers.content: Well, I guess there should be an age span. I mean people between 30 and 60 should probably be treated equally, but for young children or young adults, and senior people, it might be more reasonable to consider their needs separately because they may not be as mentally or physically capable.

questions.content: What kind of water sports do you want to try?
Have you ever done any water sports?
What water sports do you like doing?
Are water sports popular in China?

sub_questions.content: What kind of water sports do you want to try?

answers.content: Well, I think for all the sports that I’ve done, there’s nothing really that thrilling. I mean I’ve only tried simple things like swimming or diving, so given the chance, I would really like to try some extreme sports such as surfing or speedboating. Those type of activities really appeal to me.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever done any water sports?

answers.content: Yes, definitely! As I said before, I’ve been swimming for ages, which keeps me healthy and fit. Apart from that, I’ve also tried other sorts of aquatic sports. For example, I went boating with my friends last week on a lake in the central park. Rowing on the surface of the calm lake made me feel really refreshed.

sub_questions.content: What water sports do you like doing?

answers.content: For me, I reckon swimming is probably my favorite. I learned to swim when I was in primary school and I’ve been doing this exercise ever since then. Also, I’m able to swim in different styles such as breaststroke and butterfly. I think swimming is a really beneficial workout since it enhances our body without hurting any joints.

sub_questions.content: Are water sports popular in China?

answers.content: Well, it depends on the region. China is an enormous country so the regions vary geographically. The coastal cities enjoy nice beaches so sometimes people there go diving or swimming. But for some inland cities, it would be hard to find vast open water, so aquatic sports are less common in those areas.

questions.content: Where do you usually go for a walk?
Do you think people will walk more or not in the future?
Do you walk more often now than in the past?
Do you walk a lot?

sub_questions.content: Where do you usually go for a walk?

answers.content: Well, it depends on where I am living. If I’m living at home, I normally just walk on the street near my home. Or sometimes I take a stroll to the shopping area to kill time. But when I’m at school, I just walk in the playground with my friends. Sometimes we even go for a brisk walk around the playground.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people will walk more or not in the future?

answers.content: From what I can see, we probably won’t be able to walk much in the future, given the bustling city life that we are heading into, you know. People already spend so much time indoors. Well, we work on computers and entertain ourselves on electronic devices. How could we spare time to walk in the future? Actually, it may be a surprise if we could make time for walking at all!

sub_questions.content: Do you walk more often now than in the past?

answers.content: I suppose I do, yeah. I prefer going for long walks with my friends and even sometimes alone, when I need to clear my thoughts. I think walking can be a great way to bond with people, you know, because it’s a time when you slow down and actually talk. When I was a child though, I didn’t have the need or desire to do so. In fact, I was pretty reluctant to do any physical exercise back then. The few times when I actually convinced myself into walking was when I got to experience the greenery in the countryside while visiting my grandma.

sub_questions.content: Do you walk a lot?

answers.content: I suppose I do, yeah. I mean, I always prefer to travel by foot when it’s a short distance. And I take multiple short trips every day. Like, you know, I go to the library, the classroom, the school mart and the dining room on foot every day. On weekends, I sometimes hang out with my friends in the city, where it is too crowded to drive. So yeah, in that sense, I guess it’s fair to say that I do walk a lot.

questions.content: Do you want to be a superstar?
Are international superstars popular in your country?
Who is your favourite movie star?
Have you ever met a celebrity/superstar in real life?

sub_questions.content: Do you want to be a superstar?

answers.content: Definitely no! Being a celebrity means that I might lose my privacy and I really detest the feeling of being stared by the paparazzi. And it is very likely that I would be criticized by some people who know me only from the distorted reports in the attention-grabbing tabloids. Although being famous can bring a large amount of money, I still don't want to sacrifice my personal life.

sub_questions.content: Are international superstars popular in your country?

answers.content: I suppose so. As more and more international movies and TV programs are being introduced into Chinese market, we have more exposure to foreign superstars so undoubtedly, they are gaining popularity in China. Most of my friends are passionate about some international celebrities like Mr. Bean, Nicole Kidman and of course, my favorite actor Tom Cruise.

sub_questions.content: Who is your favourite movie star?

answers.content: I think Tom Cruise is probably the movie star who can attract my attention most. His appearance and acting skills are the most appealing things to me. In the movie series Mission: Impossible, his excellent performance really impressed me. By the way, his latest movie also made me more respectful to him because he did lots of extremely difficult movements although he is almost 60-year-old.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever met a celebrity/superstar in real life?

answers.content: Yes, I have! But it was quite unintentional. It was about three years ago, my friends and I went to the cinema for the latest movie. When we were waiting outside, suddenly a sharp scream caught my attention. Then I found that there was a large group of people holding posters in their hands and among them was a famous actor. That is how I met a celebrity accidentally.

questions.content: When was the last time you visited a museum?
Do you often visit museums?
Do you think museums are important?
Do you think there should be more museums in your hometown?
Are there any museums in your hometown?
How often do you visit museums?（or Do you often visit a museum）
Do you like visiting museums? Why?

sub_questions.content: When was the last time you visited a museum?

answers.content: It was during my last summer vacation. At the time there was a new museum in our city. It was a special one because it wasn’t about history, culture or science. Instead, it was about people who had broken up. In this museum. I saw many words and stories from these disheartened people, and I could really feel their disappointment and agony due to their breakups.

sub_questions.content: Do you often visit museums?

answers.content: Actually, I don't visit museums very often because all the museums are very far away from my home. And I'm quite busy with my studies, so I don't have much time to visit them. However, I still enjoy visiting them because I think they’re magical places where I can learn about our ancestors.

sub_questions.content: Do you think museums are important?

answers.content: Yes. I think museums are very important because one can learn many things there. When visiting museums, people can learn history, geography, culture, art, science, and so on. Nowadays, museums are becoming more and more interesting by holding many interactive activities and games to engage visitors. These special features are attractive as well as educational.

sub_questions.content: Do you think there should be more museums in your hometown?

answers.content: I would say yes! Museums usually display historical objects and preserve the unique culture and history of an area, and more museums will allow an increasing number of people to visit and remind them, the next generation in particular, of what happened in this place in the past, and I believe this could help them better understand the local culture.

sub_questions.content: Are there any museums in your hometown?

answers.content: Yes, there are several well-known museums in my city. The most famous one is The Provincial Museum and there are numerous unique exhibits all year round, which people cannot enjoy in other places. Because of this, each year, tourists at home and abroad go to appreciate the artworks and ancient items in this museum.

sub_questions.content: How often do you visit museums?（or Do you often visit a museum）

answers.content: It depends. Since I'm quite busy with my studies and seldom have time to go out, I rarely visit museums. To be honest, I only occasionally visit special exhibitions held in museums. But, as I just mentioned, if I travel to another place, I'll definitely visit more museums than usual.

sub_questions.content: Do you like visiting museums? Why?

answers.content: Yes, I like to visit different kinds of museums because it's a good way to see art. More importantly, visiting local museums helps us get to know a new place quickly. So, when I go to a new place for traveling, the first thing I usually do is to go to the museums so as to understand the local culture and customs.

questions.content: Do you prefer to read news online or in a newspaper?
Which do you prefer, reading magazines or newspapers?
How old were you when you first started to read newspapers?
Do you think it is important to read newspapers? (Why?/ Why not?)
Why do you think people read newspapers?
Do you prefer to read newspapers or magazines?
What are the differences between newspapers and magazines?
What do you think of newspapers?

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to read news online or in a newspaper?

answers.content: Personally, reading online news would be my preference. I used to read the newspaper but the advances in technology have really made my reading experience more diverse and dynamic. I mean online news is inexhaustible and usually contains links to other related information. So, for me it's a pleasure to retrieve news from websites.

sub_questions.content: Which do you prefer, reading magazines or newspapers?

answers.content: As far as I’m concerned, magazines and newspapers bear a lot of similarities as well as differences. When it comes to me, I enjoy magazines better than newspapers. First, magazines are richer in pictures than newspapers, which fit better into my reading habit. Second, magazines are more vivid in narration while newspapers are too serious and didactic sometimes. Besides, newspapers are published every day and magazines are usually published weekly or monthly. So it is more environmentally friendly to read magazines instead of newspapers.

sub_questions.content: How old were you when you first started to read newspapers?

answers.content: It was ten years ago when I began to read newspapers when I’d just entered middle school. It was a task arranged by our dean of students that everyone should take turns collecting and classifying news and share it with all our classmates. This activity aimed to develop the habit of reading newspapers and sharing knowledge with others. As a result, I began to read news from then on.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important to read newspapers? (Why?/ Why not?)

answers.content: For me, reading newspapers is of great significance, especially for the younger generation. In the digital era, traditional paper media is losing its leading position. An increasing number of people have switched to reading online. However, every coin has two sides. Scientific surveys have shown that the younger generation is suffering from reading and writing problems. Reading newspapers can not only improve this situation, but improve their awareness of social responsibility as well.

sub_questions.content: Why do you think people read newspapers?

answers.content: Different people read newspapers for different purposes. From my point of view, their motivations can be divided into the following groups. First, some people regard newspapers as a connection with the outside world. Newspapers, which break the limitation of space and time, bring all the amazing and astonishing news to them. Second, some people read newspapers to civilize themselves. They provide access for the populace to promote their cultural literacy. Besides both of these, some people just treat them as entertainment tools which they use to kill time after dinner.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to read newspapers or magazines?

answers.content: For me, I prefer to read newspapers more. Magazines target a specific audience based on the information being provided. So, if the magazine I get is not my scope, it is not fun to read. Newspapers offer more information in short articles and I can learn about the current news and find information that interests me.

sub_questions.content: What are the differences between newspapers and magazines?

answers.content: There are distinct differences between the two. Newspapers are published more frequently and thus the content is mainly current and ongoing news with short articles. Magazines, on the other hand, have more specific content such as fashion, sports or technology with longer articles. Newspapers are generally cheaper and some are even free. Magazines, on the other hand, are more expensive, which is due to their content as well as layout. Magazines have a complex design and more pages.

sub_questions.content: What do you think of newspapers?

answers.content: Although we are used to phones and the internet, newspapers are still an important means of conveying information to the general public. The good thing with a newspaper is it is very easy to access. Newspapers are published frequently, with most being daily or weekly, and are generally affordable or even free. For me, it is a primary source of reliable and current news, and it is quick to read as it often contains short articles.

questions.content: Do you like insects?
Are there many different insects where you live?
Do you think insects are useful?

sub_questions.content: Do you like insects?

answers.content: Well, to be honest, I am not really a big fan of insects, but I am aware that they are actually quite powerful. I’ve seen how little ants carry an object much larger than themselves in groups. That is just mind-blowing. But although I know these tiny little creatures cannot threaten my life, they sometimes frighten me a lot.

sub_questions.content: Are there many different insects where you live?

answers.content: I suppose there are a large number of insects around my neighborhood because there is a park with a variety of plants next to my place. But I don't really see them very often since their sizes are too tiny to be spotted. I need to look for them intentionally to see some.

sub_questions.content: Do you think insects are useful?

answers.content: Actually, although I understand that there has to be some reason for insects to exist, I cannot come up with any practical contributions they have made to my life. I guess they are helpful in maintaining the balance of our ecosystem and also in biodiversity, but in terms of our daily life, I don't see any direct positive impacts.

questions.content: What are the most beautiful sights you have seen while travelling?
Do you like to take scenery pictures?
Do you prefer the mountains or the sea?
Do you look out the window at the scenery when travelling by bus or car?
Is there good scenery in cities?
Do you like to take pictures of good scenery with your smart phone? Why?
Is there good scenery in your hometown?
When you travel, do you like to live in hotels with scenic views?

sub_questions.content: What are the most beautiful sights you have seen while travelling?

answers.content: One of the most stunning sights I’ve witnessed was the Northern Lights in Iceland; their vibrant colors dancing across the night sky felt magical. Additionally, the serene cherry blossoms in Japan during spring create a breathtaking spectacle. These experiences showed me nature’s incredible artistry from very different perspectives.

One of the most stunning sights I’ve encountered was the sunrise over the Grand Canyon. The way the light gradually illuminated the vast, rugged landscape was breathtaking. It wasn’t just the scenery but the peaceful atmosphere that made it unforgettable. Moments like that truly show nature’s incredible beauty.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to take scenery pictures?

answers.content: Yes, I enjoy capturing scenic photographs because they allow me to preserve beautiful moments in nature. For instance, during my last trip to the mountains, I took pictures of the sunrise that showcased stunning colors. It’s a relaxing hobby that also helps me see the world from different perspectives.

Absolutely, I enjoy capturing scenic photos, especially during my hiking trips in the mountains. The way sunlight filters through the trees fascinates me. Photographing nature not only relaxes me but also allows me to preserve beautiful moments that I can share with friends and family later.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer the mountains or the sea?

answers.content: Although both landscapes have their charm, I find the mountains more appealing. The fresh air and panoramic views offer a peaceful retreat from city life. For instance, hiking up a mountain trail allows me to connect with nature in a way the sea’s endless horizon doesn’t quite provide. This sense of adventure fascinates me.

Honestly, I’m more drawn to the sea because its vastness always calms my mind. While mountains offer breathtaking views and fresh air, the sound of waves really helps me relax after a stressful week. Plus, I enjoy beach walks and collecting shells, which makes seaside trips feel more personal and refreshing.

sub_questions.content: Do you look out the window at the scenery when travelling by bus or car?

answers.content: Yes, I often find myself gazing out the window while traveling by bus or car. Observing the changing landscapes helps me relax and pass time. For instance, on a recent bus trip through the countryside, I enjoyed spotting various farms and wildlife, which made the journey more enjoyable and interesting.

Yes, I often find myself gazing out the window during bus rides, as it allows me to observe the changing landscapes and catch glimpses of everyday life in different neighborhoods. However, when I’m driving a car, my focus is primarily on the road for safety reasons, so I rarely get the chance to appreciate the scenery around me.

sub_questions.content: Is there good scenery in cities?

answers.content: Yes, definitely. As I’ve mentioned before, lots of gardens and parks have been built in my city and I think this might be the trend for all cities. Considering that many people working in offices rarely have time to enjoy good landscapes in the suburbs, the government has attached great importance to creating nice natural scenery just inside cities. So, an increasing number of parks have been created in recent years in cities.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to take pictures of good scenery with your smart phone? Why?

answers.content: Well, actually I’m really enthusiastic about taking pictures, but when it comes to scenery, I think a professional camera would be a better choice. I don’t think smart phones have the ability to truly record the beauty of nature. However, if a camera isn’t affordable, smart phones can also get the job done.

sub_questions.content: Is there good scenery in your hometown?

answers.content: I suppose so. We’ve got nice parks in the city, including a botanic garden, where you can find amazing natural beauty. Apart from that, a 20-minute drive takes you to our nearest suburb, where there’s a small mountain with a winding river. And if you’re a fan of pretty beaches, an hour’s drive will give you just that, with a view of the dazzling sand.

sub_questions.content: When you travel, do you like to live in hotels with scenic views?

answers.content: I believe most people prefer to have accommodation with nice views and I’m, of course, no exception. You know, traveling can be exhausting sometimes, so when I get back to the hotel, a really stunning view through the window or balcony would probably relieve my tiredness and restore my energy. And for sure, waking up with a gorgeous view outside is one of the best things about a trip.

questions.content: Is it difficult for you to stay focused on something?
What do you do to help you concentrate?
What may distract you when you are trying to stay focused？
When do you need to be focused?

sub_questions.content: Is it difficult for you to stay focused on something?

answers.content: Normally it’s okay. If I’m really keen on something, I don’t really need any assistance in keeping my concentration. For instance, if I’m reading a book that I really like, I’d probably skip meals(忘食) simply because I forget them completely. But sometimes when I’m tired and still need to work, I need an absolutely quiet room to make sure I won’t get distracted.

sub_questions.content: What do you do to help you concentrate?

answers.content: Mostly I just move my mobile and other devices to another room. Cuz the way I see it, the biggest distraction nowadays would be social networks, they are like heroin(海洛因) for your brain. If my phone is within my reach, I can never really focus. At the very least, I mute it and flip it over(翻过去).

sub_questions.content: What may distract you when you are trying to stay focused？

answers.content: Well, a sudden and big noise, for instance. Say, if I am trying to work out an academic problem, and there is a huge thud(砰地一声) in the living room, of course, I’d stop working and start wondering if my dad clumsily(笨手笨脚地) tripped over himself, like, again. I’d have to check if there’s a problem, and that’s just human nature.

sub_questions.content: When do you need to be focused?

answers.content: You mean, apart from when I’m studying? Uh, I don’t know, when I’m trying to complete something dangerous, like chopping carrots, or sewing(缝) something. Cuz, apparently, I may get injured by knives or needles if I don’t fully concentrate. It did happen last summer. One afternoon I was cooking while talking to my mom, when suddenly I cut my finger. Fortunately, it wasn’t a deep cut.

questions.content: Do you prefer to talk or to listen?
Do you change your opinions frequently?
Have your discussion topics changed since you were a child?
What do you like to talk about?

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer to talk or to listen?

answers.content: It depends on the environment. If I am with a crowd of people, I prefer listening to the conversation more than talk.  Each and every individual is different with a unique story, and I love listening to all those voices, but I feel nervous when speaking in front of too many people. If I am with a few close friends, I like to share my stories and opinions.

sub_questions.content: Do you change your opinions frequently?

answers.content: I don’t think I change my opinions frequently because I rarely make comments on things that I’m not familiar with. What’s more, my argument is often well-supported by logic and evidence. As a result, it is often my friends who change their opinions frequently. However, I do make mistakes sometimes, and in such cases, I’m not afraid to change my opinion if I’m presented with a more convincing argument.

sub_questions.content: Have your discussion topics changed since you were a child?

answers.content: Yes, I think my discussion topics have changed a lot since I was a child. As a child, topics revolved around classmates, teachers and cartoons, but now I’m grown up and know more about the world, my friends and I often discuss topical issues like the covid-19 outbreak, the election of Donald Trump and Brexit.

sub_questions.content: What do you like to talk about?

answers.content: I like to talk about TV dramas because they’re an important source of joy for me and a shared interest between me and my friends. When my friends and I come together, we would talk about the themes, main characters and storylines. By doing so, I feel more connected with them and I get to know them from their unique point of view.

questions.content: What did you do last time you were by yourself?
Do you usually spend time by yourself?
Do you like spending time by yourself?
How do you usually spend your time by yourself?

sub_questions.content: What did you do last time you were by yourself?

answers.content: Well, actually, I’m a fairly solitary person. I mean I frequently spend time alone and enjoy it very much. I guess the last time I was all by myself was just last night when I was doing some final preparations for this speaking test. I went through some sample questions and answered them one by one with a glass of warm milk in my hand because it helped me calm down and concentrate.

sub_questions.content: Do you usually spend time by yourself?

answers.content: Yes, absolutely. Although I have a few roommates/flatmates, we have totally different schedules so we hardly meet, so normally I go to class/work by myself and come back and do my own stuff without any company. Only when it’s the weekend or vacation will I hang out with some friends or my family members.

sub_questions.content: Do you like spending time by yourself?

answers.content: For sure. I feel I can work/study with greater efficiency like this. What I mean to say is that I do enjoy my friends’company, but sometimes when I’m working on some important projects, I find it quite difficult to focus with other people around. I reckon spending time in solitude can really help me concentrate since there are much fewer distractions.

sub_questions.content: How do you usually spend your time by yourself?

answers.content: Well, there are several things I like doing when I’m just by myself. The first thing would be reading. When reading alone, I feel more engaged in the stories and can think deeper. Also, I’m very enthusiastic about drawing, which makes me a more inventive and expressive person. Another activity I would definitely do is cooking, and even though it’s a bit time-consuming, it’s also fun.

questions.content: Does your voice sound similar to your parents?
Do you enjoy recording your voice and listening to it?
Has your voice ever changed?
Do you like your own voice?

sub_questions.content: Does your voice sound similar to your parents?

answers.content: No, not at all. My mother speaks with an accent, but it still sounds clear and sweet. If she was a saleswoman, I suppose she would get the best sales figures amongst others. One can’t say NO to such a pleasant voice. My father has such a low and deep voice. He never barks to show he is unhappy, but he simply clears his throat. Interestingly, my voice is neither sweet nor persuasive, just the middle type.

sub_questions.content: Do you enjoy recording your voice and listening to it?

answers.content: Nuh... That could be a bit narcissistic(自恋的), from my point of view. As I’ve mentioned, I don’t find my voice especially charming. But I do, sometimes, listen to my voice messages to others in WeChat, just making sure, you know, that there wasn’t any mistake or disturbing(令人不适的) noise.

sub_questions.content: Has your voice ever changed?

answers.content: Yeah, I think so, but I assume basically everybody goes through this period when their voices change dramatically(剧烈地), like, for boys the pitch goes down, and for girls quite the contrary. That happens around adolescence(青春期), typically. I was 13 when my voice broke,. But nothing has changed ever after.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your own voice?

answers.content: Well... I don’t know, actually... I mean, I don’t think I dislike it(否定转移，我也没有不喜欢), it’s just that I don’t think there is anything special about my voice. Normally, we would describe a great voice as ‘magnetic’, or ‘three-dimensional’, as if it comes from a high-end acoustic device(声学设备), like a stereo. I don’t really think my voice has those qualities.

questions.content: How often do you use social networking apps?
Why do you use social networking apps?
What are the disadvantages of social networking apps?
Do you think it is good to make friends online?

sub_questions.content: How often do you use social networking apps?

answers.content: Well, I have to admit that I’m among those addicted to social networking apps. On average, I use them nearly 4 hours every single day. I use them to communicate with my friends, my teachers and even my family, and I also rely on them to reach new friends who share the same hobbies as me.

sub_questions.content: Why do you use social networking apps?

answers.content: For me, one of the reasons I use social networking apps is that they make it more convenient to communicate and interact with my friends, and they enable me to get to know more friends residing far away. Also, I enjoy using them since they give me a sense of belonging and friendship.

sub_questions.content: What are the disadvantages of social networking apps?

answers.content: Well, I think one of the greatest disadvantages of social networking apps is that they can make people become addicted and prevent them from enjoying their real life. For instance, many of my friends spend so much time online that they fail to have real-life communication with their peers.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is good to make friends online?

answers.content: From my point of view, I think it’s good to make friends online, mainly because for me, making friends online enables me to get to know people from different backgrounds without any limits imposed by space or time. Also, communicating online can avoid embarrassment and make it easier to make friends.

questions.content: Do you like getting up early in the morning?
What do you usually do in the morning?
What did you do in the morning when you were little? Why?
Are there any differences between what you do in the morning now and what you did in the past?
Do you spend your mornings doing the same things on both weekends and weekdays? Why or why not?

sub_questions.content: Do you like getting up early in the morning?

answers.content: Honestly, waking up early isn’t really my thing because I feel more energetic in the evening. However, on days when I have important tasks, I make an effort to get up early, as it helps me start the day productively. For example, during exam preparation, early mornings are when I focus best.

Honestly, waking up early isn’t my favorite thing because I’m more of a night owl. However, on days when I have important tasks, getting up early helps me feel more productive and energized. Sometimes, the quiet morning hours allow me to enjoy a peaceful start before the day gets busy. Overall, I appreciate early mornings occasionally but not every day.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do in the morning?

answers.content: In the mornings, I often start my day with a brisk walk around the park. This not only helps me wake up but also clears my mind for the day ahead. Sometimes, I prefer to enjoy a quiet breakfast while reading the news, which keeps me informed and focused. Overall, these routines set a positive tone for my entire day.

In the mornings, I typically start by stretching to wake my body up, then enjoy a cup of green tea while reading the news. Sometimes, I go for a jog in the nearby park to energize myself. This routine helps me feel refreshed and ready to tackle the day ahead with focus and enthusiasm.

sub_questions.content: What did you do in the morning when you were little? Why?

answers.content: When I was little, my mornings usually began with a quick breakfast, followed by playing outside with my friends. We enjoyed riding bikes and exploring nearby parks, which was exciting and helped me stay active. My parents encouraged this routine because they believed fresh air and exercise were essential for my growth and happiness.

When I was little, my mornings usually began with a family breakfast because my parents believed it was important to start the day together. After eating, I often played outside with my friends to enjoy the fresh air and sunshine before school. This routine helped me feel energized and happy throughout the day.

sub_questions.content: Are there any differences between what you do in the morning now and what you did in the past?

answers.content: I think the biggest difference between what I do in the morning now and what I did in the past is that I now do things all by myself in metropolitan cities, whereas in the past I would be accompanied by my extended family members i. e my sister and my grandpa.

Certainly! Compared to the past, my morning routine has changed quite a bit. Previously, I used to rush through breakfast, but now I take time to prepare a healthy meal. Also, I’ve started doing some light exercise, which energizes me for the day ahead. These small adjustments have made my mornings more enjoyable and productive.

sub_questions.content: Do you spend your mornings doing the same things on both weekends and weekdays? Why or why not?

answers.content: What I do on weekend mornings is different from what I do in the mornings during the week. You know, as a student, I’m often quite busy on weekday mornings, getting dressed, getting my backpack ready, having breakfast and so on. But on the weekend, I like to have relaxed mornings, like going for a run or visiting the library.

I don’t spend my mornings doing the same things on the weekends. On weekdays, I prefer to get up early to make my own breakfasts. Whereas on weekends, I like to sleep in to recoup some sleep debt. For me, sleeping is crucial to helping my body recover from the weekday schedule.

What I do on weekend mornings is different from what I do in the mornings during the week. I'm still a student, so Monday to Friday mornings I get up at 7 am to go to class. But I don't have classes on weekends, so I don't need to get up early. Usually I go for a run on Saturdays, and then I spend the rest of the morning in the library. On Sundays, I usually sleep in late.

questions.content: Do you do some cooking/help your family cook at home now?

Do you want to learn how to cook well?
Do you think your home is clean and tidy?
What housework do you like or dislike doing?

sub_questions.content: Do you do some cooking/help your family cook at home now?

answers.content: Yes, I cook from time to time now, and as a matter of fact, I just started learning to cook earlier this year, due to the pandemic and city lockdown. My dad used to do the cooking, and he was really a great chef, however, I DO want to share some of the family chores, so I bought ingredients online and learned a few recipes. So far, I have been improving a lot and it is exciting.

sub_questions.content: Did you do some house cleaning when you were young?

sub_questions.content: Do you have breakfast at home every day?

answers.content: Well, most of the time I do have breakfast at home. I find it more convenient and healthier. I usually have some bread, a glass of milk, and maybe some fruit. It gives me the energy I need to start my day. But on some really busy mornings, I might grab a quick snack on the go.

sub_questions.content: Do you want to learn how to cook well?

Therefore, I have recently made up my mind to learn one recipe each weekend, and to ask my friends for their reviews. I look forward to making healthy and tasty dishes all by myself soon.

sub_questions.content: Do you think your home is clean and tidy?

In my eyes I feel my home is clean and tidy, but in my mother's eyes my home is dirty and messy. I wash the dishes immediately after every meal and my clothes are always neatly folded.

sub_questions.content: What housework do you like or dislike doing?

Frankly speaking, I dislike all kinds of housework! If I have to choose, the one I dislike most, I would have to say cleaning the dishes after a meal. I like cooking a lot and I usually use different plates and bowls when preparing. So after I cook, there will be various kitchen utensils waiting for me to wash. It is annoying sometimes.

sub_questions.content: Did you do any household chores when you were young?

sub_questions.content: Do you have breakfast at home everyday?

sub_questions.content: What technology do you often use, computers or cellphones?

Especially during the pandemic period. Besides, it can record my data when I exercise. And that gives me a concrete idea of my training.

I have bought various kinds of electronic products recently, I bought a laptop and a Bluetooth headset which allow me to watch movies or short video clips in places such as libraries and cafés while not disturbing others. I believe these devices have been an integral part of my life.

sub_questions.content: Is there any technology you want to buy?

answers.content: I would like to try an electric car, especially one with driverless capabilities. I believe electric cars will be the future. sub_questions.content: Is technology important in your life?

I still remember a guy I once met on the Internet and later found out he was my alumnus. He gave me a lot of solid advice when I was looking for a job. It's amazing how technology connects us.

Yes! Technology means a lot to me. One of the benefits of technology is that I can research things online, I can find the best products, restaurants, and travel information all at the best prices, it can really help lower my cost of living. I can't imagine how I would find those great restaurants when I travel without those smart recommendation apps, they always know exactly what I am looking for.

sub_questions.content: Is there any technology you don't like?

answers.content: Personally, I have reservations about facial recognition technology due to privacy concerns. While it has benefits in security and convenience, its potential misuse for extensive surveillance makes me uneasy. The lack of clear regulations on its usage is also troubling. I believe balance is needed between technological advancements and safeguarding individual privacy and rights.

To be honest, I don't really like the current AI technology. I’ll give you an example why. I have noticed that every time I discuss something with a friend, whether it’s a movie, a product or a holiday, on that same day, I get advertisements for those exact things. It feels very scary. AI is infiltrating everyone's lives, and taking away our privacy.

sub_questions.content: What do you think are the trends in technology today compared to when you were young?

answers.content: When I was a kid, people focused more on the technology of communication tools. From telephones to pagers then to cellphones, people were always looking forward to smarter designs and multi-functions. But now, we are expecting technology to change our life, such as electric vehicles, automatic driving, and robots.

I think technology is more advanced now. When I was a kid, it was nice to have a tape player that could play music, but none of the tape players could automatically rewind. questions.content: Did you do puzzles in your childhood?

sub_questions.content: Did you do puzzles in your childhood?

answers.content: Yes, I was almost addicted to doing puzzles when I was a kid. I didn’t have those dolls as other girls did. Compared to dressing barbies, I enjoyed the process of solving the puzzle. You need to find different pieces and put them in the right place. You need to be logical when tracing the clues on different pieces of the puzzle. As a result, it’s more challenging. Yet the challenge also brought me a sense of achievement when I finally accomplished the task.
Yes I did, if my memory is correct. Since I am not a people person, I usually spent my spare time alone with puzzles as it was an interesting way to kill time, and stop me from being bored.

sub_questions.content: When do you do puzzles, during a trip or when you feel bored?

answers.content: I usually do the puzzles during my trip. However they are not really traditional puzzles. I buy Lego during long flights to kill the time. You don’t have wifi on the plane. You’re cut off from the outside world. Sometimes, playing with lego will make a boring trip a bit interesting.

I prefer doing puzzles during a trip, this is when I can get bored, given that the time spent on the road is always long. I like to do them in order, this also gets rid of boredom and tiredness. If I didn’t do puzzles during a trip, things would be very dull.

sub_questions.content: Do you like doing word puzzles or number puzzles? Which is more difficult for you?

answers.content: Frankly speaking, I prefer number puzzles. I have installed a number puzzle app on my smartphone which serves as a great time killer when I am on the shuttle bus to work. Obviously solving a word puzzle seems to be a much tougher task for me, sometimes I might need a dictionary. Given the fact that it’s just a game, why should I work so hard on that?

I prefer to do number puzzles rather than word puzzles because they are more difficult to play, word puzzles often come in the form of crosswords, yet number puzzles come in different forms. My favorite type of number puzzle is Sudoku. I started to play it at a very young age because it does not require much mathematical knowledge but is challenging nonetheless.

sub_questions.content: Who do you usually do puzzles with?

answers.content: Actually I am accustomed to doing puzzles alone, while I sometimes play them with my parents at home, especially after having dinner together. I believe it is an effective way to ensure quality time spent together to strengthen the bond between family members, otherwise, alienation and isolation between families may occur.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is good for old people to do puzzles?

answers.content: Yes, I definitely agree with that. We all worry about our elderly family members will feel tedious at home but just find it so hard to learn to use electronic devices like smart TVs and enjoy themselves. I reckon that puzzles might be a good solution because it has simple rules and requires no extra facilities. On top of that, it’s also expected to have a positive impact on preventing Alzheimer’s.

Yes, I think it is good for old people to do puzzles because doing a puzzle exercises the area of the brain that stores memories. A person has to remember pieces they’ve seen before when putting a puzzle together. Strengthening and exercising short-term memory is especially important for older adults because it helps them keep their minds sharp.

sub_questions.content: What's the most popular social media platform in China? Why?

answers.content: The most popular social media in China is WeChat, which has now become the most common way of contact and the most preferred social platform for people. I think the main reason is that WeChat is very full-featured. In addition to instant messaging and video and voice calls, WeChat has also developed a series of functions such as Moments, Subscriptions, Channels, etc., which meet people's needs for networking and office work.

I think one of the most popular social media platforms in China now is Douyin which is similar to Tik Tok, the difference is that the latter serves the overseas market while the former is mainly used in China. It is characterized by short videos that cover a wide range of subjects from astronomy to daily trivialities so that users can choose any topic that they are interested in, which, I think, is one of the reasons why it is so popular in China.
