Overall, pens are my preferred choice for their reliability and versatility in various writing tasks.

Honestly, neither of them, because in this digital world we use computers and mobiles more often, which means I type a lot instead of writing with a pen or pencil. When I was in school, I did use pens very often considering that electronic devices weren’t allowed at the time, so I had no choice but to pick up a pen to take notes.

questions.content: Do you spend a lot on jeans?
Why do you think jeans are popular?
Do you like wearing jeans, why?
How often do you wear jeans?
Do you wear jeans?

sub_questions.content: Do you spend a lot on jeans?

answers.content: Not really. Unlike silk shirts or cashmere(羊绒的) coats, jeans are generally inexpensive, as they were originally designed to be worn by industrial workers. But very occasionally, I buy some designer jeans that I’m crazy about, like no more than once a year. And I can wear them for a long time without wearing them out(穿坏，磨破).

sub_questions.content: Why do you think jeans are popular?

answers.content: There are multiple reasons. For a start they are made from denim, which, essentially(本质上讲), is just cotton. This is a really comfortable material, but also durable enough to avoid everyday wear and tear . Also, they can match almost all kinds of everyday outfits(外套), like I mentioned just now. What’s more, they are usually not that expensive, which means everybody can afford them.

sub_questions.content: Do you like wearing jeans, why?

answers.content: Of course, especially in spring and autumn, when the temperature is not too high. The way I see it, jeans are probably the most versatile(多面手的，多功能的) clothing in one’s closet. Say, if you wear those super-popular designer brands like Evisu, you can easily achieve a really fashionable look. Also, if you wear those dark indigo or even black ones with blazers(单西，西装外套), you can have a smart, business-casual look.

sub_questions.content: How often do you wear jeans?

answers.content: Basically, every day, I mean, if I don’t have to dress formally for an interview or something like that. Formal occasions like that would have certain dress codes(着装要求), and wearing jeans at this time could be impolite. But apart from that, I just wear them all the time. I’d say... in a typical(典型的，常规的) week, there will be at least five days when I wear jeans.

sub_questions.content: Do you wear jeans?

answers.content: Yes, absolutely. I wear jeans a lot, and I don’t see any reason why I shouldn’t. Now, some people may say that it’s not really healthy because it can affect your blood circulation(血液循环), but I think that only happens when you wear those ridiculously skinny ones. To be honest, I can’t see the point. I always prefer baggy(宽松的) ones, which are immeasurably more comfortable.

questions.content: What kind of perfume do you like?
Do you wear perfume?
Do you give perfume as a gift?
What does perfume mean to you?

sub_questions.content: What kind of perfume do you like?

answers.content: Personally speaking, in comparison to those perfumes with very strong aromas, I prefer those with lighter aromas. For example, I enjoy perfumes with natural aromas such as flowers or woods, as I consider it is more suitable for a boy, and is also more acceptable for others.

sub_questions.content: Do you wear perfume?

answers.content: I do wear perfume sometimes, but I don’t wear it every day. When attending some important events, such as weddings, ceremonies or formal dinners, I wear some on my suit. However, I’m not a fan of perfume day-to-day, and I don’t wear it frequently.

sub_questions.content: Do you give perfume as a gift?

answers.content: Yes, I do. Last winter, I traveled to Egypt, a country famous for its perfume. When I was in its capital Cairo, I found a very special perfume and I bought it for my mom. She was very delighted and fascinated by my gift, and she now often wears it when going out.

sub_questions.content: What does perfume mean to you?

answers.content: Well, for me, I think perfume is a special decoration and also a signal of one’s personality and identity. For instance, if a lady wears perfume that has a rosey aroma, it might indicate that she’s romantic and tends to date romantic men.

questions.content: Does your name have any special meaning?
Do you like your name?
How would you choose names for your next generation?
Are there any differences between how the Chinese name their children now and in the past?
What kinds of names are popular in China?
Does anyone in your family have the same name as you?
Are there many Chinese people who have the same name as you?
Are there any names that are more popular than others in China?
Is there any tradition about naming babies?

sub_questions.content: Does your name have any special meaning?

answers.content: Yes, my Chinese name shows my parents’ wish and advice to me. The Chinese word meaning humble in English always reminds me of not being arrogant in any cases, which has an major influence to my life without any doubt.

(eg：Boxu Li李博旭) Yes, my great-grandpa gave me this name, because he had high expectations of me, “Bo” means knowledge and “Xu” means sunshine, he wanted me to be as positive and optimistic as the sun, he also hoped I could be knowledgeable and well-educated when I grew up.

sub_questions.content: Do you like your name?

answers.content: I've always had a love and hate relationship with my name. I like it because this name was given by my great-grand father, although he passed away many years ago, but my name always reminds me of him; However, my name is quite boyish (girly男孩子用) actually, many people thought I was a boy/girl before they met me.

sub_questions.content: How would you choose names for your next generation?

answers.content: In my opinion, there are various ways to choose the name for my children such as combining the last name of their parents or displaying my wish to him including being healthy, handsome and so on, which is to some extent common in China.

Well, choosing the name for the next generation is quite a serious and vital issue. First, I will take my parents’ names into consideration, which shows heritage. Second, the name should have a good meaning and contain the best wishes from parents. Third, the traditional culture is an important and useful reference for naming.

sub_questions.content: Are there any differences between how the Chinese name their children now and in the past?

answers.content: The way people choose the name for their children has been changing all the time dramatically. For example, the past, the names were always related to the historical issues happening recently considering China has come across a turbulent age hundreds years ago. However, today as people are living a peaceful life, people’s names are more likely to suggest their personality and morality.

Of course, there is a huge difference between how the Chinese name their children now compared to the past. Chinese people used to name their children after some major events, like the country's establishment or the war to resist U. S. aggression and aid Korea. But now, people tend to choose some meaningful names for the next generation to express their best wishes.

sub_questions.content: What kinds of names are popular in China?

answers.content: Actually there is no specific type of name that is very popular in china, since China is a heavily populated country with over 1 billion people, but I have noticed that if the new born baby is a boy, parents like to call them “Wei” or “Qiang”, because these words mean “great and strong”, and for girls, “Li” and “Ling” is more popular, which mean “clever and pretty”.

sub_questions.content: Does anyone in your family have the same name as you?

answers.content: Actually no. Although people always share the same last name, the first name can’t be the same considering that in China it is not respectful to have the same first name with their families. If people’s names are the same, it would be troublesome.

Nope. In China, it is really weird to have the very same name as someone else in the family. It would be confusing when calling out a name. Parents will have different wishes for their kids, so they will give different meaningful names to their children. Besides, China's rich traditional culture gives people great choices to refer to when choosing the name for the next generation.

sub_questions.content: Are there many Chinese people who have the same name as you?

answers.content: To be honest, I have never met another Chinese who shares the same name with me, maybe there is someone there who has the same name with me, I feel very lucky about it actually, my name is unique and special in this way.

sub_questions.content: Are there any names that are more popular than others in China?

answers.content: Based on a survey conducted by the Ministry of Public Security of China, the top names for Chinese newborns are Mùchén (沐宸). The Mu character literally means bathing in sunshine, whereas Chen means imperial palace, a symbol of wealth and power. I think the name is popular in China because it carries the best wishes of the parents to the kids.

Surprisingly there is no specific name that is very popular in China, even though it has a population of over 1 billion people, but, I have noticed that if a newborn baby is a boy, some parents like to call them “Wei” or “Qiang”, because these words mean “great and strong”, and for girls, “Li” and “Ling” is more popular, which mean “clever and pretty”.

sub_questions.content: Is there any tradition about naming babies?

answers.content: We used to have many traditions about naming babies, but they are not popular these days, still, some older generations, who are very superstitious would follow these traditions, for instance, they wouldn’t dare name a child before he/she is born, instead they give him a fake name, something very undesirable, such as “mud face”, that is meant to disgust evil spirit and trick them to stay away from the child.

questions.content: What do you usually do on your birthday?
Do people in China celebrate birthdays/have birthday parties?
What did you do on your birthday when you were young?
How do they/children celebrate birthdays?
Do you think it is important for you to celebrate your birthday?
Are people’s birthdays very important in China?
Whose birthday do you think is the most important to celebrate in China?
Do you think it’s important for people to celebrate birthdays?
Are birthdays more important for children or for adults?
Do people in China do anything special to celebrate birthdays?

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do on your birthday?

answers.content: I prefer to celebrate my birthday with a bunch of friends. Normally, I would like to throw a party at my home and invite all my close friends to come over. We eat dinner and cakes together. Afterwards, we might go to a karaoke and enjoy the rest of the night there.

I have a tradition on my birthday that I do not accept presents. Instead, I ask my friends to write me a handwritten letter. Preferably delivered by postal service, even when it’s possible to meet in person. Then on my birthday I have a small gathering with my friends and read the letters I have received. I love the romanticism of emotions carried by ink.

sub_questions.content: Do people in China celebrate birthdays/have birthday parties?

answers.content: Yes I think so. Especially for those who are little and old. The parties for the eldest and youngest person in a family will be the grandest one.

sub_questions.content: What did you do on your birthday when you were young?

answers.content: I remember when I was in primary school, my parents loved to take me to go shopping on my birthday. They would buy me whatever gifts I wanted. I remember I have received pretty clothes, comic books, barbie dolls, etc. After shopping, they usually took me to a good restaurant for a lovely family dinner. I really miss the time that I was living together with my parents.

When I was young, I would invite a lot of my friends to my house to celebrate together. I would have a huge birthday cake with a tiger on it, for I was born in the “year of the tiger” according to the Chinese lunar calendar. My parents would sometimes invite some clowns to do some performances for me.

sub_questions.content: How do they/children celebrate birthdays?

answers.content: I think they will invite a lot of their friends to their house and celebrate together. Some parents will even invite some crowns to do some performance for their children.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important for you to celebrate your birthday?

answers.content: I think yes. Birthdays mark the beginning of a new stage for a person. Therefore, it is meaningful to do something on this special day with families and friends to keep good memories. In addition, birthday is a good occasion for me to enjoy some good time with my families and friends.

Yes, I do. Not only because my birthday is a day that is really worthwhile remembering, but also because it is a good opportunity for me to enjoy a big party with my best friends. In Chinese traditional culture, birthdays are quite important for those who are over fifty. So I think it is important for me to host grand birthday parties in my fifties, sixties, seventies, and so on.

sub_questions.content: Are people’s birthdays very important in China?

answers.content: I think so. Especially for those who are older than fifties. According to traditional culture, senior citizens have to host grand birthday parties in their fifties, sixties, seventies and so on.

sub_questions.content: Whose birthday do you think is the most important to celebrate in China?

answers.content: Even if you expand the range to the whole world, I still think my own birthday is the most important to celebrate. Because this is a date that my parents and I would never forget. For me, it helps me to remember for how long I have lived in this world and helps me to review my progress and growth in my life.

I believe the celebration of a birthday is the celebration of someone’s achievements and virtues. In China, thousands of years ago, there was a historical figure whose influences permeated the whole of Eastern Asia. His name was Confucius, a great teacher and philosopher. I still remember ceremonies we held to celebrate his birthday when I was at High School. His wisdom, Confucius School and his philosophies are world renowned, for this reason I think his birthday is the most important for us to celebrate in China.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it’s important for people to celebrate birthdays?

answers.content: Yes, I do. Not only because birthday is a day that real worthwhile to memorize but because it is a good opportunity for people to enjoy a big party with best friends.

sub_questions.content: Are birthdays more important for children or for adults?

answers.content: They are more important for children as far as I am concerned. Because compared with adults, children are more likely to enjoy themselves at birthday party.

sub_questions.content: Do people in China do anything special to celebrate birthdays?

answers.content: As far as I am concerned, the most common habit is eating noodles, which means your life expectancy will be as long as the noodles.

Do you take public transportation when you travel?

Do most people prefer public transportation in your country?
What do you want to change about the public transport?

Will you ride public transportation in the future? Why?
Will there be more people taking public transportation in the future?
What's the most popular means of transportation in your hometown?
Why did you choose that form of transport?
What form of transport do you usually use?
What vehicles (or what means of transport) do people in China most often choose to use?
What do you think of the transport situation in your hometown?
How often do you take buses?
Would you say transport costs are high in your hometown?
How has the transport system in your hometown changed in the past few decades?
Can you compare the advantages of planes and trains?
Is driving to work popular in your country?
Do you think people will drive more in the future?
Would you ride bikes to work in the future?
What will become the most popular means of transportation in China?
Do you prefer public transportation or private transportation?

sub_questions.content: What kind of public transportation do you usually take?

answers.content: Well, if I travel a short distance, like going to a restaurant or a shopping mall, I will take buses or subways. It is wise to take subways, especially during commute hours. When talking about the long journey, the public transportation I usually take is the high-speed train. You know, with the development of China’s railway transportation network, taking the high-speed train can reach almost every city in China. No need to mention that the train ticket is not so expensive, isn’t it?

When it comes to commuting in metropolitan cities, I usually take the subway because it is one of the fastest, most comfortable, and reliable forms of public transportation there is. They only stop at a limited number of locations, and there is no congestion. Buses, on the other hand, are slower, less comfortable, stop at lots of stations, and are often caught up in congested traffic.

sub_questions.content: Do you take public transportation when you travel?

answers.content: I always take public transportation when I travel. Mainly because I don't own a car and I don't think I could afford one in the near future. I actually prefer traveling by metro than by a private car because I hate being stuck in a traffic jam. Regardless of how crowded a metro train might be, it generally arrives on time, and go on unimpeded.

sub_questions.content: Do people usually use public transportation in your country?

answers.content: Apparently, they do! Considering the population we have in our country, our public transportation system is actually pretty well-developed, so a lot of us prefer to take buses or subways to school or work. Also, taking public transportation is really cheap in our nation, so for the commutators, it is a sensible choice.

sub_questions.content: When do you usually take public transportation, in your everyday life or when you are travelling?

answers.content: I would say I take public transportation in my daily life. There is no doubt that I take it when I visit other cities. But the most common situation when I take public transportation is when I go downtown from the university town. You know, taking the subway is the most suitable way in this situation. Taking a taxi is too expensive. I also take the subway when I go to a restaurant or a shopping mall. Obviously, the subway is an indispensable transportation means for most people in this city.

I take public transportation in everyday life because it is one of the most sustainable forms of transport available to me. Less fuel burned per person means better air quality for cities. In addition, more people riding on buses, trains, and other public systems mean there are fewer vehicles on the road to cause traffic congestion.

sub_questions.content: What kind of public transport do people in your country use the most? Why?

answers.content: Well, I think it depends on the regions. For some cities where people have access to subways, they would probably prefer to take subways because there would be no traffic congestion problems. But for the cities without the subway, buses can be the mostly used transportation since they are really affordable and can generally arrive on time.

sub_questions.content: Do most people prefer public transportation in your country?

answers.content: Public transportation is such an economical and convenient option that most people in my country would prefer to travel by it. Taking a high-speed train or airplane, you can arrive at a far city within just several hours. The most important thing is that you can relax or do your work along the way instead of taking control of the driving. Besides, driving by oneself is far more expensive than taking public transportation.

Yes. In Shanghai there are over ten million people on average taking the metro every day. China also has the longest high-speed railway in the world, which is also a popular choice for Chinese people. One has to admit that a private flight or even just a private car is unaffordable for the majority of our society.

sub_questions.content: What do you want to change about the public transport?

answers.content: Actually, I am quite happy with this part of our infrastructure but if I have to change anything, I would probably say, the frequency. I feel during the rush hours, maybe there should be more buses or subways while when it’s not busy we can just lower the frequency in order to save energy in the unnecessary period.

sub_questions.content: Did you take transportation when you were a kid?

answers.content: Yes. Actually, I have taken public transportation since I was 8 years old. When I was in elementary school, I took the bus home from school by myself. It might be a little bit frightening to me at the beginning. But I adapted to it quite soon. Since then, I've been taking public transportation home by myself.

Only with my parents. After all, public transportation is a bit dangerous for a kid, with so many strangers and some relatively complex instruments on the vehicles. But when I started middle school I travelled there by bus. Many of my classmates live nearby, and we would accompany each other.

sub_questions.content: Will you ride public transportation in the future? Why?

answers.content: Surely, I will! I mean I do enjoy driving my own car but sometimes I feel it’s not really eco-friendly. Since we got such well-established public transportation that can take me to almost everywhere, as a responsible citizen, I think it would be a good choice to take buses or other shared transport to help ease the traffic pressure, and to save energy.

sub_questions.content: Will there be more people taking public transportation in the future?

answers.content: I imagine so, yeah. People are realizing the terrible effects that climate change brings about. Most of us want to make an effort to improve the air quality. Plus, the major roads in most cities are crowded and it takes forever to travel. So public transportation will probably be the best option for us.

I think in the future people will use public transport more. First of all, people are more aware of the importance of environmental protection and the harm caused by private cars. With this awareness, people will be more inclined to protect the environment and use public transport. Furthermore, the cost of traveling on public transport is far less than the cost of private transport. It’s cheap and convenient, so more people will choose public transportation.

sub_questions.content: What's the most popular means of transportation in your hometown?

answers.content: Most people in my hometown travel by metro, which has now become the most useful and popular means of transportation. As far as I know, we so far own 17 lines of metro extending in all directions including rural and suburb areas of the city, and there are still some lines under construction.

sub_questions.content: Why did you choose that form of transport?

answers.content: Since I am a student without any earnings, I always choose public transportation, such as bus and subway, which saves me money. Taking public transportation is very convenient, since it is now perfectly developed in my city. Besides, when weather permitted, I would choose to ride a bike. It is the most environmentally friendly way of transportation and my favorite way of sport, which brings me a lot of fun.

sub_questions.content: What form of transport do you usually use?

answers.content: I usually choose to ride a bike. In recent years, the business of shared bicycles is very popular in China, especially in big cities, so we can find shared bicycles almost everywhere, which is quite convenient and economical. I also enjoy going cycling to the parks with my friends at weekends. We cycled slowly so that we never missed the beautiful scenery along the road. That is my favorite way of sport. Cycling makes me healthier.

sub_questions.content: What vehicles (or what means of transport) do people in China most often choose to use?

answers.content: Generally speaking, people in China prefer to go out by bus or subway. China is a country with a large population. If everyone chooses to use private transportation, that would cause terrible traffic jam and severe air pollution. So, now more and more people choose the eco-friendly way to go out. The government has controlled the price of public transportation in order to encourage people to take them, so that they would be more preferred.

sub_questions.content: What do you think of the transport situation in your hometown?

answers.content: The transport situation in my hometown is not bad. Although there could be some traffic jams in rush hours, the traffic is smooth at any other time. The elevated road and subway line help to reduce the travel time greatly. Besides, the government pays great attention to build more traffic lines, which makes some remote areas be also accessible to us, so it is more and more convenient for us to take public transportation.

sub_questions.content: How often do you take buses?

answers.content: Generally speaking, I take buses twice a day since I commute to work. Normally I spend one hour to travel by bus if there’s no traffic congestion. But on weekend I prefer metro since it is accessible in all directions of the city, which is much faster and more convenient.

sub_questions.content: Would you say transport costs are high in your hometown?

answers.content: I don’t think the transport costs in my hometown are high. Thanks to the controlling of the government, almost everyone could afford the public transport costs. People can cross the city by subway, paying no more than 10 RMB. Better still, the students and the old could even get a discount when buying traffic tickets. So I think the transport costs in my hometown are reasonable.

sub_questions.content: How has the transport system in your hometown changed in the past few decades?

answers.content: The transport system in my hometown changed a lot recently. Several decades ago, people prefer to take buses. Now, because of the construction of subway traffic, people can choose to take the subway, which is faster and more convenient. Bicycle becomes one of the modern ways of transportation in recent years. Since more and more people get the awareness of environment protection, they regard bicycles as their first choice.

sub_questions.content: Can you compare the advantages of planes and trains?

answers.content: Sure. Traveling by flight takes less time and it’s considered to be safer than taking a train since there is a very small risk of plane crashing. While traveling by trains saves more money and it provides frequency departure and relatively flexible timetable than flight does.

sub_questions.content: Is driving to work popular in your country?

answers.content: Yes, it’s quite common to drive to work here. With the increase of our living standard, cars are becoming affordable to most people, no matter singled or married. Most importantly, teeming underground carriages are unbearable indeed during rush hours compared to traffic queues on highway.

sub_questions.content: Do you think people will drive more in the future?

answers.content: Well I don’t think so. Realizing the situation of our severe traffic congestion and perfect public transportation system, I believe people will get rid of private transportation to some extent. By the way, parking difficulty is another serious problem facing those prefer to drive.

sub_questions.content: Would you ride bikes to work in the future?

answers.content: Yes, I think I will take this choice unless my working place is really far away from home. It must be good to start a day’s work with the wonder of cycling. Besides, riding to work avoid traffic queues and teeming underground carriages, which keeps me in good mood.

sub_questions.content: What will become the most popular means of transportation in China?

answers.content: Considering the large population and the large number of vehicles, subway will become the most popular means of transportation in China. More and more cities in China have been constructing rail transit systems. It's cheap, fast and convenient. For instance, I can go to almost every area in Shanghai by subway.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer public transportation or private transportation?

answers.content: From my perspective, I prefer public transportation since it is economic, efficient and environmental-friendly. Besides, along with the comprehensive public transport, our traffic modes turn to diversity: metro, buses and share bikes, which makes it no need to take private transportation.

questions.content: Do Chinese kids often go to library?
Did you go to library when you were a kid?
Do you often go to library?
What do you usually do in the library?
Do Chinese kids often go to the library?
Did you go to a library when you were a kid?
Do you often go to the library?

sub_questions.content: Do Chinese kids often go to library?

answers.content: As a college student, I would say that Chinese kids frequently visit the library. Libraries are fundamental for academic success, enabling students to access a wide range of resources for their studies. Besides, libraries offer a peaceful study environment, making them a preferred choice for students to concentrate on their school work. Therefore, it is common to see Chinese children utilizing library facilities regularly to enhance their learning experience.

With more and more Chinese people attaching importance to reading, the library becomes a good choice for parents. Many parents take their kids to the library to read every weekend. Compared with online reading, reading in the library is beneficial for kids because they could learn more by deep reading.

sub_questions.content: Did you go to library when you were a kid?

answers.content: When I was a kid, I always went to the library with my friends. I got the habit of reading in primary school when we were required to read a book every month and everyone was required to make a presentation about a book review each semester. So my friends and I often went to the library to read because there were books of all kinds and we could find everything that we needed.

sub_questions.content: Do you often go to library?

answers.content: Honestly, I go to the library less often than in the past. I hardly go to the library now because online reading is available at any time. When I was a student, I went to the library quite often because it provided me with a peaceful and quiet place to read and learn. Now our life is filled with all kinds of information, I do think going to the library is a better choice, so in the future, I would go to the library to read more often.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do in the library?

answers.content: Well, I read in the library of course. I read magazines, newspapers and books in the library. But sometimes I would bring my laptop to write essays in the library, or watch online videos. You know, the Wifi is quite strong in my school library. I never need to worry about losing Internet connection.

The common activities – reading and studying. It’s a library, what else can we do there? We can’t talk because it would appear to be rude. We can’t eat because the food might smell. But I have to confess that sometimes I fall asleep when reading or studying for long hours.

sub_questions.content: Do Chinese kids often go to the library?

answers.content: With the prevalence of the Internet, the number of the Chinese kids who go to the library to read has decreased in the past decade. Many kids choose to learn from TV programs or online reading. But there are still some kids going to the library to read because their parents think paper reading is more beneficial than online reading.

sub_questions.content: Did you go to a library when you were a kid?

answers.content: When I was a kid, my mom took me to the library every weekend to finish my reading lists that covered a wide range of books. In terms of my reading habit, I should owe thanks to my mom who was keen on reading. She was a teacher and helped me make reading lists every semester throughout my school years.

sub_questions.content: Do you often go to the library?

answers.content: I go to the library at least three or four times a month. I find that reading greatly changes the way that I am thinking and it allows me to accept various opinions. I quite enjoy it. When reading in the library, I find myself relieved and the pressure less intense.

questions.content: Do you think people are happy when buying new things?
Do you feel happy when buying new things?
When do you feel happy at work? Why?
Is there anything that has made you feel happy lately?
What do you think will make you feel happy in the future?
What made you happy when you were little?

sub_questions.content: Do you think people are happy when buying new things?

answers.content: I guess so. New things often brings people a sense of satisfaction. In another word, people pay for things that they are fond of. When they get to own something that they like, of course they would feel happy and satisfied.

Why not? In most cases, buying new things is joyful because it means we are able to afford the things we want to purchase. But things would be very different if we were buying a new house because that means we have loans to pay, and that could be quite stressful.

sub_questions.content: Do you feel happy when buying new things?

answers.content: Absolutely! Shopping makes me happy. I’m not saying that I’m a shopaholic, but to me, shopping is healing and relaxing. When I am in the shopping mall, I like to take away the items that I like. It’s a moment when I feel a sense of control over my life.

Definitely. Who doesn’t? I think most people love shopping because it means we are able to afford the things we want, and it’s a really joyful journey. New shoes, new iPhones, new outfits, I’m even happy right now imagining getting all those new things .

sub_questions.content: When do you feel happy at work? Why?

answers.content: To be honest, I feel the happiest when I am about to finish work. Well, you know, work can be stressful, especially when I have to deal with difficult people sometimes. It exhausts me. When the day is about to finish, I just feel that I can finally relax and enjoy myself for the evening.

I would really be happy if I make some achievements at work. I like challenging things and don’t wanna do routine work only. So, whenever I get out of my comfort zone and face challenges at work, I feel very thrilled and will become even happier when I handle the challenges smoothly.

sub_questions.content: Is there anything that has made you feel happy lately?

answers.content: I visited my parents last weekend and we had a great dinner together. I was glad to learn that my father got a big promotion at his work. And he was going to buy me a big gift to celebrate the promotion! That made me very happy.

Yes, I just enjoyed my Chinese New Year vacation with my family and friends, and I was super happy. See, we have a whole week off for the holiday. For the first couple of days, I spent some quality time with my family members, sitting down, chatting, and having family dinners. Later , I went traveling with my family to Hangzhou and spent a really nice time there.

sub_questions.content: What do you think will make you feel happy in the future?

answers.content: I find that what makes me happy always brings me a sense of satisfaction. For example, I felt pleased when I got the scholarship after working hard. I am applying for the master degree these days, before which I have been preparing for the test for about a year. If I could pass the exam, I would be extremely happy. However, I also find that once I spare no effort to do something, the result is also better than expected.

Many things can make me feel happy in the future. Because of the Covid-19 pandemic in the past three years, I feel that being healthy is important, so I have decided to start some workouts like going jogging, and I believe regular exercises will make me much stronger and feel refreshed and energetic as well. In addition, now countries around the world are relaxing mask mandates and I’m really looking forward to going traveling abroad next year, and I feel kinda thrilled as I’m thinking of this.

sub_questions.content: What made you happy when you were little?

answers.content: The definition of happiness varies from person to person, but I believe little kids have something in common in terms of this topic. When I was little, I was extremely happy to go to the amusement park, besides, I also got excited upon receiving gifts on my birthday or other important events. All in all, when I was little, I was curious about the world, and new things often made me happy. I still hope I could maintain such curiosity now.

There were lots of things that made me feel happy when I was young. For example, when I got my favorite dolls or toys on my birthday, I would be over the moon. Sometimes, I got good grades in math, I also felt really happy because I was not good at math. Apart from that, when I spent my summer vacation in the countryside with my grandparents and hung out with my friends in the village, I also had a lot of fun. As you can see, I had a very happy childhood.

questions.content: Did you often wear T-shirt when you were a child?
Would you buy T-shirts as souvenirs on vacation?
How often do you wear T-shirts?
Do you think older people who wear T-shirts are fashionable?
Do you like T-shirts with pictures or prints?
Do you like wearing T-shirts?

sub_questions.content: Did you often wear T-shirt when you were a child?

answers.content: As a child, I frequently opted for T-shirts with colorful prints and cartoon characters. They were my go-to choice for playtime and outings with family and friends. The comfort and ease of movement offered by T-shirts made them a staple in my wardrobe during my early years.

As a child, I frequently opted for cute dresses and skirts over T-shirts. I was quite girly and enjoyed wearing colorful outfits to express my personality. T-shirts were not my first choice, as I preferred more stylish and distinctive clothing items.

sub_questions.content: Would you buy T-shirts as souvenirs on vacation?

answers.content: Not really. I am indeed a big traveler, but to be honest, I don’t like to shop for souvenirs on tourist sites, including T-shirts. You know, souvenirs are often sold at an absurd price. In another word, it’s obvious cheating. So I don’t really do T-shirt shopping on vacations.

Definitely, I think it’s quite a good way to keep track of the places I’ve been to. You know, you really enjoy quality time on vacation, and when you wear the t-shirt again, you kinda get the mood back with it. Also, the t-shirt can be quite stylish if you choose the right one. Sometimes, I even buy t-shirts on vacation as presents for my family and friends.

sub_questions.content: How often do you wear T-shirts?

answers.content: I wear T-shirts in summer about every day. And I especially love white T-shirts. For some reason, I feel very comfortable in the white T-shirts. Depending on the cutting of the T-shirt, it can be casual or formal. So I wear it in casual occasions as well as in workplace.

Every day. Many would think that the frequency of wearing T-shirt very much depends on the season. But you know, nowadays, shopping malls and offices have their heating on, and it’s actually quite hot in there. So I wear the t-shirt under my sweater and coat, and if I feel too hot, I will take off my other layers.

sub_questions.content: Do you think older people who wear T-shirts are fashionable?

answers.content: As far as I am concerned, most older people who wear T-shirts usually prioritize comfort. T-shirts are easy to wear and match, which makes them the first choice for people of all ages. However, some older people like wearing T-shirts with unique prints or patterns. I think they are fashionable enough. But all in all, whether you are fashionable or not is not that important, there is a famous saying that fashion passes while style remains.

No, I don’t think so. Today, t-shirts are common items in people’s wardrobes. Not only the younger generation, but also the elderly prefers to wear them, and I guess it is because t-shirts are comfortable to wear and, as I said previously, they can go with everything, so more and more older people choose to wear t-shirts. I don’t deny that wearing t-shirts doesn’t make the elderly look fashionable, but I just don’t think they choose t-shirts simply for looking fashionable.

sub_questions.content: Do you like T-shirts with pictures or prints?

answers.content: I do like T-shirts with some well-designed pictures or prints. As you may know, many brands are selling T-shirts with very exquisite Chinese classic prints or intarsia that make T-shirts look fashionable and unique. My favorite T-shirt is designed with a traditional Chinese knot knitted right in the front center. I think those prints or pictures make T-shirts vivid and fashionable.

No, actually I don’t like t-shirts with pictures or prints because it looks so fancy, and that is not really my style. I prefer t-shirts in plain colors like pure white, grey, black etc. and it is OK if there are some small simple patterns such as flowers, cats, the English alphabet, etc. I believe that t-shirts without fancy pictures or prints make me look smart and they easily match my outfits.

sub_questions.content: Do you like wearing T-shirts?

answers.content: T-shirts are very popular among young people. I like wearing T-shirts so much that I wear them very often, especially in summer. The reason why I like wearing T-shirts is that they are comfortable and I could even wear them to do sports. Another reason is that T-shirts go with everything, I don't need to spend time considering what to wear every day.

It depends. Of course, I won’t wear T-shirts in the winter, especially when it is really cold. But I have to say that t-shirts really are my favorite clothes in summer and I can wear them throughout the summer. T-shirts are comfortable and more importantly, they can go with everything so you can wear them with all your clothes like jeans or skirts, and it makes you look good.

questions.content: Do you think it's a good idea to leave your keys with a neighbor?
Do you often forget the keys and lock yourself out?
Have you ever lost your keys?
Do you always bring a lot of keys with you?

sub_questions.content: Do you think it's a good idea to leave your keys with a neighbor?

answers.content: Unfortunately, no. Even if we are on rather friendly terms (关系相当友好), I don't think that I would leave them my house keys. I would say that trusting someone is too risky these days. Currently, I’m living alone, so I would rather ask my parents to keep it than leave my neighbors the spare keys whenever I go away.

I’d rather not. I grew up living in the city, and our relationships with neighbors are not that close. Most of the time, we just take good care of ourselves and don’t wanna bother other people for small favors. Also, it could be quite complicated if your house got robbed, and the neighbor has the key. So, I don’t think it’s a good idea for the neighbor to have my backup key.

sub_questions.content: Do you often forget the keys and lock yourself out?

answers.content: Not really. I’m a very careful person, and I usually check four things before I go out: my phone, my ID card, my charger, and my key. These are my going-out essentials. But sometimes if I’m really in a hurry and get hasty, it’s possible to miss one or two items.

No, I guess not, probably because I’m slightly OCD (强迫症) when I go out. I mean I would check my keys, phones, or things like that several times before going out. So, you know I can’t be locked out apart from the exception that I'm just not behaving like myself for some reason (某种原因，不在状态).

sub_questions.content: Have you ever lost your keys?

answers.content: In my childhood, I have lost the key to my home once. At that time, my parent both were outside at work, so I have to call a locksmith to help me get in. Oh, and I also lost the key to my diary book because it was so small and hard to notice. So it is quite disappointing that I haven’t been able to open my diary till now.

Yes, I lost my key when I was in high school. When I left my school and headed home, I realized I had lost the key to my bike. I spent a long time looking for it but failed to find it. It was a long ride from my school to my home, and I didn't bring any money with me. As a result, I asked my friend for help and to take me to my home with his bike.

sub_questions.content: Do you always bring a lot of keys with you?

answers.content: Well, now, of course, not. We are living in a world with well-developed technology, so tangible lock is replaced by electronic lock. Therefore, bringing a key sometimes becomes unnecessary, because you can enter most places only with a password or fingerprint . Why bother to have keys?

No, I don’t bring any keys with me now, because I don’t use them anymore. My apartment uses code lock, so I just have to remember the password. When I arrive at my work place, there is still no need for keys, because I use a swipe card to open doors and clock on. In my opinion, keys are becoming old-fashioned and are fading out of people’s lives.

questions.content: Do you make plans for your weekends?
What do you usually do on weekends? Do you study or work?
What did you do last weekend?
Do you like weekends?

sub_questions.content: Do you make plans for your weekends?

answers.content: Like I said before, I would not make a very specific plan for weekends due to my workload. I usually decide what I would do on weekends on Friday nights when I got home from work. In my opinion, making plans for weekends is unnecessary. I already feel stressed after a week of work, so I don’t think that I would have a mind making plans. Just do what I want to do at weekends, and that’s the true meaning of weekends. Don’t you think so?

Definitely, I think even if we do not have actual work or study to do over the weekends, I don’t want to waste time. Making plans can be a very effective way to make our life more productive and rewarding. That is why I always schedule different activities for Saturdays and Sundays. If I don't, I will probably end up lying in bed all day, which I don't really want.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do on weekends? Do you study or work?

answers.content: It really depends. I would schedule my weekends based on how tired I am. If I had so much works to do that week, I would use one day of that weekend for rest. Or else I would usually go out and have some fun with my friends. Basketball, tennis, and pool, you name it, we usually pick different sports for different weekends.

Well, at the moment I am working, so I have a lot of time for myself on the weekends. Normally I will just hang out with some friends and do some grocery shopping. Sometimes there will be some special occasions, such as a friend’s wedding or a party, and I will also put them into my weekend schedule.

sub_questions.content: What did you do last weekend?

answers.content: Last weekend, I spent my time learning how to play the guitar on my own. I searched for a series of guitar teaching short videos, watching them for like two or three hours. The result of my hard work was that I can play several complete songs. Besides, I also went out with my friends to play basketball. Physical exercises are crucial to my health, to which I always pay attention.

I went to my grandparents’ house and enjoyed a family reunion there. It was last Saturday, it was my grandma’s birthday so my parents and I went to her home early in the morning. I ordered a birthday cake shaped like a peach, symbolizing health and longevity. After arriving at her home, we cooked a big meal together and had lots of fun.

sub_questions.content: Do you like weekends?

answers.content: Definitely, I love weekends, because only at weekends can I be totally free from work and study. I work in a foreign business company. Sometimes, you know, my workload is pretty high, which is so stressful. Therefore, I could really use my time at weekends to do something that I am fond of.

Yes, of course, because weekends mean two days of vacation, and I can get some rest. Normally, I go to school every day and study hard from Monday to Friday. However, on weekends, I can go to the park or watch a movie with my friends, and let off some steam. Although weekends mean more homework for me, I still enjoy this precious time.

questions.content: Do you think it is good to use chocolate as gifts to others?
Why do you think chocolate is popular around the world?
Did you often eat chocolate when you were a kid?
How often do you eat chocolate?
What's your favorite flavor?
Do you like eating chocolate? Why/why not?

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is good to use chocolate as gifts to others?

answers.content: Yes, I think chocolate would be a fantastic gift because it has universal appeal. Everybody loves chocolate and chocolate products. Besides, there are tons of different flavors to select from, such as white chocolate, hazelnut chocolate and dark chocolate. People can always find the perfect flavor for themselves. Chocolate also shows love. Giving people chocolate is like expressing your fondness for them, so it’s particularly suitable as a present.

Yes, I think it is good to use chocolate as a gift to others. In fact, it goes without saying that giving chocolate as a gift is a great idea for Valentine’s Day. When people think of Valentine’s Day gifts and special anniversary surprises, they automatically think of chocolate, flowers, and love hearts.

sub_questions.content: Why do you think chocolate is popular around the world?

answers.content: I think chocolate is popular around the world because, first of all, chocolate is very simple to make delicious with easily obtained items. Add varying amounts of milk and sugar, and one can get deliciousness no matter what degrees one uses them. Second, chocolate has so many stimulating chemicals in it that almost nothing compares. That's why women are portrayed as eating it so much when depressed.

Personally, I reckon that the popularity of chocolate comes from the fact that it has a lot of varieties. If you prefer something sweet there is white chocolate and milk chocolate, but if you prefer something, let’s say, a bit bitter and healthier, you can always go for a dark chocolate.

sub_questions.content: Did you often eat chocolate when you were a kid?

answers.content: When I was a kid, I would eat milk chocolate once or twice a week. Every time my family went to the supermarket, I would ask my mom to buy chocolate for me. In retrospect, I think the reason why I used to be attracted to milk chocolate as a kid was that sugary product is something my body attracts to.

Unfortunately, no. When I was little, my parents did not allow me to eat too much chocolate as it could rot my teeth and make me lose my appetite for other healthy food. I only got to eat some sweets, including chocolate, for special occasions, such as my birthday and other people’s weddings.

sub_questions.content: How often do you eat chocolate?

answers.content: I would say I eat dark chocolate once or twice a month, depending on my mood and availability. I don't eat dark chocolate as often as I did when I was a kid, however, I think it tastes better than other snacks. In other words, it's a childhood memory for me.

Well, it depends on the type of chocolate. I have this habit of eating a small chunk of dark chocolate every day, as it is said to be healthy and can keep us energetic. However, for milk chocolate or white chocolate, which contains a lot of sugar, I barely eat it, because I want to keep my diet healthy.

sub_questions.content: What's your favorite flavor?

answers.content: I love dark chocolates because when it gives satisfaction to my taste buds a freshness to my mind. Whenever I’m sad it makes me feel better. Also, dark chocolates are close to what actual cocoa tastes like. The other chocolates have cocoa with sugar where the taste of sugar is more dominating. Frankly speaking, dark chocolate with a good cup of black coffee is the best combination, in my opinion.

My favorite flavor is dark chocolate because there is less sugar and cream in it. Compared to other types of chocolate, dark chocolate is healthier, and it contains a key compound that prevents heart disease. Some people may think dark chocolate is too bitter, but for me, it is a tasty, healthy snack.

sub_questions.content: Do you like eating chocolate? Why/why not?

answers.content: I like eating chocolate very much. Chocolate has a unique flavor. It’s bitter, but it’s a deep, rich bitterness. I love chocolates because they can lift my mood no matter where I am or what I am doing. Moreover, eating chocolate reminded me of happy memories of having warm chocolate chip cookies as a kid with my grandma.

Yes, I absolutely love eating chocolate. I like its special flavor, which combines sweetness and bitterness. I remember when I was in elementary school, my mother would buy me chocolate bars as bonuses each time I got a satisfactory grade. For me, chocolate is more than a tasty treat. It’s also a symbol of achievement.

questions.content: Do you like your current learning atmosphere
Is there anything you want to change about your school?
What are the difference between your school and other schools?
Where do you go to school?
What changes would you like to see in your school?
Do you go to a good school?

sub_questions.content: Do you like your current learning atmosphere

answers.content: Yes. I enjoy the atmosphere my school creates. First of all, the school enrolled (招生) plenty of talented and smart students. Surrounded by them, I feel very energetic and inspired all the time. Besides, we have a comparatively relaxed academic environment that allows students to challenge themselves and make innovations.

Yes. I think I am satisfied with the learning environment, where I don’t need to worry about competition with peers and just focus on what I am interested in. For a long period of time back in high school, I was told to pay attention to the rankings and test scores. Now, I just borrow books from libraries and select courses to my interests, which is quite pleasant for me.

sub_questions.content: Is there anything you want to change about your school?

answers.content: If possible, I would like to expand the library. Our library is not big enough, which makes it difficult to find a place during the final weeks since students just crowd in the library to prepare for the test. So, I think it would be better if we have a larger library.

Of course, nothing is perfect. I think the teacher in my school could be more involved in students’ learning experiences. Even though we are already college students, we still need a lifelong mentor to keep our company, who can inspire us when necessary and help us tackle some detailed academic problems.

sub_questions.content: What are the difference between your school and other schools?

answers.content: Compared to other schools, my school is relatively small in size. We are more targeted at social science, so we don’t have fancy labs for you to do some medical experiments. Also, there are fewer students and thus a smaller campus.

I think the curriculum (课程) of my school doesn’t follow the traditional one compared to others. Here we have Tai Chi (太极拳), swimming , career planning, etc. It’s very considerate of my school to care for students’ mental health and future development in this way.

sub_questions.content: Where do you go to school?

answers.content: I went to Beijing for higher education because Beijing is China’s capital city. Going to school there places me in the hustle and bustle of city life. What’s more, there are more people to meet and network with, which can also help me make valuable connections and jumpstart my future career.

Well, actually, I went to school in my hometown in a small city, which was located in the southern part of China. The school was situated on top of a little hill, and it was surrounded by fences and walls. In our school, we had around 200 students and there were not many teachers.

sub_questions.content: What changes would you like to see in your school?

answers.content: I would like to see the curriculum in my school adapt to the skill sets of the 21 century. For example, the Board of Schools can have a group of successful professionals document the 10 most important things they know, and the 10 most important skills, then compare and contrast them with national academic standards to create the most efficient curriculum for every major.

Honestly, I’m pretty satisfied with my school. If I have to name some changes, I guess they could try and improve the school's exposure in the media, cause lots of people aren’t even familiar with the name of my school. Yeah, what a pity!

sub_questions.content: Do you go to a good school?

answers.content: Yes, I have always been going to good schools because my parents attach great importance to education as a means of enhancing a person's worth and career. In their eyes, a school is good when it uses every resource, advantage, gift, and opportunity it has to grow students and tends to see more resources, and opportunities than lower-performing schools.

Well, the school that I go to is not a well-known one, but it’s quite nice. It’s located in the city suburbs where there are lots of plants and rocky roads. It’s especially beautiful when it rains lightly. The teachers are easygoing and caring. So, overall, I think it is a good school.

questions.content: Do you know many small businesses where you live?
Have you ever thought about starting your own business?
Have you ever worked in small businesses?
Do you prefer buying things in big companies or small businesses?
Do you know many small businesses?

sub_questions.content: Do you know many small businesses where you live?

answers.content: I know some small businesses. For example, my mom is a small business owner who offers a variety of curtain products to the local community. To effectively run the curtain business, my mom is conversant with many important aspects of this business such as sewing, window treatments, and interior decoration, among other things.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever thought about starting your own business?

answers.content: Not really. I’m quite conservative, so starting one’s own company is risky. It is possible that you can be successful and earn a lot of money, but at the same time, you can also get broken. And I think this fact has been tested when we see so many small businesses closing down during covid.

Yes, I have given it some thought. I would like to start a bakery. I really like people, so I would love to be of service to them. I’m such a sucker for baking (特别喜欢烘焙). I am sure that I would want to try all sorts of dishes to present to my customers. But the most obvious reason is probably that I don't like working for a boss. What I mean to say is that I want to be independent and make my own decisions.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever worked in small businesses?

answers.content: Not really. I have only worked in international companies, and they are all on the list of Fortune 500. But after all these years working in similar environments, I’d love to make some changes and try something new in smaller industries or businesses.

Yeah, of course. My first internship was in a small-scale private company with less than 50 people. What impressed me most there was its comfortable and relaxed working atmosphere. But generally speaking, I would like to take up a job in a big company (在一家大公司工作). You know big corporations are more reputable and prestigious than small ones, and they can also provide satisfactory welfare benefits (福利) along with other perks (额外待遇).

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer buying things in big companies or small businesses?

answers.content: For my part, I prefer to buy things from big companies because companies can produce products more efficiently when operating at scale. Moreover, their size sometimes enables them to design higher quality products and to improve them faster and more efficiently. In these cases, products and services get better and cheaper when provided by large companies, to the benefit of consumers like me.

Well, it depends. When I buy expensive products like a TV or a vacuum, I would go for big companies because they represent ‘quality’ for me, whereas for small businesses, I would only buy small or cheap products there. I don’t mean that the quality of small businesses is not good, but big companies normally offer better customer service and after-sales service. It would be much easier for me to purchase expensive products from them.

sub_questions.content: Do you know many small businesses?

answers.content: Yes, as a matter of fact, I do know a lot of small businesses. For example, the milk tea shops around the street corner are my favorite small businesses. On top of that, all the street stands which serve amazing food are also my favorite. I find that small businesses are closer to our lives than big companies, and they bring more convenience to us.

questions.content: What exercise did you do as a child that you don't do anymore?
Should schools encourage young students to do more physical exercise?
Do you think it is important for people to exercise?
Do you know any people who are good at sports?
Do you think students need more exercise?
Did you do some sports when you were young?
Where did you learn how to do it?
What sports do you like?

sub_questions.content: What exercise did you do as a child that you don't do anymore?

answers.content: As a child, I used to enjoy playing soccer a lot, but unfortunately, I don't engage in it anymore. Back then, I used to spend hours playing with my friends every evening after school. However, as I grew older, my interests shifted towards different activities and responsibilities, so I haven't played soccer for quite some time now.

I used to practice taekwondo regularly, but unfortunately, it is something I no longer engage in. Despite the nostalgia, due to time constraints, I shifted my focus to swimming and weightlifting. Although I miss the discipline and agility taekwondo instilled in me, I find solace in the strength training and the serenity of swimming.

sub_questions.content: Should schools encourage young students to do more physical exercise?

answers.content: Yes, schools should definitely encourage young students to engage in more physical exercise. Regular physical activity not only promotes physical health but also enhances cognitive function, concentration, and academic performance. Moreover, it instills lifelong habits of health and fitness, contributing to overall well-being and development. Introducing students to a variety of sports and activities can help them find enjoyable ways to stay active.

Yes, schools should definitely encourage young students to do more physical exercise. Regular physical activity is crucial for the overall development and well-being of children. Exercise not only helps in maintaining a healthy weight but also improves concentration, enhances cognitive abilities, and reduces the risk of chronic diseases later in life. Moreover, engaging in sports and physical activities promotes teamwork, leadership skills, and discipline among students. By encouraging physical exercise, schools can instill healthy habits and promote a balanced lifestyle among young students.

sub_questions.content: Do you think it is important for people to exercise?

answers.content: Yes, absolutely. Exercise is crucial for maintaining physical health, improving mental well-being, and enhancing overall quality of life. Regular physical activity helps prevent chronic diseases, boosts mood, increases energy levels, and promotes longevity. It's an essential aspect of a healthy lifestyle.

Yes, I believe it’s crucial for people to exercise regularly. Regular physical activity offers numerous benefits to both the body and mind. First and foremost, exercise helps us maintain a healthy weight and prevents the development of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and obesity. Additionally, exercise boosts our energy levels, improves muscle strength and flexibility, and enhances overall cardiovascular health. Furthermore, engaging in physical activities releases endorphins, which reduce stress and anxiety, promoting mental well-being. In conclusion, exercising is essential for maintaining a healthy lifestyle and ensuring both physical and mental well-being.

sub_questions.content: Do you know any people who are good at sports?

answers.content: Yes, I know several people who are very good at sports. For example, my friend Lisa is an excellent swimmer, and she has won several medals in swimming competitions. Another acquaintance, Jack, is a skilled basketball player and often participates in local tournaments.

Yes, I know several people who are quite skilled in sports. Some of my friends excel in basketball, others in swimming, and a few are talented runners. It's impressive to see their dedication and athleticism in their respective sports.

sub_questions.content: Do you think students need more exercise?

answers.content: I’m afraid that yes, I do think children and adolescents need to participate more in sports. I mean, their school lives just emphasize studying, so young children are forced to read books all the time, or take online classes. It’s rare to even see a kid without glasses nowadays which just shows how little they get out.

Yes, I think they do need more exercises. In today’s world, students spend most of their time inside, learning, reading and taking classes online or inside the classroom. I’m afraid they do need to go outside to build their muscles. After all, a healthy body is the foundation of all.

sub_questions.content: Did you do some sports when you were young?

answers.content: Definitely. My parents believed that children should stay outside for as long as possible. I was never allowed to watch TV or play video games when I was a kid. Instead, I was taught many sorts of sports, like swimming, roller-skating and various ball games.

Yep, absolutely! My parents like to call me nature’s child. I was a wild kid, and I was never afraid to try new things. That’s how I picked up many different kinds of sports, like water sports, ball games, skating, and etc.

sub_questions.content: Where did you learn how to do it?

answers.content: Well, as I already mentioned, I attended a tennis academy when I was younger. Before that, my father used to take me to the community stadium to play all sorts of ball games. He taught me the very basic skills in numerous sports, but found I was best at tennis so that’s why I had to attend tennis lessons.

Well, I learned how to swim and dive because I watched people do them about everyday while I was living in Sanya. I just naturally got the skills, I suppose. As for surfing, it’s my older cousin who introduced it to me. He gave me a surfing board as my 10th birthday present! And he taught me the master skills of this sport.

sub_questions.content: What sports do you like?

answers.content: I am especially into water sports and gymnastics. Well, I lived in Sanya, a beach city, for a couple of years when I was a kid. I watched and learned how to swim, dive, and surf. I find those water activities relaxing. As for gymnastics, it’s always been my favorite activity in PE class, for some reason.

Well, to be honest, I’m not much of a sports person generally. I’m only really interested in certain ball games, especially tennis. I guess that’s because I was sent to a tennis academy to play tennis when I was like seven or eight years old. Though I still like the game, I’m not very good nowadays.

questions.content: What do you do when you stay up late?
What does it feel like the next morning if you stay up late?
Did you stay up late when you were a kid?
What do you usually do when you stay up late?
Do you often stay up late?

sub_questions.content: What do you do when you stay up late?

answers.content: Well, when I was staying up late, I would indulge in various activities. Sometimes, I would immerse myself in a captivating novel, exploring different realms through the power of storytelling. Other times, I would enhance my creative skills by sketching or painting, letting my imagination flow freely. Additionally, I would use that time to catch up on my favorite TV series or movies, allowing myself to unwind and relax. Overall, staying up late provided me with the opportunity to engage in hobbies and interests that brought me joy and allowed me to explore my creative side.

Well, I am not a night person. If I had to burn the midnight oil, it would probably be the case that I would finish pending office work, binge-watch a TV series or program that I find irresistible, or talk to a friend over the phone.

sub_questions.content: What does it feel like the next morning if you stay up late?

answers.content: If I continued to stay up for more than 3 days, I would wake up tired, yawn during conversations, and nearly fall asleep during the day. And it may also lead to bigger and bigger dark circles under the eyes and acne on the face which will make others have a bad first impression of me.

Well, as a morning person, I have to say that if I cannot go to sleep early, the next day will feel totally wasted. I will not be able to concentrate on my studies/work and I lose my appetite. Also, in the afternoon, the exhaustion will be overwhelming for me, and I will definitely need to go to bed extra early.

sub_questions.content: Did you stay up late when you were a kid?

answers.content: Seldom. Pulling an all-nighter was strictly forbidden when I was a kid. My parents would regulate my timetable and I had to go to bed before 10 pm because they believe that enough sleep is conducive to the growth of my brain. But now, when I have to catch up on the deadline of my work or study, I have to stay up late because you know there is not enough time in one day.

Not really. I mean I wanted to go to bed late sometimes, but you know when we were a kid, our parents always worried about us not getting enough sleep. So, they would remind us when it was bedtime and made sure that we really slept. However, for some special occasions, such as New Year’s Eve, we could stay up a bit later.

sub_questions.content: What do you usually do when you stay up late?

answers.content: Well, I am not a night person. If I had to burn the midnight oil, it would probably be the case that I would finish pending office work, binge-watch a TV series or program that I find irresistible, or talk to a friend over the phone.

I usually stay up late doing my work. Because the deadline is approaching and I haven't finished the assigned task yet. Sometimes, I stay up late because I want to watch the last episode of my favourite TV show because I'm so captivated by its storyline that I just can't save it for the next day.

sub_questions.content: Do you often stay up late?

answers.content:
stay up late?
Not really. You know, being a night owl could take a toll on our body function and even work efficiency. If I can get a good night’s sleep every day, it helps me to have a clear and alert brain, which allows me to focus, learn and remember information and be creative.

Yes, I guess I stay up late pretty often because it seems to me that things can never be finished no matter how hard I’ve tried. And even if I did get things done, I always want to spend some time on my own doing things like binge-watching my best TV series on Netflix.

questions.content: Do people usually use public transportation in your country?
What kind of public transport do people in your country use the most? Why?
What do you want to change about public transport?
Will you ride public transportation in the future? Why?
Would you say transport costs are high in your hometown?

sub_questions.content: Do people usually use public transportation in your country?

answers.content: Definitely! Considering the population we have in our country, our public transportation system is actually pretty well-developed, so a lot of us prefer to take buses or subways to school or work. Also, taking public transportation is really cheap in our nation, so for commuters, it is a sensible choice.

sub_questions.content: What kind of public transport do people in your country use the most? Why?

answers.content: Well, I think it depends on the region. For some cities where people have access to subways, they would probably prefer to take them because there would be no traffic congestion problems. But for cities without the subway, buses are the most used mode of transportation since they are really affordable and can generally arrive on time.

sub_questions.content: What do you want to change about public transport?

answers.content: Actually, I am quite happy with this part of our infrastructure but if I have to change something, I would probably say the frequency. I feel during rush hour, maybe there should be more buses or subways, while when it’s not so busy we can just lower the frequency in order to save energy in the quieter periods.

sub_questions.content: Will you ride public transportation in the future? Why?

answers.content: Sure I will! I mean I do enjoy driving my own car but sometimes I feel it’s not really eco-friendly. Since we have such well-established public transportation that can take me to almost anywhere, as a responsible citizen, I think it would be a good choice to take buses or other shared transport to help ease the traffic pressure, and to save energy.

sub_questions.content: Would you say transport costs are high in your hometown?

answers.content: I don’t think the transport costs in my hometown are high. Thanks to the control of the government, almost everyone can afford public transport costs. People can cross the city by subway, paying no more than 10 RMB. Better still, students and elders can even get a discount when buying transport tickets, so I think the transport costs in my hometown are reasonable.

questions.content: What is your favorite number? Why?
Do you usually use numbers?
Are you good at math?
Are you good at remembering phone numbers?

sub_questions.content: What is your favorite number? Why?

answers.content: My favorite number is 7. For me, ‘7’ is the number of luck and good fortune. It symbolizes harmony, perfection, and prosperity. Additionally, 7 holds significance in various contexts such as the seven colors of the rainbow, the seven days of the week, and the seven wonders of the world.

Well, actually I don't really have a favorite number, but if I had to choose one I would probably say 3 because I was born in a core family, which means we’ve only got three members: my parents and me. Also, this is my third time taking the IELTS test, so hopefully this will be my lucky number and mean no more tests from now on!

sub_questions.content: Do you usually use numbers?

answers.content: Yes, I frequently use numbers in daily life for various purposes such as counting, measuring, calculating, and keeping track of time. Numbers are essential for tasks ranging from simple arithmetic to complex financial calculations.

Definitely, yes. Although my study/work is not numeracy-related, I think my life is actually centered around numbers. For instance, checking the time is what I’m always doing. And also, calculating the price of a product with a discount is what I do every time I go shopping.

sub_questions.content: Are you good at math?

answers.content: Yes, I consider myself proficient in maths. Maths has always been my favourite subject. I have a good grasp of mathematical concepts and enjoy solving problems involving numbers, equations and logical reasoning.

Not really. I’ve always been more talented in literacy-related subjects. Although a lot of effort has been put into learning maths, it’s still my Achilles heel. However, I’m fully aware that being good at it can bring lots of benefits so I hope I can improve my maths skills in the future.

sub_questions.content: Are you good at remembering phone numbers?

answers.content: Yes, I am quite good at remembering phone numbers. I find it helpful to associate numbers with patterns, repetitions, or personal significance, which aids in retention. However, with the widespread use of smartphones and digital contacts, the need to remember phone numbers has diminished somewhat.

Generally speaking, yes. Considering that I’m still quite young, it’s not that hard for me to remember a series of numbers, especially when I pay enough attention to it. But if you tell me the numbers in another language, let’s say... English, it might be a little bit challenging for me to memorize.

questions.content: What’s your last haircut?
Do you like having your hair cut?
How often do you have a haircut?
How long have you had your current haircut?
Have you ever had an unhappy haircut experience?

sub_questions.content: What’s your last haircut?

answers.content: It was like really short, and that was during my high school, when I found that having short hair could be so much more convenient. It indeed was a bit too boyish(男孩子气的), I guess, my relatives sometimes called me ‘tomboy’. But I’m fine with that. Then I decided to have a change and became... Let’s say, a bit more chic. And that’s when I changed to my current hairstyle.

sub_questions.content: Do you like having your hair cut?

answers.content: Um... If you mean ‘do I enjoy having a haircut’, then no. I’m not that keen on polishing(抛光，修饰) my appearance. I think it has to be neat and clean, and that’s all. I don’t really enjoy sitting there for at least half an hour waiting to get my hair cut, as I can’t even see anything clearly without my glasses.

sub_questions.content: How often do you have a haircut?

answers.content: Well, about once a month, sometimes even longer. My hair doesn’t grow very fast, compared to my cousin. He goes to the barber’s every two weeks cuz otherwise, he’d look like a rock star – I meant no offence(无意冒犯) to them, but, you know what I mean(你懂的). I, however, don’t have that problem.

sub_questions.content: How long have you had your current haircut?

answers.content: I don’t really like to change my haircut style too often, and I think I’ve kept it this way for the last three or four years . I mean, yeah, there are new hair trends almost every season, but I just don’t see the point of(不理解为什么) following them. It’s too tiring for me.

sub_questions.content: Have you ever had an unhappy haircut experience?

answers.content: That’s more like(更像是，不如说是) every time, ha-ha!
