You see, I just have this feeling that I have a very different understanding of concepts like ‘a LITTLE bit shorter’, from, say, ninety-nine percent of hairdressers. They almost always get my fringe(刘海) way too short making me look pretty silly. To be honest, I don’t know what their problem is.

questions.content: What do you do with garbage when you are on the street?
Why do some people throw garbage on the street?
How do you feel when you see people throwing garbage on the street?
Do you think your city is clean ?

sub_questions.content: What do you do with garbage when you are on the street?

answers.content: First of all, when I’m on the street, I would never throw garbage irresponsibly, and I would always help to keep the streets clean. Secondly, if I pass by a piece of garbage, I usually just pick it up and throw it in the nearest bin.

sub_questions.content: Why do some people throw garbage on the street?

answers.content: From my understanding, I think people throw garbage on the street mainly because they find it a more convenient way to get rid of garbage than throwing it in the bin. Also, I would say it’s because there aren’t enough garbage bins around the city.

sub_questions.content: How do you feel when you see people throwing garbage on the street?

answers.content: When witnessing people throwing garbage, I always feel very angry because I think as citizens we all have a responsibility to take care of the city and abide by the regulations. Only by doing this can we build a beautiful and clean place to live.

sub_questions.content: Do you think your city is clean ?

answers.content: Well, I suppose my city is quite clean most of the time, thanks to the hard work of sanitation workers，and you can see tidy streets and designated trash bins everywhere. If you ever travel to my city and take a look around, you’ll be amazed by how perfectly maintained it is as a clean place to live.

questions.content: Do you want to live on another planet?
Do you like to see the stars at night?
Do you like to look at the sky? Why?
How often do you look at the sky?
Do you prefer the sky in the morning or the sky at night?
Can you see the moon and stars at night where you live?
Is there a good place to look at the sky where you live?

sub_questions.content: Do you want to live on another planet?

answers.content: To be honest, I don't want to. Our planet, the earth, has so many breath-taking places that I haven’t explored yet, and I love my current life with my family, friends and my loved ones around me. I definitely do not want to move to another planet and lose all the fun on the earth.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to see the stars at night?

answers.content: I love it! It’s a shame that in most cities, it’s barely possible to see the stars at night. But in some rural areas, people can still enjoy the beauty of the stars at night. I remember when I travelled to New Zealand and we stayed in a camper van on a star-gazing site in the South Island. We were watching the stars in the sky and even the Milky Way was clearly visible. That kind of beauty is really hard to express in words.

sub_questions.content: Do you like to look at the sky? Why?

answers.content: Yes, I like to look at the sky, especially when the weather is good. I like it because I enjoy the blue of the clear sky with some snow-white clouds. When there is a breeze, you can see some thin clouds moving slowly. All this makes me feel like I’m in the quietest fairy tale. That’s why I like to look at the sky.

sub_questions.content: How often do you look at the sky?

answers.content: Frankly speaking, I barely look skywards because for most of the time I stay indoors, so there is no chance for me to do that. But when I was little, my mom and I used to take a walk every day after dinner so when walking around, I often raised my head to appreciate the starry sky.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer the sky in the morning or the sky at night?

answers.content: Actually, I don't have a preference for either of them, but if I have to make a choice I would say the sky in the early hours. The reason is that, in the morning the sky is still covered in mist and I am really into this kind of vague view. Also, the rising sun in the morning sky represents the brand-new beginning of another day, which I think is a positive symbol.

sub_questions.content: Can you see the moon and stars at night where you live?

answers.content: Well, to be honest, I believe it is a pity that I can hardly see those celestial bodies clearly in the evening these days. Since the air pollution has influenced the visibility at night greatly. The moon and stars can be spotted sometimes when the weather is nice enough, but I have to say it is becoming less and less frequent for me to see them.

sub_questions.content: Is there a good place to look at the sky where you live?

answers.content: Yes, I suppose so. I’ve got a tiny but clean balcony in my room where I think it is a perfect location to appreciate the sky. Standing on the balcony and staring towards the sky, I always find it mysterious and attractive. It’s like an exquisite masterpiece of Vincent van Gogh. So, whenever I have time, I go to this small balcony to enjoy the appealing view.

questions.content: What's the weather like where you live?
Do you like the weather in your hometown?
Do you prefer cold or hot weather?
Do you prefer dry or wet weather?
Are you in the habit of checking the weather forecast? When/How often?
What do you think are the effects of climate change in recent years?
Would you like to visit other cities/countries that have different climates from where you live?
What kind of weather do you like most?
What kind of weather is typical in your hometown?
What’s your favourite season?

sub_questions.content: What's the weather like where you live?

answers.content: I am living in Shanghai, which is a city located in the south of the country and is close to the ocean. Therefore, it rains a lot in this city, especially in summer. In the winter, the temperature tends to be above zero degrees, so it means it seldomly snows. Generally speaking, I think Shanghai is a nice city to live in.

I live in the Northern part of China. The weather in my hometown is typically cold and dry in winter, and hot and humid in summer. Spring is a good time for travel in my hometown because the climate is much milder than in winter and summer. While it's true that spring can be rainy, you won't find the extreme temperatures, and therefore sightseeing can be much more enjoyable.

sub_questions.content: Do you like the weather in your hometown?

answers.content: Yes, I like the weather in my hometown. Fall is my favorite time. The weather is usually pretty glorious and because we're in the north, we have a number of opportunities to see fall foliage. I really love fall foliage. In addition, because the weather in my hometown is relatively dry, there aren't many cockroaches. I really hate those creatures.

Sure. My hometown is Kunming, a city famous for its mild and pleasant climate. In China, people call it “The Spring City” due to its weather. The perfect geography there makes the four seasons feel like just one season, so it’s neither too hot in summer nor too cold in winter.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer cold or hot weather?

answers.content: Definitely cold weather. When there's too much sun, everyone is just angry and frowning. When there's too much sun, insects are everywhere, outdoors as well as indoors. In hot weather, you sweat easily even if you do nothing but breathe, which makes you have to shower twice a day. I've always been a fan of cold weather.

Personally, I prefer cold weather. I can’t bear the feeling of being surrounded by heat waves as soon as I step out of the room in summer. I also hate the ever-present mosquitoes in hot weather. On the contrary, I shiver when I walk in the open air during cold weather, but I love the feeling of being wrapped up in warm clothes.

sub_questions.content: Do you prefer dry or wet weather?

answers.content: Dry weather all the way, humid weather is too uncomfortable at all temperatures. I could really live in a semi-arid place, I guess. Rain is nice, but I don't need the air to be humid the rest of the time. And unlike the rainy seasons, the weather in dry seasons are very predictable. You are free to go anywhere you want without worrying about rain.

If I were to choose between the two, I would probably go with dry weather. I think wet weather makes people feel hot and humid and sometimes even a bit depressed. People generally feel more comfortable with Low humidity. Most importantly, if wet days continue for a long time, that damp black mould will begin to grow.

sub_questions.content: Are you in the habit of checking the weather forecast? When/How often?

answers.content: Yeah, you bet. Whenever I make travel plans I check the local weather forecast and then I arrange my itinerary accordingly. No one wants bad weather to ruin a long-awaited trip. Generally speaking, sunny days are best for hanging out, outdoors, and on rainy days, I’d rather stay indoors, enjoying a romantic movie starring my favorite actors, so I check the weather regularly so I can plan my days,

Yes, I am. Every night before I go to bed, I will check the weather forecast for the next day. It determines what I should wear to work/school the next day and what time I should get up. After all, you stand a good chance to get stuck in traffic on a rainy day.

sub_questions.content: What do you think are the effects of climate change in recent years?

answers.content: Well, one obvious result of climate change is global warming. Summer is getting so hot these years. And in fact, mountain fires are becoming common in some places. Also, some animal species died out because of this, such as the Chinese Paddlefish and wild Yangtze sturgeon.

I think the impact you can see in recent years is an increase in natural disasters and extreme weather. For example, this year and last, many places experienced high temperatures, and some places even experienced the highest temperature ever recorded. Wildfires and floods have also occurred in many parts of Europe, Australia and America. These are the effects of climate changes in recent years.

sub_questions.content: Would you like to visit other cities/countries that have different climates from where you live?

answers.content: Yes, absolutely. I want to visit Melbourne in Australia, actually. I’ve got a couple of friends who are studying there. In their words, in Melbourne, you can experience four seasons in one day. Well, for someone who’s lived in a place where the temperature is stable all year round, that’s hard to imagine!

Why not? I live in central China, but I have always wanted to visit tropical cities and countries, such as Thailand, Singapore and The Maldives. I have seen a lot of pictures of these places online and I have friends who have actually been there. They said that the climate is different from the climate here. They are hot all year round with a lot of precipitation, and there's an abundance of tropical vibes, so I’d like to visit these places they sound beautiful.

sub_questions.content: What kind of weather do you like most?

answers.content: I like rainy days. Rain is one of the most beautiful phenomena from Mother Nature. Most people love to spend time watching the rain, smelling the aroma of rainy earth and listening to the sounds of the rain. i love to enjoy the rain, as I find peace and joy during rainy days. Who doesn’t love the feeling of rain droplets touching their cheeks with a relieving rhythm?

I like a good many types of weather. Generally, I like cool weather suitable for outdoor activities, the coolness that wind brings, as long as the wind or rain is not too severe. I just prefer cold weather to hot weather.

sub_questions.content: What kind of weather is typical in your hometown?

answers.content: I live in Northern part of China. The weather in my hometown is typical cold and dry in winters, hot and humid in summers. Spring is a good time for travel in my hometown because the climate is much milder than in winter and summer. While it's true that spring can be rainy, you won't find the extreme temperatures, and therefore sightseeing can be much more enjoyable.

My hometown is Shanghai, a city in mainland China. This is a city where you can experience changes in the weather. For half the time, it’s like warm summer weather, with a slight breeze and beaming sun. The rest of the time is pretty freezing with wet days and lots of heavy rain but not so much snow.

sub_questions.content: What’s your favourite season?

answers.content: I do like winter a lot, as I just prefer cold weather to hot weather. Sadly, I rarely experience that as I live far south of China. Most days, it’s over 20 outside and we have surprisingly warm weather even in winter. That’s why I like cold weather with a lot of heavy snow.

questions.content: Do you usually buy jewelry?
Why do you think some people wear a piece of jewelry for a long time?
What type of jewelry do you like to buy?
Do you often wear jewelry?

sub_questions.content: Do you usually buy jewelry?

answers.content: Not really. Jewelry is usually very expensive, and I can’t afford to buy new pieces of jewelry frequently. But I really love fashion and want to make myself look stylish. So I usually buy small accessories. They’re cool and not that expensive.

Seldom. I’m not someone who is a big fan of (非常喜欢) jewelry. It seems to me that simplicity is my first principle when it comes to fashion, so chances that I buy jewelry are pretty slim (机会非常渺茫). But I totally acknowledge some people probably like to buy jewelry just because they wanna be more attractive, charming, or fashionable (时尚的).

sub_questions.content: Why do you think some people wear a piece of jewelry for a long time?

answers.content: Well, in most cases, if people get attached to a certain piece of jewelry, there might be a story behind it. It could be a family heirloom that has been passed down from generation to generation so it has a special meaning to the owner. Or it may belong to someone they hold dear such as their spouse or soul mate. Another reason is that they keep wearing the same jewel just out of habit, you know they get accustomed to it.

I guess that is probably because jewelry usually carries some special meaning and the person who wears it wants to remind themself about that regularly. For example, wedding rings are one of the things some people wear all the time, and they can remember the happy moments with their partners.

sub_questions.content: What type of jewelry do you like to buy?

answers.content: If I were to choose, I’d opt for a gold necklace or bracelet. The reason behind this is that they are eye-catching and I would look gorgeous with some accessories on such body parts. For me, any piece of jewelry has its own beauty, and they can vary from luxurious ones such as silver, gold, and diamond to more affordable and street-style ones like artificial stones, copper, or other metals.
Among all kinds of jewelry, I like silver ones best because they show my high social status. What’s more, I heard that wearing silver jewelry is good for physical health due to the silver element.

sub_questions.content: Do you often wear jewelry?

answers.content: Once in a while, especially when there is a special event like a romantic date, a birthday party, or an important festival activity. On such occasions, I often have my bracelet or necklace on to adorn myself so that I can feel more confident about myself.

Not often. When I have some important meetings such as conference meetings with clients, I will choose to wear jewelry to make myself look more confident and beautiful. But in my daily life, I won’t wear them because it is somewhat dangerous if you wear luxurious ones walking outside.
